symptom: Client resolves Azure Monitor endpoint FQDN to incorrect private IP address when using AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)
rootCause: Multiple AMPLS resources exist on the same VNET or peered VNETs, violating the single-AMPLS-per-network principle, causing DNS override
solution: 1) Check for other AMPLS resources in subscription via ASC Properties > Private Links 2) Validate expected IP from PE DNS zone records matches nslookup result 3) If mismatch, look for competing AMPLS in peered VNETs or other subscriptions 4) Involve Azure Networking team if source of incorrect IP not found
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Investigate incorrect private IP resolution for an AMPLS Azure Monitor resource

symptom: Stale Private DNS Zone preventing proper name resolutions for Application Insights with AMPLS - telemetry ingestion fails due to DNS resolution failure
rootCause: Private DNS Zone records become stale/outdated, causing Azure Monitor endpoint FQDNs to fail DNS resolution or resolve to wrong IPs
solution: 1) Validate DNS connectivity for Application Insights endpoints 2) Check Private DNS Zone records for stale entries 3) Update or recreate Private DNS Zone records to match current Private Endpoint IPs 4) Verify resolution with nslookup after cleanup
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/Support Topics/I receive no data or cannot run queries/DNS not resolving to correct IP address

symptom: No telemetry data flowing to Application Insights after configuring AMPLS - ingestion endpoint resolves to public IP instead of private IP
rootCause: Web app/function hosted on App Service without VNET support (e.g., free tier) cannot resolve AMPLS private endpoints - App Service free tier does NOT offer VNET support
solution: 1) Ensure web app/function is on at least a private network with VNET support 2) Upgrade from App Service free tier to a plan that supports VNET Integration (Basic or higher) 3) Configure VNET Integration for the App Service 4) Verify DNS resolution returns private IP after VNET integration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/Support Topics/I receive no data or cannot run queries/No data in Log Analytics, Metrics, or Application Insights

symptom: Cannot add Action Group directly to Built-In Azure Monitor Alerts (Azure Backup, Stack Edge, Stack HCI, Data Box Gateway, SCOM MI, Azure Local) - no option to attach Action Group
rootCause: Built-In Azure Monitor Alerts have no resource ID because they skip the Alerts Data Plane; the onboarded service processes alerting logic in its own environment, so there is no alert rule resource to attach an Action Group to
solution: Use Alert Processing Rules instead of directly adding Action Groups. Alert Processing Rules can match Built-In alerts and route them to the desired Action Group. Exception: Event Grid Built-In Alerts have an Event Subscription resource ID, so Action Groups CAN be directly added.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Built-In Azure Monitor Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert (LSA) fires false alerts or misses alerts due to late arriving data - ingestion delay causes inconsistent evaluation results
rootCause: Ingestion delay of logs to Kusto causes data to arrive after the alert evaluation window has passed; retries increase load on Draft/Kusto backend
solution: LSA uses sliding window optimization: the query is automatically modified to cover multiple windows of timespans in a single execution, simulating additional retries. Each result appears in multiple executions but alerts fire only once per bucket (both stateful and stateless). The cutoff time for retries is 30 minutes. Prepended query uses LSA_frequency, LSA_winNumber, LSA_windowTimeSpan variables with bin_at() and mv-expand to create overlapping evaluation windows.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Log Alerts optimization flow and query manipulation

symptom: Log search alert rule querying across tenants fails or stops working - cross-tenant target resource no longer supported
rootCause: Since March 2024, cross-tenant queries for log search alert rules are no longer supported unless Azure Lighthouse is used to assign RBAC roles across tenants
solution: Use Azure Lighthouse to assign RBAC roles with adequate access across tenants for the alert rule identity (default or managed). Direct cross-tenant target resource is deprecated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Log Data Access for Log Search Alert Rules

symptom: Log search alert rule evaluation fails with 403 Forbidden 'InsufficientAccessError' / 'NspValidationFailedError' when workspace is in Network Security Perimeter
rootCause: Alert rule is not part of the same Network Security Perimeter (NSP) as the Log Analytics workspace it queries
solution: Add the log search alert rule to the same NSP as the Log Analytics workspace. If resource-centric and not in NSP, evaluation succeeds but data from NSP-protected workspace is silently hidden.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Log Data Access for Log Search Alert Rules

symptom: Log search alert rule using linked storage account fails after July 2024 or when using system-assigned managed identity with linked storage
rootCause: Since July 2024, linked storage requires managed identity; only user-assigned managed identity is supported (not system-assigned). Also requires Storage Blob Data Contributor role, not just Contributor or Reader.
solution: 1) Configure user-assigned managed identity on the alert rule 2) Assign Storage Blob Data Contributor role on the linked storage account (write operations needed for storing log queries) 3) Assign Reader role on the workspace
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Log Data Access for Log Search Alert Rules

symptom: Log search alert rule evaluation succeeds but returns no data - data is hidden from resource-centric alert rule
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace access control mode is 'Require workspace permissions' and the alert rule identity lacks workspace-level permissions; data from that workspace is silently hidden
solution: Grant the alert rule identity Reader role on the Log Analytics workspace, or change workspace access control mode to 'Use resource or workspace permissions'. Check access control mode in Azure Support Center > Workspace Settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Log Data Access for Log Search Alert Rules

symptom: Log search alert rule cross-service query to ADX or ARG fails - default identity does not work for cross-service queries
rootCause: Default identity is not supported for cross-service (Azure Data Explorer / Azure Resource Graph) log search alert rules; managed identity is required
solution: Switch to system-assigned or user-assigned managed identity. For ADX: Reader on workspace + Reader on ADX resource + database viewer on all queried databases. For ARG: Reader on workspace + Reader on queried resources.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Log Data Access for Log Search Alert Rules

symptom: Log Search Alert Resource Health shows Unavailable with NO_REASON for alert rules with evaluation frequency > 15 minutes (e.g. 30 min, 1 hour). Status appears as unavailable but alert is functioning normally.
rootCause: Alert rules that run less frequently than every 15 minutes will not provide health status updates to Resource Health. This is by design - the health check interval is 15 minutes, so lower-frequency rules naturally appear unavailable between evaluations.
solution: This is expected behavior, not a real issue. Verify the alert rule evaluation frequency. If frequency > 15 min, the unavailable status is normal. Also may appear for newly created alerts until first evaluation completes. Doc: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/log-alert-rule-health
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert Resource Health shows Degraded - Setting up resource health (RESOURCE_STATE_NOT_IN_DATAMART) after alert rule creation.
rootCause: Newly created alert rules need time for the first evaluation to complete before Resource Health can report status. The health data has not been populated in the datamart yet.
solution: Wait until the first evaluation of the alert rule completes. This is a transient state that resolves automatically after the first successful evaluation cycle.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert Resource Health shows Degraded - Query validation error (DRAFT_QUERY_VALIDATION). Alert evaluation keeps failing with validation error.
rootCause: Most commonly caused by customer attempting to create an alert rule targeting Basic Logs tables. Log search alerts do not support Basic Logs data plan - only Analytics plan tables are supported.
solution: Check if the target table uses Basic Logs data plan. If so, switch the table to Analytics plan or restructure the alert to query an Analytics plan table. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/basic-logs-configure?tabs=portal-1#compare-the-basic-and-analytics-log-data-plans
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert Resource Health shows Degraded - Workspace not found (DRAFT_WORKSPACE_NOT_FOUND) or Application Insights resource not found (DRAFT_APPLICATION_NOT_FOUND).
rootCause: The alert scope resource (Log Analytics workspace or Application Insights) was moved, renamed, or deleted after the alert rule was created. The alert rule still references the old resource.
solution: Recreate the alert rule with a valid target Log Analytics workspace or Application Insights resource. The original alert rule cannot be repaired if the scope resource no longer exists.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert Resource Health shows Degraded - Query is throttled (DRAFT_THROTTLING). Alert evaluation keeps failing with HTTP 429 throttling error.
rootCause: The alert rule KQL query exceeds Log Analytics query rate limits or concurrency limits, causing throttling (HTTP 429).
solution: Review and optimize the query to reduce resource consumption. Check query limits at https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/service-limits#user-query-throttling. Reduce query complexity, narrow time range, or reduce evaluation frequency.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert evaluation fails with Active alerts limit exceeded (LIMIT_DAILY_ALERT_COUNT_EXCEEDED). Alert stops firing new alerts.
rootCause: A single alert rule can fire up to 12,000 times within 48 hours (documented as 6,000 fired alerts per rule at a time). When this limit is exceeded, further evaluations are blocked.
solution: Reduce the number of fired alerts by: (1) adjusting alert threshold to reduce firing frequency, (2) configuring auto-resolve, (3) reviewing if the alert is too sensitive. Check limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/service-limits#alerts
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert evaluation fails with Dimension combinations limit exceeded (LIMIT_DIMENSION_COMBINATION_COUNT_EXCEEDED).
rootCause: The alert query returns too many unique dimension value combinations exceeding the limit. Stateless alerts: 6,000 dimension combinations. Stateful alerts: 300 dimension combinations.
solution: Reduce the number of dimension combinations by: (1) reducing the number of dimensions used, (2) adding filters to narrow results, (3) switching from stateful to stateless if > 300 combinations needed. Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/service-limits#alerts
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert Resource Health shows Degraded - NSP validation failed (DRAFT_NSP_VALIDATION). Query evaluation fails due to Network Security Perimeter restrictions.
rootCause: The Log Analytics workspace is configured with Network Security Perimeter (NSP) that only allows query access for resources within the perimeter. The log search alert rule is not included in the NSP or no inbound rules allow Alerts service to query the workspace.
solution: Adjust NSP settings: either include the log search alert rule in the perimeter, or add inbound rules to whitelist the Alerts and Action Groups IP addresses. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/network-security-perimeter
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Alert fired but Logic App action group connector fails with 'connection removed' error in workflow run history output
rootCause: The API connection for the Logic App connector (e.g., Office 365) has been removed or expired, causing the connector step to fail
solution: Navigate to Logic App Designer, find the failed connector, click 'change connection' at the bottom, and follow the wizard to sign in to the proper account to recreate the connection
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How-To Validate Logic App Action

symptom: Alert fired but Logic App workflow did not run at all - no execution appears in Logic App Run History, Action Group test returns 404 error
rootCause: The Logic App HTTP trigger URL (Callback URI) has changed or been regenerated, causing a mismatch between the Action Group's stored callback URL and the current Logic App trigger URL
solution: Compare the Callback URL in ASC (Action Group details) with the HTTP URL in the Logic App Designer's first connector ('When an HTTP request is received'). If mismatched, remove the Logic App action from the Action Group and re-add it to get the updated trigger URL
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How-To Validate Logic App Action

symptom: After May 17/18 2024, users who inherit RBAC roles through Azure AD group membership started receiving unexpected Email Resource Manager Role notification emails from Action Groups or Smart Detection (Failure Anomalies)
rootCause: Design change on May 17-18, 2024: Action Groups with Email Resource Manager Role notification type now send emails to individual users who inherit the specified RBAC role through group-level assignments. Previously, only users with direct role assignments received emails.
solution: This is by-design behavior change. Verify user's RBAC assignment type (direct vs group inheritance) via Azure Support Center → Tenant Explorer → Resource Explorer → Access Control → Check Access. If customer wants to stop emails to group-inherited users, they need to change notification type or adjust RBAC assignments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How to check which users have an ARM role to investigate the notification type Email Resource Manager Role or smart detections emails

symptom: Need to check the delivery status of an Azure Notification (email, SMS, webhook, etc.) - customer reports notification not received or wants to verify notification was sent
rootCause: Notification delivery issues can be caused by rate limiting, invalid recipient, mechanism failure, or upstream alert evaluation issues. NotificationState and FailureReason in AzNS Kusto table reveal the exact cause.
solution: Query AzNSTransmissions_All table in Kusto (cluster: aznscluster.southcentralus.kusto.windows.net, database: AzNSPROD) using NotificationId or SubscriptionId. Check NotificationState (complete=success, failed/skipped=check FailureReason), MechanismType, CompletedTime. KQL: AzNSTransmissions_All | where NotificationId == "<id>" | sort by CreatedTime desc | project-reorder NotificationId, TransmitId, NotificationState, MechanismType, CreatedTime, CompletedTime, FailureReason, AssociatedGroupId, AdditionalInfo
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How to get status of an Azure Notification from Kusto

symptom: Need to check delivery status of an Azure Service Health Notification - customer reports not receiving Service Health alerts or wants to trace a specific Service Health event notification
rootCause: Service Health notifications flow through AzNS with UserIdentity SHR_dialtone@activitylogalertsvc.com. Delivery failure reasons visible in NotificationState and FailureReason fields.
solution: Query AzNSTransmissions in Kusto (cluster: aznscluster.southcentralus.kusto.windows.net, database: AzNSPROD) filtered by action group resource ID and UserIdentity =~ SHR_dialtone@activitylogalertsvc.com. Optionally filter by SHAMetadata contains tracking ID. KQL: AzNSTransmissions | where AssociatedGroupId =~ "<actionGroupResourceId>" | where CreatedTime > ago(7d) and UserIdentity =~ "SHR_dialtone@activitylogalertsvc.com" | where SHAMetadata contains "<trackingId>"
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How to get status of an Azure Service Health Notification from Kusto

symptom: Email notification from Azure Monitor alert not received or partially delivered - need to trace email delivery through MEO (Microsoft Email Orchestrator, formerly AEO)
rootCause: Email delivery failure through MEO can be caused by SMTP relay rejection, invalid recipient domain, or message processing errors. MessageStatusHistory shows processing status (Added→Generated→Sent) and MessageDelivery shows SMTP response codes.
solution: 1) Get AEO BatchId from AzNS trace logs (search for AEOBATCH in Message property). 2) Query meoshared.westus.kusto.windows.net / AeoAnalytics: use MessageStatusHistory_All_Shared and MessageDelivery_All_Shared tables filtered by BatchId. 3) Check Status: Added=queued, Generated=email created, Sent=forwarded to SMTP. SmtpCode 250=accepted. 4) If AzNS confirms sent to MEO but email not delivered → escalate to MEO via ICM template in ASC (only after confirming AzNS processed successfully).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How to get trace logs for emails sent via MEO (Formerly AEO) in Kusto

symptom: Azure Monitor alert notification not received - suspected rate limiting on action group notifications (email, SMS, voice, webhook)
rootCause: Azure Notification Service applies rate limits to prevent notification flooding. When rate limit is reached, notification is suppressed with Need Notify = False flag.
solution: 1) Get NotificationId. 2) Trace notification in ASC or Jarvis. 3) Search diagnostic log results where Message contains "need notify". 4) If matched with "Need Notify - False", rate limiting was applied and target receiver was suppressed. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/alerts-rate-limiting
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How to identify if rate limits have been reached for an Azure Notification

symptom: Action Group runbook action may be using legacy built-in runbook (Run As Account) instead of updated V2 runbook (Managed Identities) - need to identify which version is in use
rootCause: Action Groups with built-in runbook actions (e.g. RestartAzureVM) were originally created using Run As Accounts. Updated V2 runbooks use Managed Identities. Legacy runbooks may fail when Run As Account is deprecated.
solution: Three identification methods: 1) Check runbook Jobs in Automation Account - if name contains V2 (e.g. RestartAzureVMAlertGlobalRunbookV2), it is the updated version. 2) Use Get-AzAutomationWebhook to list webhooks and match to Action Group JSON view under automationRunbookReceivers. 3) As support engineer: find webhook name in ASC Action Group details, then query OaaS Kusto cluster (oaasprodeu.northeurope.kusto.windows.net / OaaSProd) - query AzureAutomationAuditLogs + EtwAll by webhook name and correlationId. If RunbookName ends with V2, it is updated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/Identify if an Action Group runbook action uses a legacy built-in runbook or an updated built-in runbook

symptom: Need to analyze Activity Log Alert event evaluation history in sovereign clouds (Mooncake/Fairfax) where ASC execution history is not yet available
rootCause: Activity log alerts re-platforming is not yet completed in sovereign clouds, so ASC execution history tab is unavailable. Must use Kusto directly.
solution: Query SettingEvaluation table in sovereign Kusto clusters. Mooncake: azureinsightsmc.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn (database: azureinsightsmc). Fairfax: azureinsightsff.usgovtexas.kusto.usgovcloudapi.net (database: azureinsightsff). KQL: SettingEvaluation | where TIMESTAMP between (StartTime..EndTime) | where isnotempty(ruleTriggered) | where scopeId == alertRuleSubscriptionId | where ruleResourceGroup == alertRuleResourceGroup | where ruleName == alertRuleName | join kind=inner (EventData) on $left.eventId == $right.eventDataId | project TIMESTAMP, scopeId, ruleTriggered, eventId, category, eventTimestamp, operationName, properties
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Activity Log Alerts/How to analyze Activity Log Alert event evaluation history in Kusto for sovereign clouds

symptom: Service Health Alert rules do not fire as expected; alerts configured with invalid or outdated service names via REST API or ARM template fail silently
rootCause: A bug allowed customers to create Service Health alert rules with invalid/outdated service names through API or ARM templates. The portal Services dropdown showed valid names, but API/ARM had no validation, so rules with wrong names never matched incoming events.
solution: PG deployed a fix (rollout started Dec 2023, completed ~Feb 2024): (1) Validation now rejects invalid service names on create via API/ARM; (2) Existing rules with invalid names are automatically mapped to correct names behind the scenes (transparent to user). Customer action: run the provided PowerShell script (Azure Automation with System Managed Identity + Monitoring Reader role) to identify misconfigured rules. Valid service names available via API: GET https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.ResourceHealth/metadata?api-version=2022-10-01. Customers may start receiving alerts for services they previously didn't (because previously invalid rules are now valid). Tracking ID: JN_B-HSZ.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Activity Log Alerts/How to identify Service Health Alerts configured with invalid service names using PowerShell

symptom: Alert processing rules with filters on non-common alert schema fields (e.g., resourceName, resourceId, portalLink, Alertrulename, description, Alertcontextid) stop working after bug fix rollout; breaking change for rules using non-standard alert context payload filters
rootCause: Bug in alert processing rules allowed filtering on non-common alert schema fields in alert context (payload) filter; fix enforces only common alert schema fields as documented
solution: Migrate alert processing rule filters to use alternative filter types: Platform/Resource Health alerts → use "Resource" filter instead of resourceName/resourceId; Application Insights/Log Alerts v2 → use "Alert rule name" and "Description" filters; Log Analytics v1 → review filter after rollout as format changes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Alerts Management/How to change alert processing rule filters on alert context (payload) to match those of the common alert schema

symptom: Need to query fired alert instances programmatically at scale across multiple subscriptions
solution: Use Azure Resource Graph (ARG) via Resource Graph Explorer in Azure Portal. Query 'alertsmanagementresources' table with KQL to get alert details (severity, status, state, firedTime, etc.). Preferred over Alerts Management API for cross-subscription queries. Also available via CLI/PS/REST.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Alerts Management/How to get fired alerts using Azure Resource Graph query

symptom: Customer accidentally deleted an alert rule and needs to recover its configuration; or ASC is down and need to retrieve alert rule config
rootCause: Alert rule accidentally deleted; configuration snapshots available in Kusto
solution: Query AlertRuleTelemtry table in azalertsprodweu.westeurope cluster (Insights database) with alert rule ARM ID. Snapshots taken every few hours. For metric alerts use MetricAlertsTelemetry table. Supports log search, activity log, metric, and Prometheus rule types. Note: search query values and description are redacted.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/All Alerts/How to get alert rule configuration using Kusto

symptom: Need to investigate fired alert history and notification flow for troubleshooting alert delivery issues
solution: Query AzureAlertsManagement database on azalertsprodweu.westeurope cluster. For metric alerts: traces table filtered by AlertRuleId + 'POST alerts/createorupdate' operation. For activity log/log search alerts: similar traces query filtered by MonitorCondition. Use operation_ParentId for full trace. Filter cloud_RoleName=='Microsoft.AlertsMgmt.Notification' for notification flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/All Alerts/How to get history of fired alerts from Kusto

symptom: ITSM v1 action fails with 'No such host is known' or 'Web App - Unavailable' or 'Error 403 - This web app is stopped'
rootCause: Server error from ServiceNow side - ServiceNow instance is unavailable or stopped
solution: Customer should reach out to ServiceNow support to investigate their instance availability
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/ITSM/How to interpret ITSM v1 action failures in ASC

symptom: ITSM v1 action fails with 'AccessToken and RefreshToken invalid. User needs to authenticate again'
rootCause: ITSM connector token expired or integration rights missing for OMS_App_User
solution: 1. Re-enter credentials in ITSM connector configuration and re-sync. 2. Ensure integration user (OMS_App_User) was created and configured correctly per documentation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/ITSM/How to interpret ITSM v1 action failures in ASC

symptom: ITSM v1 action fails with 'Failed to retrieve connection configuration'
rootCause: Newly created ITSM Connector instance has not finished initial sync or connector was not defined correctly
solution: Re-enter credentials in ITSM connector configuration and re-sync
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/ITSM/How to interpret ITSM v1 action failures in ASC

symptom: ITSM v1 action fails with 'Target record not found. Operation against file incident was aborted by Business Rule'
rootCause: ServiceNow Business Rules (server-side logic) are rejecting/ignoring the incident creation
solution: Customer should reach out to ServiceNow support to review and update the business logic/rules
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/ITSM/How to interpret ITSM v1 action failures in ASC

symptom: ITSM v1 action fails with 'Target record not found. Row transform ignored by onBefore script'
rootCause: Custom script deployed in ServiceNow or modified OMS Integrator app code is causing incidents to be ignored
solution: Customer should reach out to ServiceNow support to disable or adjust the custom script
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/ITSM/How to interpret ITSM v1 action failures in ASC

symptom: Log search alerts fire with delay; customer observes 'delay in mins' value in ASC Execution History
rootCause: Log Search Alerts have built-in retry mechanism (30s, 5min, 30min intervals) that re-queries for late-arriving data. Non-optimized flow retries same period; optimized flow uses sliding window model. Maximum retry cutoff is 30 minutes.
solution: 1. Check Execution History in ASC for the fired alert. 2. Find the Operation ID and check Alert latency in Execution Summary. 3. Review Execution Logs for retry details. 4. Check if alert rule is optimized via Properties > Is Optimized field. 5. Late-arriving data (<30 min) is expected to cause delay due to retry mechanism.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to Validate Log Search Alerts fired with delay - Retry mechanism
Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to interpret log alert evaluation failure messages
symptom: Log alert evaluation fails with 403 Forbidden: Given credentials do not have access to read logs (no NSP details)
solution: Customer edits the alert rule (even a simple enable/disable) with user credentials that have read access to query the logs. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/manage-access
rootCause: For non-managed-identity rules, permissions are based on the last user who edited the rule. That user lost or never had read permissions on the target workspace/table.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to interpret log alert evaluation failure messages
symptom: Log alert evaluation fails with 403 Forbidden and NspValidationFailedError: Access denied due to failed Network Security Perimeter validation
solution: Adjust NSP settings: either include the log search alert rule in the perimeter, or whitelist Alerts and Action Groups IP addresses in NSP inbound rules.
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace is configured with NSP that requires resources to be within the perimeter. The log search alert rule is not part of the NSP or no inbound rules allow Alerts to query the workspace.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to interpret log alert evaluation failure messages
symptom: Log alert evaluation fails with 400 BadRequest: Failed to resolve scalar expression named <column>; or column not found in Measure/Dimensions
solution: Ensure the column referenced in Measure/Dimensions is returned by the query, or change the measure/dimension to a field that the query actually returns.
rootCause: Column selected in Measure or Dimensions is not returned by the alert rule query. Rule was created before validation was deployed, or customer used -skipQueryValidation flag.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to interpret log alert evaluation failure messages
symptom: Log alert evaluation fails with 404 NotFound: The workspace could not be found / The application could not be found
solution: Delete the alert rule and create a new one with the updated scope pointing to the correct workspace/AI component.
rootCause: The Log Analytics workspace or Application Insights component that holds the data was moved or deleted.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to interpret log alert evaluation failure messages
symptom: Log alert with managed identity fails with 403 Forbidden or identity-related errors (unexpected response payload, resource does not have access, identity not found)
solution: 1) Grant the managed identity read access on all queried workspaces/ADX clusters. 2) For inconsistency errors, disable/enable the rule. 3) If identity is missing, add a managed identity to the rule. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview
rootCause: Managed identity associated with the alert rule lacks read access on all workspaces/data sources queried, or identity was deleted/detached, or MSI state inconsistency between ARM and LSA.

solution: Add ingestion_time() filter to reproduce evaluation-time data: append | where TimeGenerated >= datetime(QueryStartTime) and TimeGenerated <= datetime(QueryEndTime) | where ingestion_time() < datetime(FinalSearchExecutionTime) to the customer query. For join/union queries, add the filter to each table separately. Run in ASC Query Customer Data tab with custom time range.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to validate query execution results on false or missed Log alert
rootCause: Ingestion delay of logs to Kusto causes alert query evaluation against partial data. Results differ because more data arrives after evaluation time.
symptom: Log alert fired when it should not have (false alert) or missed firing (missed alert); re-running customer query returns different results than alert evaluation

solution: Replace bin(TimeGenerated, interval) with bin_at(TimeGenerated, interval, datetime(QueryEndTime)) when validating alert query results to match the exact bucketing used during evaluation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to validate query execution results on false or missed Log alert
rootCause: LSA service automatically converts bin() to bin_at() at runtime using query end time as fixed reference point. Manual bin() rounds to nearest interval from epoch, causing different bucket boundaries.
symptom: Log alert query using bin() function produces different time bucketing results when re-running manually vs during alert evaluation

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Metric Alerts/How to identify Red-Black mismatch causing false or missed metric alerts
rootCause: Red-Black mismatch in Geneva Metrics: metric data is not identical on both instances (red/black) of the stamp, causing inconsistent threshold evaluation results.
symptom: Metric alert fires falsely or misses firing; metric value differs between red and black instances of Geneva Metrics stamp
solution: In ASC evaluation logs, add the second instance (-red or -black suffix) to the Datacenter filter, then filter by LogTag in (ThresholdViolated,CombinationEvaluation). Compare ThresholdViolated and metric values between instances. If one shows ThresholdViolated and the other does not → confirmed red-black mismatch (escalate to PG).

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Metric Alerts/How to use the Metric Delay dashboard to identify late arriving data
rootCause: Late arriving metric data: metric ingestion latency exceeded the 3-minute buffer that metric alerts enforce before firing. Alert evaluated against incomplete metric values.
symptom: Metric alert fired when it should not have (false alert); metric value at evaluation time differs from resource metric chart value
solution: Check MetricDelay Jarvis dashboard (https://jarvis-west.dc.ad.msft.net/dashboard/MetricTeamInternalMetrics/Sla/MetricDelay). Set Metrics Backend and Environment, filter by evaluation time. For platform metrics check Metric Delay chart; for custom metrics (CUSTOM account or azmon stamp) check Metric Puller Service absolute publication delay chart. If latency > 180 seconds at metric data window end time → confirmed false alert due to late arriving data.

symptom: Prometheus rule group updates not taking effect after modification - changes made to rule group are not being processed by the data plane
rootCause: Synchronizer component (ModifiedRuleGroupIdsSynchronizerRunner / RuleGroupsSynchronizerProcessor) failed to sync rule group updates from ARM control plane to data plane in Azalertsprodweu cluster
solution: 1) Query AlertRuleTelemtry in Azalertsprodweu/Insights with RuleArmId to get RuleUpdateTimes 2) Check synchronizer processing logs in Application Insights (promalerts-ai-privatepreview) for ModifiedRuleGroupIdsSynchronizerRunner + RuleGroupsSynchronizerProcessor entries 3) If processing logs empty, updates were not synced 4) If error message found, open CRI using "Azure Monitor | Prometheus alerts control plane" escalation template in ASC
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Prometheus alerts (public preview)/How to check if updates to Prometheus rule group were processed from Kusto

symptom: Customer wants to restore deleted alert rules or action groups
rootCause: Most Azure Monitor alert resource types (Log Search Alert, Activity Log Alert, Service Health Alert, Metric Alerts, Smart Alert Rules) cannot be restored once deleted. Only Action Groups support restoration via ICM.
solution: For deleted Action Groups: request restoration via ICM to PG. For deleted alert rules (all types): PG can only provide last known configuration — customer must recreate the rule manually using that info.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Known Issues/Resource Recoverability

symptom: Log alert notification does not contain search results in payload
rootCause: Starting with log alerts API version 2021-08-01, search results were removed from alert notification payload. Only rules created with older API (2018-04-16) still include them.
solution: Use Logic Apps to add search results back to notification. See 'Customize alert notifications by using Logic Apps' in MS Learn docs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Support Area Paths (SAPs)/Action or notification did not work as expected/Action or notification has an unexpected content

symptom: MetricValue field shows as 'null' in resolved log alert notification
rootCause: By design — stateful log alerts use time-based resolution logic rather than value-based. No metric value caused the alert to resolve; it resolved due to elapsed time.
solution: This is expected behavior. Inform customer that stateful log alerts resolve based on time, not value, so MetricValue will be null in resolved notifications.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Support Area Paths (SAPs)/Action or notification did not work as expected/Action or notification has an unexpected content

symptom: Log alert fired as expected when no results returned, but dimensions list is empty or alert title does not contain dimension name
rootCause: By design — when a query returns 0 rows, the resource ID field (basis for populating dimension and title fields) is empty, resulting in empty dimensions and missing dimension names in the title.
solution: This is expected behavior. When no rows are returned, dimension information cannot be populated. Inform customer this is by-design behavior.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Support Area Paths (SAPs)/Action or notification did not work as expected/Action or notification has an unexpected content

symptom: Custom properties defined in alert rule are missing from email, SMS, or push notifications
rootCause: By design — custom properties are only passed to action payloads (webhook, Azure function, logic app), not to notification channels (email, SMS, push).
solution: Custom properties only work with actions (webhook/Azure Function/Logic App). If customer needs custom data in email, use Logic App with email action instead of direct email notification.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Support Area Paths (SAPs)/Action or notification did not work as expected/Action or notification has an unexpected content

symptom: Log search alert remains in fired state and does not resolve automatically when the alert condition is no longer met
rootCause: Alert rule not configured as stateful (Automatically resolve alerts not enabled), or alert processing rules suppress resolved condition notifications, or retention period exceeded (max 30 days)
solution: Verify alert is configured as Stateful with "Automatically resolve alerts" enabled in portal (or autoMitigate=true via API). Check no alert processing rules suppress resolved condition notifications on the relevant scope. Note: maximum retention period for alert investigation is 30 days; older alerts cannot be investigated. For stateful log alerts, the alert resolves automatically when the condition is no longer met. Reference TSGs: Troubleshooting Log Search alert didn't resolve, How to check if a Log alert rule is stateful in Kusto, How to get stateful log alert state change history.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Support Area Paths (SAPs)/A fired alert wasn't resolved when it should/Log search alert issue

symptom: Metric alert remains in fired state and does not resolve automatically when the metric no longer meets the threshold
rootCause: Metric time series still meets condition within 3 consecutive evaluation periods; or alert rule defined as stateless (autoMitigate=false / Automatically resolve alerts unchecked); or alert processing rules suppress resolved condition notifications; or customer checking wrong metric time series (wrong resource/dimension)
solution: Metric alert resolves when condition not met for 3 consecutive evaluations (e.g. 15 min for 5-min aggregation granularity). 1) Verify correct metric time series — if rule monitors multiple resources or dimensions, each combination fires/resolves independently. 2) Ensure autoMitigate=true / "Automatically resolve alerts" enabled. 3) Check no alert processing rules suppress resolved condition on the relevant scope. Reference TSG: Troubleshooting Metric alert didn't resolve when it should.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Support Area Paths (SAPs)/A fired alert wasn't resolved when it should/Metric alert issue

symptom: Availability test alert fires unexpectedly (false positive); customer believes the metric conditions did not warrant an alert to fire
rootCause: Mismatch between Azure Portal metric chart settings and alert rule configuration: Aggregation type differs from alert rule, Time granularity differs from Lookback period, or dimensions configured in chart not matching those in the alert rule. Alternatively, autoMitigate=false causes new alerts even with existing unresolved alerts.
solution: 1) In Azure Portal, open the fired alert and review "Why did this alert fire?" section to see Metric Value vs Threshold at fire time. 2) Ensure Aggregation in metric chart matches Aggregation type in alert rule. 3) Ensure Time granularity matches Lookback period (not set to automatic). 4) Verify dimensions in metric chart match those in alert rule. 5) If rule fires new alerts with existing unresolved ones, check autoMitigate setting — if false, alerts won't auto-resolve and new ones keep firing. Availability alerts are metric alerts for Application Insights web test metrics. Reference TSG: Troubleshooting Metric alert fired when it shouldn't have (False Alert).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Support Area Paths (SAPs)/An alert fired when it shouldn't have/Availability test alert issue

symptom: Activity log alert fired with delay; delay observed between event occurrence (step 1) and Activity Log ingestion (step 2)
rootCause: Resource Provider submitted the event into Activity Log (OBO) with delay. Expected delay: up to ~2.5 minutes.
solution: Open a collaboration task with the applicable resource provider support team to investigate the RP-side delay.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Activity log alert fired with delay

symptom: Activity log alert fired with delay; delay between Activity Log ingestion (step 2) and internal LA workspace ingestion (step 3) for non-health events
rootCause: OBO ingestion delay in Activity Logs pipeline. Expected delay: < 1 minute.
solution: Escalate to Activity Logs PG via CRI. Use Azure Monitor | Activity Logs escalation template in ASC. Must provide EventCorrelationId found in ICM description.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Activity log alert fired with delay

symptom: Activity log alert fired with delay; delay between LA workspace data available (step 3) and query availability (step 4)
rootCause: Log Analytics ingestion latency. Expected delay: 30 seconds to 3 minutes.
solution: Escalate to LA ingestion PG via CRI. Use Azure Log Analytics | Ingestion escalation template in ASC. Provide LA workspace details.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Activity log alert fired with delay

symptom: Activity log alert fired with delay; delay between query availability (step 4) and alert sent to AMP (step 5)
rootCause: Activity Log Alerts evaluation engine processing delay. Expected delay: up to 3-4 minutes.
solution: Escalate to Alerts data plane team via CRI. Use Azure Monitor | Activity Log Alerts - Data Plane - False or missed alerts escalation template in ASC.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Activity log alert fired with delay

symptom: Activity log alert fired with delay; delay between AMP receiving alert (step 5) and AMP acknowledgment (step 6)
rootCause: Alerts Management Platform (AMP) component processing delay. Expected delay: ~30 seconds.
solution: Escalate to AMP PG via CRI. Use Azure Monitor | Alert Management Platform (AMP) escalation template in ASC.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Activity log alert fired with delay

symptom: Event Hub action not triggered after alert fired; exception in notification trace: target Event Hub does not exist
rootCause: Target Event Hub configured in action group does not exist or namespace/name is misconfigured
solution: Verify Event Hub name, namespace, and connection settings in the action group. Ensure the Event Hub exists and the action group has proper authorization to send messages.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Azure Event Hub actions not being received

rootCause: Application failure ratio did not exceed the baseline minimum value determined based on the app request/dependency volume. Detection rejected due to minimum ratio threshold.
symptom: Failure Anomalies (Smart Detection) alert did not fire - detector run log shows minimum ratio rejection
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Failure Anomalies alert didnt fire when it should (Missed Alert)
solution: Check azalertsprodweu/DeepInsights traces for SmartDetectorId=FailureAnomaliesDetector. Look for detection rejected due to minimum ratio message. Compare actual failure ratio against MinRatio threshold. This is expected behavior - no alert should fire.

rootCause: Failure spike rate was below the 3-Sigma threshold (baseline average rate + 3 * standard deviation). For example, spike at 0.079% but threshold is 0.185% (baseline 0.038% + 3*0.049%).
symptom: Failure Anomalies (Smart Detection) alert did not fire - detector run log shows 3-Sigma rejection
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Failure Anomalies alert didnt fire when it should (Missed Alert)
solution: Check azalertsprodweu/DeepInsights traces for SmartDetectorId=FailureAnomaliesDetector. Look for Rejection Reason = 3-Sigma in DeepInsightResult. Compare DetectionSpikeRate against DetectionBaselineThreshold. This is expected behavior - failure spike not significant enough.

symptom: Azure Monitor Investigation (AIOps) fails with 'No access' result. User cannot run investigations on alert rules.
rootCause: User lacks required role assignment. Must have Issue Contributor, Monitoring Contributor, or Contributor role for the target resource.
solution: Assign the user Issue Contributor, Monitoring Contributor, or Contributor role for the target resource. Contact subscription owner for access.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Issues and Investigation

symptom: Azure Monitor Investigation returns 'No findings' result. AIOps investigation does not detect any anomalies.
rootCause: Investigation did not detect any anomalies in metrics, logs, or monitored data. This is expected behavior and does not necessarily indicate absence of issues.
solution: Expected behavior - no anomalies detected in current data. Does not rule out underlying issues. Consider manual investigation of specific metrics and logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Issues and Investigation

symptom: Azure Monitor Investigation shows OpenAI failure error. Investigation summary generation fails due to OpenAI service issues.
rootCause: Azure Monitor Investigation depends on OpenAI for generating summaries. OpenAI may experience temporary issues.
solution: Refresh page and run investigation again from scratch. While OpenAI is unavailable, users can still review detected anomalies without the summary.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Issues and Investigation

symptom: Log Search Alert (LSA) fires false alert when using adx("") or arg("") cross-resource query pattern — managed identity lacks permissions on the target data source (ADX cluster or ARG resources), causing query to return empty results which meets the threshold condition
rootCause: The managed identity assigned to the log alert rule does not have the required permissions (e.g., Reader role) on the external data source — Azure Data Explorer cluster for adx() queries or target resources for arg() queries
solution: Add the necessary RBAC permissions to the managed identity on the external data source. For ADX: grant at least Viewer role on the ADX cluster/database. For ARG: grant Reader on the queried resources. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/alerts-create-log-alert-rule/#managed-id
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Log Search alert fired when it shouldn't have (False Alert)

symptom: Customer believes Log Search Alert fired incorrectly (false alert) — customer's manual query re-run shows different results from alert evaluation, but customer used incorrect time range or didn't account for now()/ago()/bin() function timestamp conversion
rootCause: Kusto time functions (now(), ago(), bin()) in the alert query are internally converted to absolute timestamps relative to the evaluation time. When customers manually re-run the query later, these functions resolve to different timestamps, producing different results that make the alert appear incorrect
solution: Use the Troubleshooting Query from ASC Alert run details tab (which uses absolute timestamps matching the evaluation time) instead of re-running the original query. Ensure the query time range matches the evaluation time range exactly. Reference the Log Alerts optimization flow docs if LSA_optimization_sliding_win appears in results
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Log Search alert fired when it shouldn't have (False Alert)

symptom: Metric alert fired as false alert; metric chart shows different value than evaluation data due to aggregation window mismatch
rootCause: Time granularity in the metric chart does not match the alert rule's period/evaluation window, causing discrepancy between the charted metric and the evaluation data used by the alert engine
solution: Ensure the metric chart's Time Granularity field matches the alert rule's period. Use the Metric Data Window field from ASC Execution History to align the evaluation window exactly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Metric alert fired when it shouldn't have (False Alert)

symptom: Metric alert fires unexpectedly or stays in fired state constantly; evaluation values differ from metric chart; metric values of 0 are treated as nulls
rootCause: Resource Provider has FillGapsWithZero=false (ValueOnNull property), causing Azure Monitor to interpret emitted 0 values as Nulls. Partial null windows cause different aggregation between metric chart and alert evaluation.
solution: Check the ValueOnNull property in the alert rule's monitor configuration via ASC. If confirmed as FillGapsWithZero=false, submit a collaboration request to the Resource Provider team to update their metric configuration if possible.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Metric alert fired when it shouldn't have (False Alert)

symptom: Metric alert fired as false alert; plotting metric now shows different values than those evaluated at fire time; transient discrepancy
rootCause: Red-Black mismatch — a transient issue in backend metric storage systems where metric values at evaluation time differed from the backfilled values available later
solution: Use the MetricDelay dashboard to identify Red-Black mismatch (see How to identify Red-Black mismatch wiki page). Provide customer RCA communication explaining transient backend issue and metric backfill. If customer needs deeper RCA, escalate using 'Azure Monitor | Metric Alerts - Data Plane - False Alerts' ICM template.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Metric alert fired when it shouldn't have (False Alert)

symptom: Metric alert fired with incorrect metric values; evaluation log shows one value while MDM/Jarvis shows a different value for the same timestamp
rootCause: Late arriving data — metric ingestion latency exceeds the 3-minute wait window, causing the alert rule to evaluate against incomplete/partial metric data
solution: Verify late arriving data using the MetricDelay dashboard. Provide customer RCA explaining metric delay and backfill. If customer needs deeper RCA, escalate using 'Azure Monitor | Metric Alerts - Data Plane - False Alerts' ICM template.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Metric alert fired when it shouldn't have (False Alert)

symptom: Prometheus/PromQL-based metric alert fired but expression returns no data when queried retrospectively; data latency caused false alert evaluation
rootCause: Data latency issue — at evaluation time, delayed data satisfied the alert condition, but data was since backfilled showing different values
solution: Customer can add an offset modifier of 1-2 minutes to their PromQL rule query (see Prometheus offset modifier documentation). In rare cases where it's a collection or metric store issue, escalate CRI to 'Container Insights / AzureManagedPrometheusAgent'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Query Metric alert fired when it shouldn't have (False-Alert)

symptom: HTTP 400 Bad Request error when creating ITSM connection in Azure Monitor; error 'ITSM is not supported in this region'
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace is in an unsupported region for ITSM Connector
solution: Create or move the Log Analytics workspace to a supported region. Supported regions: East US, West US 2, South Central US, West Central US, US Gov Arizona, US Gov Virginia, Canada Central, West Europe, South UK, Southeast Asia, Japan East, Central India, Australia Southeast.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting errors creating ITSM connection

symptom: Error creating ITSM connection: 'Microsoft OMS Integrator App not installed in Service Now Instance'
rootCause: Customer did not install the 'User App for Microsoft OMS integration' from the ServiceNow Store, which is a prerequisite for ITSM Connector
solution: Install 'User App for Microsoft OMS integration' from the ServiceNow Store as documented in 'Connect ServiceNow to IT Service Management Connector in Azure' public documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting errors creating ITSM connection

symptom: Error creating/updating ITSM connection: 'access_denied', 'server_error', or Unauthorized response when authenticating with ServiceNow
rootCause: Misconfiguration of ServiceNow service integration user and/or roles; the authentication credentials provided for the ITSM connection are invalid or insufficient
solution: Validate authentication by sending a direct token request to ServiceNow using Postman or similar tool with the same credentials. If response is Unauthorized, it's a ServiceNow configuration issue — fix user roles and permissions per the ITSM Connector documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting errors creating ITSM connection

symptom: Duplicate records in Application Insights telemetry tables (e.g., AppRequests) — identical rows with same timestamp, operation_Id, id, sdkVersion etc.
rootCause: When using LA-backed Application Insights with Diagnostic Settings configured to export to a Log Analytics workspace, data is ingested into the App* tables both directly (from the AI component) and through diagnostic settings, causing exact duplicates
solution: Create custom tables (e.g., AppRequests_CL) with the same schema as the original App* tables. Add workspace transformation rules to block data from reaching the original tables (using a dummy filter like 'source | where AppRoleInstance == never32323') and redirect the diagnostic settings data to the custom tables via outputStream. Repeat for each affected table.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Avoid Duplication in Diagnostic Settings Using Transformation Rules

symptom: Log to Metric (LAMI) alert fired when it shouldn't have - metric value observed doesn't match evaluation data
rootCause: Ingestion latency in Northstar pipeline or LogToMetric-to-MDM pipeline caused stale/delayed metric values to be used during Geneva Health evaluation, resulting in false threshold violation
solution: Step 1: Verify corresponding log alert for data ingestion exists. Step 2: Review metric chart in ASC. Step 3: Compare metric values with evaluation logs (Jarvis ThresholdViolated). Step 4: Check Northstar pipeline latency via Kusto. Step 5: Check LogToMetric-to-MDM latency. If LogToMetricToMDMLatencyInMS high → escalate to Metric Alerts PG. If maxLatency high → escalate to Log Analytics GDS team via ICM to Geneva Monitoring/Consumption Platform CRIs Team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Log to Metric alert fired when it shouldn't have (False Alert)

symptom: Prometheus/Query-based Metric alert didn't fire when it should have (missed alert) - expression returns data but alert not triggered
rootCause: Data latency issue: at evaluation time, Prometheus metric store returned 0 timeseries due to collection/ingestion delay, even though data exists when checked later
solution: Customer can add offset modifier (1-2 minutes) to the PromQL expression per Prometheus docs. Example: rate(http_requests_total[5m] offset 2m). In rare cases, it may be a collection or metric store issue - file CRI to Container Insights / AzureManagedPrometheusAgent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Query Metric alert didn't fire when it should (Missed-Alert)

symptom: Metric alert didn't resolve when it should have - metric value is below threshold but alert remains in fired state
rootCause: Metric alerts require 3 consecutive healthy evaluations to resolve. If any evaluation within the 3-consecutive window still shows threshold violated, the alert won't resolve.
solution: Verify in ASC Execution History that 3 consecutive healthy evaluations have occurred. Use Jarvis evaluation logs: filter by Method contains 'EvaluateCombination' and Message startswith 'Monitor'. Check each evaluation shows healthy state. If 3 consecutive healthy evals confirmed but alert still not resolved → escalate to PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Metric alert didn't resolve when it should (Missed Alert)

symptom: Cannot create tenant-level Service Health alert rule - unauthorized error 'No access'
rootCause: User lacks tenant admin access. Creating tenant-level SH alert rules requires read access to tenant data via an Entra role (e.g., Global Reader).
solution: Ensure the user has tenant admin access per docs: Roles with tenant admin access (Azure Service Health). If user lacks permissions, they should consult their subscription owner for access grant.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Tenant Level Service Health Alerts

symptom: Fired tenant-level Service Health alerts are not shown in the Azure portal alerts list
rootCause: By design - current release does not support displaying fired tenant-level SH alerts in the alerts list. Alert actions (email, webhook) will contain a link to the Service Health portal page instead.
solution: This is expected behavior. Advise customer to view tenant-level events directly in the Service Health page in the Azure portal. Support for displaying fired tenant-level SH alerts in alerts lists will be added in future releases.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Tenant Level Service Health Alerts

symptom: Tenant-level Service Health alert rule didn't fire for subscription-level Service Health events
rootCause: Tenant-level SH events only cover tenant-related (Entra) issues. They are separate from subscription-level events and won't fire for subscription-scoped problems.
solution: Customer needs to create separate alert rules with different scopes: (1) A Service Health alert rule scoped to the tenant for tenant-level events; (2) A Service Health alert rule scoped to each subscription they want to monitor for subscription-level events.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Tenant Level Service Health Alerts

symptom: Alert email notification not received - email action was disabled/unsubscribed by a distribution group member
rootCause: Someone with access to the email (especially distribution group members) clicked the unsubscribe link in a previous alert notification, which disabled the email action in the action group.
solution: Customer needs to remove and re-add the email action to the action group. This will send a subscription confirmation email. Warn customer to notify all DL members not to unsubscribe again. Check action group configuration in ASC to confirm action shows Enabled/Disabled status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting email notifications not being received

symptom: Alert email notification not received for Email Azure Resource Manager Role type - user has the role but didn't get the email
rootCause: ARM Role emails only sent to Azure AD users/groups directly assigned at subscription scope with primary SMTP address. Common issues: user assigned at wrong scope (resource group instead of subscription), email set as additional address instead of primary SMTP, or user is a service principal.
solution: Verify: (1) User is directly assigned the role at subscription scope (not inherited). (2) User's email is set as the Primary SMTP address property (not additional email). (3) User is an AAD user or AD group (not service principal). Check via ASC: user role/permissions/assignment scope + user AAD info.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting email notifications not being received

symptom: Service Health alert email shows NotificationState=Skipped in ASC notification data
rootCause: Service Health alerts use a separate dial-tone pipeline. The normal AzNS pipeline shows Skipped status because the notification was processed by the dial-tone pipeline instead. The Notification ID in ASC differs from the one in Kusto logs.
solution: This is expected behavior for Service Health alerts. Use Kusto logs to get the correct dial-tone Notification ID and trace the actual notification delivery. The ASC Notification ID is from the skipped normal pipeline.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting email notifications not being received

symptom: Prometheus rule group creation fails with 'Resource not found' or 'Target scope doesn't exist or not in the same location'
rootCause: Azure Monitor workspace (monitoring account) does not exist, was moved to a different resource group, or is in a different region than the rule group
solution: Verify: (1) Target Azure Monitor workspace exists and use the correct resource ID. (2) Ensure the rule group and target AMW are in the same region. (3) Customer needs at least read permissions on the AMW to create rule groups.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Prometheus rule group create, update and delete operations

symptom: Prometheus rule group creation/update fails with expression syntax error or payload validation error
rootCause: Invalid PromQL expression syntax or rule group property values outside allowed ranges (e.g., Interval must be 1-15, TimeToResolve must be 1-180)
solution: For syntax errors: refer customer to Prometheus.io querying/basics documentation for correct PromQL syntax. For payload validation: error messages are self-explanatory with allowed ranges. For unexplained server errors (500): use Kusto to investigate operation traces. For successful update not reflecting: check Kusto if updates were processed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Prometheus rule group create, update and delete operations

symptom: Metric alert didn't fire but evaluation shows threshold violated — alert is already in a fired state because threshold is constantly met
rootCause: Metric alerts are stateful. If the alert fired previously and was never resolved (threshold continuously breached), subsequent violations are skipped because the alert is already in 'Error' state.
solution: Check 'Last Fired' in ASC. Review extended metric chart since last fired time. Explain stateful nature of metric alerts to customer. Assist with fine-tuning alert rule condition/threshold to allow resolution cycles.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Metric alert didn't fire when it should (Missed Alert)

symptom: Metric alert missed — evaluation data shows different values than metric chart; evaluation skipped due to null data
rootCause: Resource provider has FillGapsWithZero=false configuration, causing Azure Monitor to interpret 0 values as Nulls. If the entire evaluation window has nulls, evaluation is skipped. Partial nulls cause aggregation discrepancies.
solution: Check evaluation logs for 'SkipOnNoDataException' message. The FillGapsWithZero (ValueOnNull property) is determined by each resource provider. Submit a collaboration request to the RP to update their metric configuration if possible.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Metric alert didn't fire when it should (Missed Alert)

symptom: Metric alert missed — metric chart now shows values meeting threshold but evaluation at the time didn't fire
rootCause: Red-Black mismatch: a transient issue in one of the backend systems responsible for metric alert evaluation caused discrepancies between evaluation data and actual metric values (metric values were since backfilled).
solution: Follow 'How to identify Red-Black mismatch causing false or missed metric alerts'. Provide customer communication template explaining the transient backend issue. If customer wants RCA, use 'Azure Monitor | Metric Alerts - Data Plane - Missed Alerts' CRI template.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Metric alert didn't fire when it should (Missed Alert)

symptom: Metric alert missed — evaluation log shows metric value not meeting threshold but Jarvis/MDM shows it should have met threshold at that time
rootCause: Late arriving data: metric ingestion latency exceeds the 3-minute wait time before metric alert evaluation. Alert evaluates against inaccurate (incomplete) metric values.
solution: Verify using the MetricDelay dashboard. Provide customer communication template about late arriving data. If customer needs RCA or MetricDelay findings are inconclusive, escalate via 'Azure Monitor | Metric Alerts - Data Plane - Missed Alerts' CRI template.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Metric alert didn't fire when it should (Missed Alert)

symptom: Resource Health alert didn't fire — activity log event exists but payload doesn't match alert rule condition (e.g., cause field mismatch)
rootCause: Alert rule condition specifies a specific cause (e.g., PlatformInitiated) but the actual Resource Health event has a different cause (e.g., Unknown). The event properties don't satisfy all allOf/anyOf conditions in the alert rule.
solution: Compare the activity log event payload against the alert rule condition JSON. Check fields like currentHealthStatus, previousHealthStatus, and cause. If event matches condition but alert still didn't fire, escalate to Activity Log Alerts PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Resource Health alerts

symptom: Prometheus alert didn't fire — PromQL expression returns no data in the values key
rootCause: The PromQL expression's comparator condition is not met by the actual metric values. Prometheus rules need a returned value to consider the alert triggered — if no values satisfy the comparison, the alert won't fire.
solution: Remove the comparator from the expression and re-run to see raw values. Compare actual values with the expression threshold. Verify the expression in ASC 'Query Prometheus Endpoint with Range' or customer's Managed Grafana.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Prometheus alert didn't fire when it should (Missed Alert)

symptom: Prometheus alert didn't fire — expression returns data but Execution History shows 0 returned time series
rootCause: Data latency issue: at the time of evaluation, the Prometheus engine received 0 timeseries due to delayed metric data, even though the data is available when queried now.
solution: Customer can add an offset modifier of 1-2 minutes to the rule query (see Prometheus offset modifier syntax). In rare cases, it could be a collection or metric store issue — escalate CRI to 'Container Insights / AzureManagedPrometheusAgent'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Prometheus alert didn't fire when it should (Missed Alert)

symptom: Query-based metric alert (PromQL criteria) didn't resolve when expected
rootCause: Auto-resolve is set to false on the alert rule, making it stateless — the alert will never automatically resolve.
solution: Check alert rule properties in ASC for 'auto-resolve' and 'time for resolution' settings. If auto-resolve is false, the alert is stateless and won't resolve. Customer needs to enable auto-resolve and set appropriate resolution time.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Query Metric alert didn't resolve when it should (Missed-Alert)

symptom: Query-based metric alert (PromQL) didn't resolve — expression still returns values
rootCause: The alert condition is still being met. The PromQL expression continues to return values during the evaluation period, so the alert remains in condition-met state.
solution: Validate the expression in ASC or Managed Grafana. The condition must be in un-met state (expression returns no data) consistently for the configured 'time to resolve' period before a resolution notification is sent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Query Metric alert didn't resolve when it should (Missed-Alert)

symptom: ITSM secure webhook action failed — NotificationState is Failed in ASC fired alerts history
rootCause: ITSM secure webhook not configured properly. Customer may have missed steps in the Azure-to-ITSM secure webhook configuration.
solution: Ensure customer followed all steps in 'Configure Azure to connect ITSM tools using Secure Webhook' (MS Learn doc). If NotificationState is Complete but work item not created, the issue is on ServiceNow side — refer customer to ServiceNow support.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting an ITSM action that failed to trigger or was not received appropriately

symptom: ITSM connector incident not mapped to configuration item — CI field in ServiceNow work item is empty or partial
rootCause: Query results don't contain a Resource/Computer column, or the configuration item in query results doesn't exactly match items in ServiceNow cmdb_ci database.
solution: 1) Update query to return resource/computer column. 2) Add missing CIs to cmdb_ci. 3) Or enable 'Create New Configuration Item in ITSM Product' checkbox in ITSM connector config. Note: CI mapping is NOT supported for metric alerts (by design).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting an ITSM action that failed to trigger or was not received appropriately

symptom: ITSM connector — ServiceNow predefined templates don't appear in templates dropdown of ITSM ticket configuration UI
rootCause: Expired authentication tokens in the ITSM connection.
solution: Ask customer to re-sync their ITSM connection to refresh tokens.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting an ITSM action that failed to trigger or was not received appropriately

symptom: Application Insights availability test failing with 'The remote name could not be resolved' error
rootCause: The URL used in the availability test has a DNS name that is not publicly registered. The DNS server may not be functioning properly, or the domain is only available in private DNS.
solution: Check DNS registration using resolve-dnsname (PowerShell), nslookup, or mxtoolbox.com. Ensure the DNS record is publicly discoverable. If DNS server issues found, investigate the DNS server associated with the domain.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Availability Tests References/Check URLs and Availability Test are Publicly accessible

symptom: Application Insights availability test failing with 'This url is not supported' error
rootCause: The hostname resolves to a private IP address (RFC 1918 range). Availability tests require public IP resolution.
solution: Ensure hostname resolves to a public IP, not private IP ranges. Customer must allowlist Azure Monitor availability test IPs (see 'IP addresses used by Azure Monitor' doc). For connection timeout errors, test with another known URL to isolate service-side vs customer-side issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Availability Tests References/Check URLs and Availability Test are Publicly accessible

symptom: Log search alert didn't resolve when expected — alert remains in fired/active state beyond expected time
rootCause: Resolution timing depends on evaluation frequency: 1-min frequency → 10 min; 5-15 min → 3 periods; 15 min-11 hr → 2 periods; 11-12 hr → 1 period. Customer may not be aware of the resolution delay rules. Also, if autoResolve is disabled, alerts won't resolve.
solution: Check alert rule frequency and corresponding resolution time in ASC. Verify 'Automatically resolve alerts' is enabled. Check if custom 'timeToResolve' is set (API 2022-08-01-preview). Review state change history using Kusto query for stateful log alert state changes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Log search alert didn't resolve when it should

symptom: Log search alert resolved unexpectedly — alert resolved when customer expected it to remain active
rootCause: Alert condition was not met for the required resolution period based on the evaluation frequency. Log search alert resolution is time-based: the alert resolves X time after the last time the threshold was met.
solution: Review state change history in Kusto. Check the evaluation frequency and corresponding resolution window. Verify query results during the unexpected resolution period to confirm condition was indeed not met.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Log search alert didn't resolve when it should

symptom: HTTP 400 Bad Request error when creating ITSM connection in Azure Monitor; error ITSM is not supported in this region
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace is in an unsupported region for ITSM Connector
solution: Create or move the Log Analytics workspace to a supported region: East US, West US 2, South Central US, West Central US, US Gov Arizona, US Gov Virginia, Canada Central, West Europe, South UK, Southeast Asia, Japan East, Central India, Australia Southeast.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting errors creating ITSM connection

symptom: Error creating ITSM connection: Microsoft OMS Integrator App not installed in Service Now Instance
rootCause: Customer did not install the User App for Microsoft OMS integration from the ServiceNow Store, which is a prerequisite for ITSM Connector
solution: Install User App for Microsoft OMS integration from the ServiceNow Store as documented in Connect ServiceNow to IT Service Management Connector in Azure public documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting errors creating ITSM connection

symptom: Error creating/updating ITSM connection: access_denied, server_error, or Unauthorized response when authenticating with ServiceNow
