rootCause: Misconfiguration of ServiceNow service integration user and/or roles; the authentication credentials provided for the ITSM connection are invalid or insufficient
solution: Validate authentication by sending a direct token request to ServiceNow using Postman or similar tool with the same credentials. If response is Unauthorized, it is a ServiceNow configuration issue - fix user roles and permissions per the ITSM Connector documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting errors creating ITSM connection

symptom: Duplicate records in Application Insights telemetry tables (e.g. AppRequests) - identical rows with same timestamp, operation_Id, id, sdkVersion
rootCause: When using LA-backed Application Insights with Diagnostic Settings configured to export to a Log Analytics workspace, data is ingested into App* tables both directly from the AI component and through diagnostic settings, causing exact duplicates
solution: Create custom tables (e.g. AppRequests_CL) with same schema as original App* tables. Add workspace transformation rules to block data from reaching original tables using a dummy filter and redirect diagnostic settings data to custom tables via outputStream. Repeat for each affected table.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Avoid Duplication in Diagnostic Settings Using Transformation Rules

symptom: Log to Metric (LAMI) alert fired false alert - metric value does not match evaluation data
rootCause: Ingestion latency in Northstar pipeline or LogToMetric-to-MDM pipeline caused stale/delayed metric values during Geneva Health evaluation
solution: Verify corresponding log alert exists. Review metric chart. Compare with evaluation logs (Jarvis ThresholdViolated). Check Northstar pipeline latency. Check LogToMetric-to-MDM latency. High LogToMetricToMDMLatencyInMS: escalate to Metric Alerts PG. High maxLatency: escalate to LA GDS via ICM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Log to Metric alert fired when it shouldn't have (False Alert)

symptom: Prometheus/Query Metric alert missed - expression returns data later but alert not triggered at evaluation time
rootCause: Data latency: at evaluation time Prometheus metric store returned 0 timeseries due to collection/ingestion delay
solution: Add offset modifier (1-2 min) to PromQL expression: rate(metric[5m] offset 2m). In rare cases file CRI to Container Insights / AzureManagedPrometheusAgent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Query Metric alert didn't fire when it should (Missed-Alert)

symptom: Metric alert did not resolve - metric below threshold but alert still in fired state
rootCause: Metric alerts require 3 consecutive healthy evaluations to resolve. If any evaluation within the window still shows threshold violated, alert will not resolve.
solution: Verify in ASC Execution History that 3 consecutive healthy evaluations occurred. Use Jarvis logs: filter by Method contains EvaluateCombination and Message startswith Monitor. If confirmed but still not resolved: escalate to PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Metric alert didn't resolve when it should (Missed Alert)

symptom: Cannot create tenant-level Service Health alert rule - unauthorized error No access
rootCause: User lacks tenant admin access. Creating tenant-level SH alert rules requires read access to tenant data via an Entra role (e.g. Global Reader).
solution: Ensure user has tenant admin access per docs: Roles with tenant admin access. If lacking permissions, consult subscription owner for access grant.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Tenant Level Service Health Alerts

symptom: Tenant-level Service Health alert rule did not fire for subscription-level events
rootCause: Tenant-level SH events only cover tenant-related (Entra) issues. Separate from subscription-level events.
solution: Create separate alert rules: (1) SH alert scoped to tenant for tenant-level events; (2) SH alert scoped to each subscription for subscription-level events.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Tenant Level Service Health Alerts

symptom: Alert email notification not received - email action disabled/unsubscribed by distribution group member
rootCause: Someone (especially DL member) clicked unsubscribe link in previous alert notification, disabling the email action in action group.
solution: Remove and re-add email action to action group. Warn customer to notify all DL members not to unsubscribe. Check action group config in ASC for Enabled/Disabled status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting email notifications not being received

symptom: Alert email not received for Email Azure Resource Manager Role type - user has role but no email
rootCause: ARM Role emails only sent to AAD users/groups directly assigned at subscription scope with primary SMTP address. Common: wrong scope, additional email instead of primary SMTP, or service principal.
solution: Verify: (1) User directly assigned at subscription scope. (2) Email is Primary SMTP address. (3) User is AAD user or AD group (not service principal).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting email notifications not being received

symptom: Prometheus rule group creation fails with Resource not found or Target scope not in same location
rootCause: Azure Monitor workspace does not exist, was moved, or is in a different region than the rule group
solution: Verify: (1) Target AMW exists with correct resource ID. (2) Rule group and AMW in same region. (3) Customer has at least read permissions on AMW.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Prometheus rule group create, update and delete operations

symptom: Missing telemetry data in Log Analytics workspace after Data Collection Rules (DCRs) or Ingestion Time Transformations are configured on Application Insights tables
rootCause: Data Collection Rules (DCRs) or Ingestion Time Transformations are filtering out or altering telemetry before it reaches the workspace
solution: Check DCR configuration via Azure Portal → workspace Tables pane. Important: DCR filtering does NOT reduce ingestion costs (data is still ingested and charged). To reduce costs, filter telemetry at the SDK source level instead. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/data-collection-rule-overview
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Ingestion References/Check for Data Collection Rules (DCRs) and Ingestion Time Transformations

symptom: HTTP 5XX errors (Server Errors) not showing in Application Insights telemetry despite occurring on the server
rootCause: The HTTP 5XX error was generated by a component in the IIS request pipeline (HTTP.sys, W3SVC, ANCM, or IIS module) before the request reached the .NET runtime where the Application Insights SDK operates, so the SDK never intercepted the request
solution: For Linux: Query WorkerApacheAccessLogEventTable and compare Status vs UpstreamStatus columns - if UpstreamStatus is empty/null, the app container never received the request. For Windows: Check FREB logs via AntaresWebWorkerFREBLogs to determine which module set MODULE_SET_RESPONSE_STATUS_ERROR - if ManagedPipelineHandler, it was the app; if AspNetCoreModuleV2 or other module, it was infrastructure.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/HTTP 5XXs (Server Errors)

symptom: HTTP 500 errors on Windows App Services - need to determine if application or IIS infrastructure caused the error for Application Insights investigation
rootCause: An IIS native module (e.g., AspNetCoreModuleV2) returned the 500 status before the request reached ManagedPipelineHandler, meaning the application code never processed the request and Application Insights could not capture it
solution: Query AntaresWebWorkerFREBLogs filtering StatusCode==500 and inspect Details field for which module triggered MODULE_SET_RESPONSE_STATUS_ERROR. If ManagedPipelineHandler set the status → app caused it (AI should have captured it). If AspNetCoreModuleV2 or other IIS module → infrastructure issue (AI cannot capture). Use Copilot to help parse FREB output.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/HTTP 5XXs (Server Errors)

symptom: All telemetry data stops flowing into Application Insights tables periodically and resumes on a daily cycle - data ingestion follows a pattern of stopping and restarting each day
rootCause: Application Insights Daily Cap is being hit, causing all telemetry to be dropped until the cap resets the next day. Application Insights Daily Cap sits in front of Log Analytics Daily Cap, so whichever is hit first takes effect.
solution: 1) Check Throttle Status tab in ASC to confirm Daily Cap is being hit. 2) Use Read Aggregate by Dropped By Reason to identify 'Quota Exceed / Q' drops. 3) Check both AI and Log Analytics daily caps (whichever is lower takes effect first). 4) Remediate by: increasing Daily Cap, implementing more aggressive sampling, or filtering unnecessary telemetry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Ingestion References/Determine if Daily Cap is impacting telemetry

symptom: KQL query calculating daily ingestion shows value below Daily Cap limit, but Daily Cap throttling is still occurring - calculated ingestion appears lower than the configured cap
rootCause: Using Gibibyte (GiB - 1024) calculation (sum(_BilledSize)/1024/1024/1024) instead of Gigabyte (GB - 1000). Since October 2024 global rollout, Daily Cap uses decimal GB (1000) calculation to match billing. The GiB calculation yields ~7% lower values, creating a false impression the cap shouldn't be hit.
solution: Use sum(_BilledSize)/1000/1000/1000 (Gigabyte/decimal) instead of /1024/1024/1024 (Gibibyte/binary) in KQL queries to match actual Daily Cap thresholds and billing calculations. Note: KQL's format_byte() function uses GiB (1024) which will also show lower values.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Ingestion References/Determine if Daily Cap is impacting telemetry

symptom: Missing function execution logs in Application Insights for Azure Functions hosted in Function App - function invocations not appearing in AI telemetry
rootCause: The Azure Functions SDK may be failing to send telemetry to the Breeze (Application Insights ingestion endpoint), or Breeze may be dropping/partially accepting the data. The FunctionsLogs Kusto table records TransmissionStatus showing Breeze HTTP response details.
solution: In AppLens, navigate to 'Function Logs' detector under 'Availability and Performance', click 'Data Sources' then 'Run in Kusto Explorer'. Query: FunctionsLogs | where Summary contains 'TransmissionStatus' to check items sent/accepted/types. Requires runtime version 4.15.2+ (check FUNCTIONS_EXTENSION_VERSION in appsettings detector). Parse Summary column with parse_json() for detailed status codes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Ingestion References/Missing Function Logs in Application Insights

symptom: Events in ASC (Azure Support Center) don't appear in the same chronological order as shown in Azure Portal's End-to-End Transaction details or Logs blade
rootCause: ASC default timestamp ordering only resolves down to the second level, while the Azure Portal orders events down to milliseconds. Events within the same second appear in arbitrary order in ASC.
solution: Use format_datetime() to force millisecond-level precision in KQL queries. Classic schema: extend PreciseTime = format_datetime(todatetime(timestamp), 'yyyy-MM-dd hh:mm:ss fffffff') | order by PreciseTime asc. Workspace-based: extend PreciseTime = format_datetime(todatetime(TimeGenerated), 'yyyy-MM-dd hh:mm:ss fffffff') | order by PreciseTime asc.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Query and REST API References/Match Order of Events shown in End-to-End Transaction or Logs blade in ASC

symptom: Customer suspects duplicate telemetry records in Application Insights component; needs to determine duplication rate
rootCause: Duplicate records can occur due to ingestion process complexity. A low rate (~1%) is expected/normal. Higher rates may indicate SDK or auto-instrumentation configuration issues (e.g., codeless + SDK collision).
solution: Use KQL hash-based query to calculate duplicate percentage: `let _data = materialize({table}); _data | extend Hash = hash(dynamic_to_json(pack_all())) | summarize recordsCount=count() by Hash | where recordsCount > 1 | project recordsCount = recordsCount-1 | summarize duplicateRecords = sum(recordsCount)`. If <1%, considered normal. If itemId column exists, use `project-away itemId` first. To view individual duplicates: sort by timestamp, extend Hash, compare with prev(Hash).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Ingestion References/Determine the percent of duplicate data

symptom: Application Insights geolocation mapping (client_City, client_StateOrProvince, client_CountryOrRegion) shows incorrect location for known IP addresses
rootCause: Geolocation lookup chain (Vercara -> Interflow -> Ingestion service) can have stale databases at any layer. When using X-Forwarded-For headers, ingestion uses the rightmost IP (proxy) instead of client IP because client IPs are typically private.
solution: Investigation flow: 1) Check 3rd-party geo services (Maxmind/iplocation.net) - if wrong, engage Interflow team to contact Vercara. 2) Check Interflow API (POST interflowinternal.azure-api.net) - if wrong, escalate via ASC CRI 'Interflow Geolocation Mapping Issue'. 3) Check Ingestion telemetry via ASC - if wrong, escalate via ASC CRI 'Ingestion Service Geolocation Mapping Issue'. For X-Forwarded-For: rightmost IP is used by design. Updates may take days to weeks across the chain. No SLA for geomapping accuracy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Ingestion References/Troubleshoot incorrect Geolocation mapping

symptom: Application Insights portal blade shows 'Error retrieving data' message
rootCause: User only has Contributor role but is missing Reader role RBAC access on the Application Insights resource. Contributor alone is not sufficient for data retrieval in the portal.
solution: Ensure the user has Reader role RBAC access on the Application Insights resource. Contributor role is NOT sufficient for data retrieval.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Portal Experience References/Troubleshoot general portal blade issues

symptom: Query against Application Insights or Log Analytics REST API hits limits - maximum number of rows, result set size, or query rate limits - especially when exporting historical data
rootCause: API has documented limits on rows returned, result set size, and concurrent query rates. Users frequently hit this when trying to export historical data after enabling Continuous Export or Log Analytics Diagnostic logging.
solution: Use batch querying with row_number() to paginate: set batchSize (e.g., 495000), increment batchNumber each execution, use `| extend rn=row_number() | where rn between (startingRow .. endingRow)`. Pre-test to ensure batchSize does not exceed max result size. Limits documented at: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/service-limits#log-analytics-workspaces
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Query and REST API References/Query Application Insights (Log Analytics) data and effectively manage the API Limits

symptom: Log query returns error: 'Your query cannot be executed as it was throttled by the system to protect the stability and service level for all users'
rootCause: System throttles queries by design to protect stability and service level for all users when too many concurrent queries are being executed by the same user/tenant.
solution: Wait and resend the query. Refine queries to use fewer resources per KQL best practices (https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-explorer/kusto/query/best-practices). This is by-design behavior; there is nothing that can be changed on the service side.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Query and REST API References/Query Application Insights (Log Analytics) data and effectively manage the API Limits

symptom: HTTP 5XX errors visible in App Service metrics or IIS logs but not appearing in Application Insights telemetry (requests table)
rootCause: The HTTP 5XX was generated by a component in the request pipeline (HTTP.sys, IIS module, AspNetCoreModuleV2/ANCM) before the request reached the .NET runtime where the Application Insights SDK operates. The SDK can only capture telemetry once the request reaches the ASP.NET middleware pipeline (ManagedPipelineHandler).
solution: Check FREB logs (Windows) or WorkerApacheAccessLogEventTable via AppLens HTTP Server Errors detector (Linux) to determine which module generated the 5XX. Windows: query AntaresWebWorkerFREBLogs for StatusCode==500 and check if Details contains 'Module: ManagedPipelineHandler' (app-level) vs other modules (platform-level). Linux: compare Status vs UpstreamStatus columns - if UpstreamStatus is empty, the app never saw the request. If the error originated before ManagedPipelineHandler, Application Insights cannot capture it by design.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/HTTP 5XXs (Server Errors)

symptom: Telemetry missing from Application Insights tables despite SDK sending data; Data Collection Rules (DCRs) or Ingestion Time Transformations may be silently filtering out telemetry
rootCause: DCRs configured on the Log Analytics workspace contain transformation rules that filter out or alter telemetry before it reaches queryable tables; DCR transformations do NOT prevent ingestion charges
solution: Check DCRs via Azure Portal > Log Analytics workspace > Tables pane; review transformation KQL in each DCR; to reduce costs, filter telemetry at the SDK source instead of using DCR transformations which still incur ingestion charges
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Ingestion References/Check for Data Collection Rules (DCRs) and Ingestion Time Transformations

symptom: All telemetry stops flowing into Application Insights tables periodically then resumes daily after the Daily Cap reset time; pattern repeats every day
rootCause: Application Insights Daily Cap or Log Analytics Daily Cap has been reached; AI Daily Cap sits in front of LA Daily Cap so whichever is encountered first takes effect and stops all data ingestion
solution: Check throttle status in ASC (Use Throttle Status tab); check Read Aggregate by Dropped By Reason for quota exceeded (Q) indicator; verify both AI and LA daily caps; increase daily cap, implement sampling, or filter noisy telemetry at SDK source to stay under limit
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Ingestion References/Determine if Daily Cap is impacting telemetry

symptom: Daily Cap calculation using GiB (divide by 1024) shows ingestion below limit but Daily Cap is actually being triggered; discrepancy between calculated usage and cap threshold
rootCause: Calculation method mismatch: Daily Cap uses Gigabyte/decimal (GB, divide by 1000) not Gibibyte/binary (GiB, divide by 1024); GiB interpretation was temporarily used Spring 2023 to Fall 2024 but globally reverted to GB by October 31 2024; KQL format_byte() function also uses GiB internally causing further confusion
solution: Use sum(_BilledSize)/1000/1000/1000 instead of /1024/1024/1024 when comparing against Daily Cap; align query time range with Daily Cap reset time; use native queries (union browserTimings, dependencies, etc.) or workspace queries (union AppBrowserTimings, AppDependencies, etc.) with correct GB divisor
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Ingestion References/Determine if Daily Cap is impacting telemetry

symptom: Duplicate records observed in Application Insights tables (requests, pageViews, etc.); customer reports seeing same telemetry items appear multiple times
rootCause: Low rate (~1%) of duplicate data is expected in Application Insights ingestion pipeline due to retry mechanisms; higher rates may indicate SDK misconfiguration, multiple instrumentation instances, or network retries
solution: Use hash-based KQL query to determine exact duplicate percentage: materialize table, hash all columns with hash(dynamic_to_json(pack_all())), group by hash and count duplicates; if <1% it is expected/normal; use project-away itemId if table exposes that column; for viewing individual duplicates sort by timestamp and compare consecutive hashes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/Ingestion References/Determine the percent of duplicate data

symptom: Application Insights Daily Cap reset time shown in portal UI is incorrect; attempting to configure Daily Cap reset time via ARM API no longer works despite documentation stating it is configurable
rootCause: In Fall 2024, all Application Insights resources globally had their Daily Cap reset time changed to a system-defined unique per-resource time for platform stability. The UI does not properly reflect the current reset time, and the reset time is no longer configurable.
solution: Use AppLens (applens.trafficmanager.net) → look up the Application Insights resource by type → go to 'Configuration and Management' → 'Component Info' detector → copy the 'properties' column → search for 'InternalDailyCapResetTime' to find the actual reset time (UTC).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Billing/How to manage Daily Cap Reset issues

symptom: Cannot use standard Application Insights Availability Tests because the target URL is not publicly accessible on the internet
rootCause: Standard Availability Tests require public internet access to the monitored endpoint. Private or disconnected endpoints cannot be reached by the Azure-hosted test agents.
solution: Use custom availability tests: (1) Azure Function with TrackAvailability() API for endpoints in Azure VNETs — link the Function App to the same subnet; (2) For on-prem scenarios without Private Link, use a PowerShell 7 script with the Application Insights SDK (Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.dll) calling TelemetryClient.TrackAvailability() in a loop. See docs: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/availability-private-test
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Code Samples and Lab Walkthroughs/Code Samples/Availability Tests - No public internet accessibility

symptom: Entra ID authentication for Application Insights using Service Principal fails with AADSTS53003 'Access has been blocked by Conditional Access policies' when running from home or remote location (internal Microsoft tenant)
rootCause: Microsoft's internal Conditional Access policy blocks Service Principal token issuance from outside the physical Microsoft corporate network (official support sites and offices).
solution: Install, enable and connect to the MSFT-AzVPN-Manual VPN before running the application. Once connected, the SPN token acquisition will succeed. See aka.ms/AzureVPN for setup instructions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Code Samples and Lab Walkthroughs/Code Samples/Entra ID authentication/Entra ID authentication for Application Insights using Service Principal

symptom: Application Insights telemetry data is missing or lost when application shuts down; not all telemetry appears in portal after app restarts
rootCause: The Application Insights SDK sends telemetry in batches at fixed intervals (default 30 seconds) or when buffer is full (default 500 items). If application shuts down before the buffer is flushed, cached telemetry is lost.
solution: Call Flush() or FlushAsync() at application shutdown. FlushAsync() is preferred (available since SDK update) as it provides a completion status to know when all data has been sent. Only call these methods at shutdown time — calling at other times can cause issues. Example: await telemetryClient.FlushAsync(CancellationToken.None). For Node.js, see: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/nodejs#flush
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Code Samples and Lab Walkthroughs/Code Samples/Manage data lost on application shutdown and Flush usage

symptom: Application Insights SDK captures incorrect URL in RequestTelemetry when the web app is behind Azure Front Door or a layer-7 load balancer. The URL shows the private backend hostname instead of the original public-facing hostname.
rootCause: Azure Front Door (or similar L7 proxy) modifies the HTTP Host header, replacing the original public URL with the backend private URL. The Application Insights SDK reads the Host header to populate the 'url' field in RequestTelemetry, resulting in incorrect URL values.
solution: Create a custom Telemetry Initializer (ITelemetryInitializer) that reads the 'X-Forwarded-Host' HTTP header and overrides the URL property on RequestTelemetry. For ASP.NET Framework: implement the initializer class, register it in ApplicationInsights.config under <TelemetryInitializers>. Test via Postman by adding X-Forwarded-Host header to verify the override.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Code Samples and Lab Walkthroughs/Code Samples/Telemetry Initializers/Override 'url' field with the value of 'X-Forwarded-Host' HTTP header

symptom: Customer needs to disable Live Metric Stream (QuickPulse) in Application Insights while keeping all other SDK telemetry collection active.
solution: SDK deployment: In ApplicationInsights.config, locate and comment out or remove the line: <Add Type="Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.PerfCounterCollector.QuickPulse.QuickPulseTelemetryModule, Microsoft.AI.PerfCounterCollector" />. Then redeploy the application. This stops only Live Metric Stream data flow while preserving all other App Insights telemetry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Code Samples and Lab Walkthroughs/Lab Walkthroughs/Disable Live Metric Stream

symptom: Customer needs to completely disable all Application Insights telemetry collection including Live Metric Stream.
solution: SDK deployment (.NET): In your application startup code (e.g., WebApiConfig.cs), add: TelemetryConfiguration.Active.DisableTelemetry = true; Save and redeploy the application. This disables all App Insights data collection including Live Metric Stream.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Code Samples and Lab Walkthroughs/Lab Walkthroughs/Disable all Telemetry collection

symptom: Application Insights JavaScript SDK stops collecting browser-side telemetry after disabling local authentication and enabling Entra ID authentication on the App Insights resource.
rootCause: The Application Insights JavaScript SDK does not have built-in support for Entra ID-based authentication. When local auth is disabled on the App Insights resource, the JS SDK cannot authenticate its telemetry ingestion requests, causing all browser telemetry to be rejected.
solution: Deploy Azure API Management (APIM) as a telemetry proxy between the browser and App Insights ingestion endpoint: 1) Create APIM instance with System-Assigned Managed Identity; 2) Assign 'Monitoring Metrics Publisher' role to the MI on the App Insights resource; 3) Add HTTP API in APIM with backend URL pointing to ingestion endpoint /v2.1/track and API suffix /v2/track; 4) Add <authentication-managed-identity resource="https://monitor.azure.com" /> inbound policy; 5) Configure <cors> policy for browser pre-flight requests; 6) Update JavaScript SDK connection string IngestionEndpoint to point to APIM hostname. Optional: add ip-filter, rate-limit-by-key policies for security.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Code Samples and Lab Walkthroughs/Lab Walkthroughs/How to use API Management as a telemetry proxy to enforce Entra ID authentication to Application Insights ingestion service

symptom: Smart detection alert rules accidentally deleted from Application Insights resource and need to be recreated.
rootCause: Smart detection alert rules were manually deleted. If the entire resource group was deleted, recovering the AI component via ASC also recovers the alerts. But if only the alert rule itself was deleted while the AI resource still exists, it must be manually recreated via ARM template.
solution: 1) Find an existing smart detection alert of the same type (e.g., Failure Anomalies) on another App Insights resource; 2) Click 'Export template' and download the template.json and parameters.json files; 3) Modify the default parameter values (resource name, resource group, location) to match the target App Insights resource; 4) Deploy using PowerShell: New-AzResourceGroupDeployment -Name SmartDetectionAlertRecreation -ResourceGroupName <rg-name> -TemplateFile <path/template.json> -TemplateParameterFile <path/parameters.json>. ARM schema reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/templates/microsoft.alertsmanagement/smartdetectoralertrules
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Code Samples and Lab Walkthroughs/Lab Walkthroughs/Recreate deleted smart detection alerts

symptom: Application Insights not capturing HTTP 404 request telemetry — customer claims 404s visible in IIS but missing from App Insights requests table
rootCause: The HTTP 404 status code was set by IIS Web Core module (MODULE_SET_RESPONSE_ERROR_STATUS with ModuleName=IIS Web Core), not by the ASP.NET ManagedPipelineHandler. Application Insights SDK only captures telemetry at the .NET managed layer and is unaware of webserver-level events.
solution: Check FREB logs for MODULE_SET_RESPONSE_ERROR_STATUS entry — if ModuleName is 'IIS Web Core' (not 'ManagedPipelineHandler'), the 404 is set at IIS layer before reaching managed code. Customer needs to modify IIS config to route the request through the managed handler so App Insights can capture it. Same behavior applies to both codeless and SDK instrumentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Tools/Capture Failed Request Event Buffering logs (FREB Logs)

symptom: FREB (Failed Request Event Buffering) logs are truncated — XML output file is incomplete due to large file size
rootCause: Default maxLogFileSizeKB for FREB logs is too small, causing truncation when request processing generates large trace output.
solution: Create applicationHost.xdt file at d:\home\site\applicationHost.xdt with XDT transform to increase traceFailedRequestsLogging settings: set maxLogFileSizeKB to 4096/8000/16000 and maxLogFiles to 100 (default is 50). Restart the app after applying. Verify transform took effect at D:\local\Config\applicationhost.config. Reference: https://github.com/projectkudu/kudu/wiki/Xdt-transform-samples
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Tools/Capture Failed Request Event Buffering logs (FREB Logs)

symptom: Application Insights Java 3.0 Agent self-diagnostics configured with TRACE level but only captures DEBUG level output
rootCause: The environment variable APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_SELF_DIAGNOSTICS_LEVEL takes precedence over the self-diagnostics level specified in the applicationinsights.json configuration file
solution: Check if the environment variable APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_SELF_DIAGNOSTICS_LEVEL is set. Remove it or set it to TRACE to allow TRACE-level self-diagnostic log collection
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Application Insights Diagnostic Logs/Java Agent

symptom: Application Insights .NET SDK diagnostic log files (SDK Logging method) not generated when deployed to Azure App Service Web App
rootCause: The default LogFilePath may reference C: drive which is not writable in Azure App Service; the accessible drive letter varies between App Service instances (C or D)
solution: Set LogFilePath to 'D:\HOME\SDKLOGS' (or the correct drive letter for your App Service — check via Kudu default page). For SDK 2.18+, prefer Self-Diagnostics via ApplicationInsightsDiagnostics.json placed in <drive>:\home\site\wwwroot with LogDirectory set to <drive>:\home
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Application Insights Diagnostic Logs/dot-Net Core

symptom: Application Insights auto-instrumentation on .NET Core Linux App Service shows AppAlreadyInstrumented=true in status JSON — custom SDK telemetry may conflict with auto-instrumentation
rootCause: The application is already instrumented with the Application Insights SDK, causing conflict with auto-instrumentation (codeless) on the App Service
solution: Either: (1) disable auto-instrumentation in App Service Application Insights settings and rely on SDK instrumentation, or (2) enable PreemptSdk to stop SDK telemetry flow and yield to auto-instrumentation exclusively
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Status Page - App Services Web Apps and Application Insights/Net (Core) Running on Linux Web App

symptom: Java 3.X Application Insights: operation_Name / request name values only contain HTTP method (POST or GET) but no actual route/path information
rootCause: Default span naming in OpenTelemetry Java agent uses only the HTTP method without the URL path. Telemetry processor configuration is needed to combine http.request.method and url.path attributes into the span name.
solution: Configure a telemetry processor in applicationinsights.json under preview.processors: use type 'span', include matchType 'regexp' with spanNames ['POST|GET'], and set name.fromAttributes to ['http.request.method', 'url.path'] with separator ' '. Use diagnostic logs (search for AgentSpanExporter entries) to identify the correct attribute keys from the AttributesMap section of the SpanData.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Customize telemetry/Java 3.X

symptom: Java 3.X Application Insights agent: self-diagnostics configured at TRACE level in applicationinsights.json but only DEBUG level output appears in logs
rootCause: The environment variable APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_SELF_DIAGNOSTICS_LEVEL takes precedence over the self-diagnostics level specified in the JSON configuration file. If this env var is set to DEBUG (or another level), it overrides the JSON config.
solution: Check if the environment variable APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_SELF_DIAGNOSTICS_LEVEL is set. If so, either update it to TRACE or unset it to allow the JSON configuration to take effect. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/java-standalone-config#self-diagnostics
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Application Insights Diagnostic Logs/Java Agent

symptom: Application Insights self-diagnostics log file not generated on App Service Web App (.NET Core / .NET Framework SDK ≥ 2.18)
rootCause: ApplicationInsightsDiagnostics.json placed in wrong drive or directory. App Service Web Apps may use C: or D: drive depending on configuration. SDK must be version 2.18+ for self-diagnostics. VMs/VMSS with extension not yet supported.
solution: 1) Check drive letter via KUDU default page. 2) Place ApplicationInsightsDiagnostics.json in {drive}:\home\site\wwwroot. 3) Set LogDirectory to {drive}:\home (Windows) or '.' (Linux). 4) Verify SDK version ≥ 2.18. 5) Wait ~10 seconds for log file to appear. 6) Rename .json to .bak to stop logging. For OpenTelemetry .NET exporter, same approach works.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Application Insights Diagnostic Logs/dot-Net Core

symptom: Application Insights SDK diagnostic logs not generated on App Service Web App when using SDK logging method (LogFilePath)
rootCause: LogFilePath set to C: drive path which is not accessible on App Service. The default documentation path does not work for App Service deployments because C: is read-only.
solution: Set LogFilePath to 'D:\\HOME\\SDKLOGS' in ApplicationInsights.config instead of default C: path. The SDKLOGS folder will be auto-created. Disable logging after data collection to prevent disk space issues. Collect log files from D:\HOME\SDKLOGS via KUDU Debug Console.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Application Insights Diagnostic Logs/dot-Net Core

symptom: Application Insights auto-instrumentation conflicts with existing SDK instrumentation on App Service Linux Web App; duplicate or missing telemetry
rootCause: AppAlreadyInstrumented flag is true in status JSON file (/home/LogFiles/ApplicationInsights/status/status_*.json), indicating the application already has SDK instrumentation that conflicts with codeless auto-instrumentation.
solution: 1) Navigate to KUDU Advanced Tools → BASH. 2) Check status file: cd LogFiles/ApplicationInsights/status && cat status_*.json. 3) If AppAlreadyInstrumented=true: Option A — disable auto-instrumentation in App Service blade (keep SDK); Option B — enable PreemptSdk to stop SDK custom telemetry and yield to auto-instrumentation. Note: AppContainsDiagnosticSourceAssembly and AppContainsAspNetTelemetryCorrelationAssembly are NOT applicable to .NET Core.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Status Page - App Services Web Apps and Application Insights/Net (Core) Running on Linux Web App

symptom: Application Insights not capturing HTTP 404 request telemetry for certain requests on IIS/App Service
rootCause: HTTP 404 response is set by IIS Web Core module at the webserver layer (MODULE_SET_RESPONSE_ERROR_STATUS with ModuleName='IIS Web Core'), not by the managed pipeline handler. Application Insights SDK only captures telemetry at the .NET managed layer and is not aware of IIS-level events.
solution: 1) Capture FREB logs with status codes 200-999. 2) Check MODULE_SET_RESPONSE_ERROR_STATUS entry — identify ModuleName. 3) If ModuleName='IIS Web Core' → request never reached managed handler → modify IIS config to route request through ManagedPipelineHandler. 4) If ModuleName='ManagedPipelineHandler' → App Insights should capture it; investigate SDK configuration. Behavior is same for both codeless and SDK instrumentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Tools/Capture Failed Request Event Buffering logs (FREB Logs)

symptom: FREB (Failed Request Event Buffering) logs are truncated; incomplete request trace data on App Service
rootCause: Default FREB log file size and maxLogFiles (50) are too small for complex requests. When maxLogFiles limit reached, half the files are deleted. Default file size cannot accommodate verbose trace data.
solution: Create applicationHost.xdt in d:\home\site\ with: maxLogFileSizeKB='4096' (or 8000/16000), maxLogFiles='100'. Content: <traceFailedRequestsLogging enabled='true' customActionsEnabled='true' directory='D:\home\LogFiles' maxLogFileSizeKB='4096' maxLogFiles='100' />. Restart app after changes. Verify transform at D:\local\Config\applicationhost.config. Reference: https://github.com/projectkudu/kudu/wiki/Xdt-transform-samples
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Tools/Capture Failed Request Event Buffering logs (FREB Logs)

symptom: Application Insights not capturing HTTP 404 request telemetry - customer claims 404s visible in IIS but missing from App Insights requests table
rootCause: The HTTP 404 status code was set by IIS Web Core module (MODULE_SET_RESPONSE_ERROR_STATUS with ModuleName=IIS Web Core), not by the ASP.NET ManagedPipelineHandler. Application Insights SDK only captures telemetry at the .NET managed layer and is unaware of webserver-level events.
solution: Check FREB logs for MODULE_SET_RESPONSE_ERROR_STATUS entry. If ModuleName is IIS Web Core (not ManagedPipelineHandler), the 404 is set at IIS layer before reaching managed code. Customer needs to modify IIS config to route the request through the managed handler so App Insights can capture it. Same behavior applies to both codeless and SDK instrumentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Tools/Capture Failed Request Event Buffering logs (FREB Logs)

symptom: FREB (Failed Request Event Buffering) logs are truncated - XML output file is incomplete due to large file size
rootCause: Default maxLogFileSizeKB for FREB logs is too small, causing truncation when request processing generates large trace output.
solution: Create applicationHost.xdt file at d:\home\site\applicationHost.xdt with XDT transform to increase traceFailedRequestsLogging settings: set maxLogFileSizeKB to 4096/8000/16000 and maxLogFiles to 100 (default is 50). Restart the app after applying. Verify transform took effect at D:\local\Config\applicationhost.config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Tools/Capture Failed Request Event Buffering logs (FREB Logs)

symptom: Application startup time increases after adding Application Insights .NET/.NET Core SDK due to byte-code instrumentation
rootCause: Byte-code instrumentation setup disables certain .NET Runtime startup optimizations and adds recompilation of instrumented methods
solution: 1) Discontinue use of profiling libraries to collect SQL command name. 2) Warm the application instance up before adding to the rotation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: RPS drop or CPU increase immediately after application startup under high RPS with Application Insights SDK
rootCause: Adaptive Sampling's first evaluation during steep RPS incline may be inaccurate, allowing higher sampling rate for ~2 minutes causing computational and memory overhead
solution: Configure AdaptiveSamplingTelemetryProcessor with InitialSamplingPercentage set to desired initial rate, or shorten evaluation period / SamplingPercentageDecreaseTimeout.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: Continuous CPU/RPS degradation throughout application lifetime at high RPS with Application Insights SDK
rootCause: Disabling adaptive sampling leads to noticeable overhead; even with sampling enabled, telemetry collection/initialization consumes CPU; effect worsens as CPU approaches 100%. Minimal sampling rate 0.1% still processes ~50 items at 10000 RPS.
solution: 1) 1-10% overhead is expected range. 2) Ensure adaptive sampling is enabled and rates are correct (check ItemCount in Request table). 3) Scale up/out to keep CPU below 90% without AI so with AI it stays under 100%. 4) Remove unnecessary Telemetry Initializers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: Memory consumption shows saw-like pattern with Application Insights SDK - quick rise to high memory, abrupt drop, repeat (looks like memory leak with restarts)
rootCause: Server garbage collection (GC) works differently from desktop GC - it executes cleanup less frequently after memory reaches ~35% of available memory threshold. This is normal server GC behavior on multi-processor systems.
solution: 1) No action needed - this is how server GC was designed to operate. 2) Optionally replace server GC with desktop GC: set <gcServer enabled="false"/> in <runtime> configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: Memory consumption linearly increases and never goes down with Application Insights SDK (true memory leak pattern)
rootCause: Manual telemetry submission code creates multiple long-lifetime AI objects. Common pattern: instantiating TelemetryConfiguration or TelemetryChannel on every request or every Azure Function invocation, creating orphaned threads.
solution: Review usage of telemetry classes from Application Insights libraries. Find patterns that instantiate many long-lifetime objects like TelemetryConfiguration. Refactor to use a few shared long-lifetime objects.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: TCP connection exhaustion or timeouts related to Application Insights SDK
rootCause: Multiple TelemetryConfiguration objects each create their own channel, leading to connection competition. Older SDK versions (<2.7.1) had HTTP Client connection leak. AI endpoint/Live Metrics timeouts can cause connection buildup.
solution: 1) Update to Application Insights SDK v2.7.1+ (earlier versions had HTTP Client connection leak). 2) Review and refactor code to reduce TelemetryConfiguration/TelemetryChannel instances - ideally single instance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: Response time increase and lock contention in System.Diagnostics.Trace logs after updating Application Insights Trace Listener SDK to 2.3+
rootCause: autoflush="true" setting in web.config causes Flush() to be called after each log message. SDK 2.3+ implements Flush() which creates lock contention in telemetry items buffer access.
solution: 1) Do not use autoflush="true" in production. 2) Use Application Insights Trace Listener 2.2 which does not implement Flush(). Tip: Filter PerfView traces on 'TelemetlyClientFlush' to confirm autoflush issue by checking flush call frequency.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: File system access contention and race conditions between multiple Application Insights channels
rootCause: Multiple identical ServerTelemetryChannel instances are configured (intentionally or by mistake), competing for the same local folder for retries and leveraging the same internal components, creating race conditions.
solution: Review usage of telemetry classes from Application Insights libraries. Find patterns that instantiate multiple TelemetryConfiguration/TelemetryChannel objects. Refactor to use preferably only one instance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: Attempts to create or update tags on Application Insights resources fail with 500 InternalServerError containing AADSTS7000112: Application Insights Configuration Service (AppId 6a0a243c-0886-468a-a4c2-eff52c7445da) is disabled
rootCause: The Application Insights Configuration Service first-party app has been disabled in Microsoft Entra ID, blocking authentication flows required for control plane tag operations on AI resources
solution: Re-enable the Application Insights Configuration Service enterprise application in Microsoft Entra ID. Guidance: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/disable-user-sign-in-portal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/106818

symptom: Diagnostic settings are not deleted when the associated Azure resource is deleted or migrated, resulting in orphaned diagnostic settings that are no longer accessible but still exist
rootCause: Diagnostic settings are extension proxy resources of the Azure resource they are associated with. The delete action on the parent resource does not cascade to related diagnostic settings, leaving them orphaned and inaccessible
solution: Option 1: Recreate the Azure resource under the same subscription, resource group, and resource name to make the diagnostic settings accessible again, then delete them before re-deleting the resource. Option 2: Submit a PG escalation request via https://aka.ms/azmonpgescalation to remove the orphaned settings. For automated processes, include a step to delete diagnostic settings BEFORE deleting the associated Azure resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/14416

symptom: Need to find Application Insights component details (name, subscription, resource group) when only an instrumentation key (iKey) or component name is available
solution:
Use Kusto query against the AI backend cluster:

```kql
cluster('aibeftprd.kusto.windows.net').database('Cdsproddatabase').ActiveComponents()
| where iKey == "<ikey guid here>"
```

Or search by component name:
```kql
cluster('aibeftprd.kusto.windows.net').database('Cdsproddatabase').ActiveComponents()
| where name =~ "<component name here>"
```
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Kusto/Query for Application Insights Component

symptom: Tagging operations (create/update) on Application Insights resources fail with 500 InternalServerError: AADSTS7000112 Application Insights Configuration Service (6a0a243c-0886-468a-a4c2-eff52c7445da) is disabled
rootCause: The Application Insights Configuration Service first-party app (App ID: 6a0a243c-0886-468a-a4c2-eff52c7445da) has been disabled in Microsoft Entra ID, blocking authentication flows required for tag control plane operations on Application Insights resources.
solution: Re-enable the Application Insights Configuration Service in Microsoft Entra ID. Guidance: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/disable-user-sign-in-portal?pivots=portal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/106818

symptom: Diagnostic settings become orphaned (inaccessible but still existing) after the associated Azure resource is deleted or migrated across Resource Groups/Subscriptions
rootCause: Diagnostic settings are extension proxy resources of the Azure resource. The delete action against the Azure resource does not cascade to the related diagnostic settings, leaving them orphaned. This is by-design behavior.
solution: Option 1: Recreate the Azure resource under the same subscription, resource group, and resource name to make the orphaned diagnostic settings accessible again, then delete them before re-deleting the resource. Option 2: Submit a PG escalation request via https://aka.ms/azmonpgescalation to have orphaned diagnostic settings removed. For automated processes, include a step to delete diagnostic settings BEFORE deleting the associated Azure resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/14416

symptom: App Services Auto Instrumentation (.NET) fails with 'Could not load file or assembly System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe.dll' after ANT build version update (98.0.7.575 → 98.0.10.735). App Insights extension 2.8.44 causes apps to fail to initialize or startup.
rootCause: New App Insights extension 2.8.44 pulls a newer version of System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe.dll (4.0.4.1), which conflicts with dormant bindingRedirect entries in the customer's web.config (pointing to version 4.0.6.0). If the dll is not present in the app's \bin folder, the redirect causes a FileNotFoundException.
solution: Option 1: If System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe.dll is NOT in the \bin folder, remove the <bindingRedirect> for that assembly from web.config, or change it to point to version 4.0.4.1. Option 2: If the dll IS in \bin folder, temporarily set ApplicationInsightsAgent_EXTENSION_VERSION=2.8.37 in App Service settings to revert to the older extension. Use AppLens 'Antares Version' and 'Application Insights Auto Instrumentation' detectors to check versions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/34102

symptom: The 'client_OS' field in 'browserTimings' and 'pageViews' tables in Application Insights reports incorrect or inaccurate OS data. For example, Windows 11 clients are reported as 'Windows 10'.
rootCause: Since Windows 11, browsers have frozen the User-Agent header value, making it no longer a reliable source for populating client_OS in Application Insights telemetry.
solution: Currently no fix available. Microsoft plans to investigate viable workarounds using User-Agent Client Hints. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/web-platform/how-to-detect-win11
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/37425

symptom: Java application with Application Insights Java Agent experiences high CPU usage caused by Reactor and Reactor Netty instrumentations
rootCause: The Reactor and Reactor Netty instrumentations applied by the Java Agent can significantly increase CPU usage. Can verify by enabling self-diagnostics at DEBUG level and checking for 'Applying instrumentation: reactor' and 'Applying instrumentation: reactor-netty' in logs.
solution: Disable Reactor/Reactor Netty instrumentations by setting environment variables OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_REACTOR_ENABLED=false and OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_REACTOR_NETTY_ENABLED=false, or use JVM system properties -Dotel.instrumentation.reactor.enabled=false and -Dotel.instrumentation.reactor.netty.enabled=false
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Troubleshooting performance impact involving Java agent

symptom: Starting, stopping, or creating an Availability Test in Application Insights can take several minutes to take effect. Stopping a test may still result in one or more additional test runs before the status change is processed.
rootCause: By Design. There is a queueing mechanism to notify the backend instances running the test. Multiple factors affect how fast this queue is processed, causing fluctuating delays.
solution: No workaround. This is by-design behavior. Set customer expectation that status changes may take several minutes to propagate to all test regions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/40384

symptom: No SQL Dependency telemetry in Application Insights for .NET Framework 4.x application using Microsoft.Data.SqlClient with Profiler enabled. Other telemetry types (Requests, Traces, some Dependencies) are present but SQL calls are missing.
rootCause: Application Insights SDK only byte-code instruments System.Data.SqlClient classes via Profiler. When Profiler is enabled, Microsoft.Data.SqlClient calls are not hooked, so SQL Dependency data is not collected. GitHub issue: microsoft/ApplicationInsights-dotnet#2736.
solution: Option 1: Disable the Profiler — SDK falls back to EventSource which supports Microsoft.Data.SqlClient. For App Service: stop ApplicationInsightsProfiler3 webjob. For Azure VMs: disable InstrumentationEngine via Application Insights Agent. Option 2: Switch application code from Microsoft.Data.SqlClient to System.Data.SqlClient to keep Profiler and still capture SQL Dependency telemetry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/40472

symptom: Application Insights auto-instrumentation (App Services attach / VM extension / on-premises agent) stops reporting telemetry after code deployment or when first enabled. Conflicting DLLs detected in wwwroot/bin: Microsoft.ApplicationInsights, System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource, or Microsoft.AspNet.TelemetryCorrelation.
rootCause: Auto-instrumentation variants depend on specific versions of certain NuGet packages. If the application introduces a different version of these DLLs, auto-instrumentation detects the conflict and backs off, leaving the app uninstrumented.
solution: Remove the conflicting package to let auto-instrumentation attach. If removal is not feasible (e.g. System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource is a transitive dependency), switch to manual SDK instrumentation. For .NET Core on App Services, enable 'Interop with Application Insights SDK' to force auto-instrumentation despite conflicting DLLs. Diagnostics: check Kudu /ApplicationInsights endpoint, inspect wwwroot/bin for AI DLLs, or use the AppLens Auto Instrumentation detector.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65322

symptom: Application Insights auto-instrumentation intermittently stops collecting telemetry after app restart (no code deployment). Telemetry resumes after another restart or re-enabling AI. Memory dump shows only partial AI modules loaded (missing Microsoft_AI_Web, Microsoft_AI_DependencyCollector, etc.).
rootCause: Transient DLL loading timing issue: DLLs are dynamically loaded into .NET AppDomain on demand. If a dependency like Azure.Identity.dll loads System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource.dll into the AppDomain before auto-instrumentation checks, the backoff condition triggers. On subsequent restarts, if the loading order differs, auto-instrumentation succeeds.
solution: Restart the application or disable/re-enable Application Insights to re-trigger auto-instrumentation. For persistent issues, switch to manual SDK. Verify via memory dump (lm command to check loaded modules) or App Service auto-instrumentation logs (WebAppsAppInsightsExtension table in Kusto).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65322

symptom: Application Insights Profiler not collecting traces on Azure VM/VMSS after enabling App Insights VM Extension. Bootstrap logs show error: Standard Collector uninstallation failed with exit code 1605. Event Log shows 'Profiler bootstrap process prematurely ended with exit code 1'.
rootCause: The Profiler installer detects an existing Standard Collector Service (VSStandardCollectorService150) installed by Visual Studio or Visual Studio Remote Debugging Tools on the VM. It attempts to uninstall the conflicting service but fails with exit code 1605 ('product not currently installed' mismatch), blocking Profiler installation.
solution: Uninstall Visual Studio or Visual Studio Remote Debugging Tools from the VM. Use a clean VM without Visual Studio installed. Profiler will then install successfully and attach to .NET Framework or .NET Core apps in IIS. The Profiler team is working on a fix for a future VM extension version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65488

symptom: FREB (Failed Request Event Buffering) logs are truncated on App Service
rootCause: Default FREB log file size and maxLogFiles (50) too small. When maxLogFiles reached, half are deleted.
solution: Create applicationHost.xdt in d:\home\site\ with maxLogFileSizeKB=4096+ and maxLogFiles=100. Restart app. Verify at D:\local\Config\applicationhost.config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Tools/Capture Failed Request Event Buffering logs (FREB Logs)

symptom: Node.js apps using Axios library produce extra failed dependency telemetry records during HTTP 301 redirects. An additional dependency record with failed status (no resultCode) appears alongside the successful 301 and final 200 response in Application Insights.
rootCause: By design — internal processing within the Axios module triggers extra HTTP events that the Application Insights Node.js SDK hooks into. The SDK team has no control over Axios internals.
solution: Three options: (1) Ignore the extra failed dependency records; (2) Replace Axios with the standard Node.js http module for that request; (3) Add a telemetry processor to drop failed dependency items: filter baseType==='RemoveDependencyData' where success===false and resultCode is empty, return false to drop.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/52155

symptom: JavaScript SDK telemetry correlation headers (W3C/HTTP) are not sent on outbound XmlHttpRequests from web pages despite enabling distributedTracingMode configuration setting.
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: (1) SDK version too old (< 2.8.12 doesn't support W3C+HTTP headers); (2) IFrames making requests without their own SDK initialization; (3) Web Workers not initializing SDK separately; (4) XMLHttpRequest polyfill overriding prototype functions before or after SDK hooks, bypassing the SDK's open/send instrumentation.
solution: Troubleshoot checklist: (1) Capture Fiddler/F12 HAR trace of outbound requests; (2) Verify SDK config follows latest docs; (3) Confirm SDK version ≥ 2.8.12 with distributedTracingMode=1; (4) Ensure IFrame pages also load JS SDK; (5) Initialize SDK in each Web Worker; (6) Check for XMLHttpRequest polyfill interference — ensure polyfill loads after AI SDK init or uses prototype references instead of cached functions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/52157

symptom: Customer receives Azure Service Health notification about deprecation of instrumentation key-based global ingestion for Application Insights and needs to identify which AI components and applications are using global ingestion endpoints
rootCause: Deprecation of instrumentation key-based global ingestion endpoints in Application Insights (EOL March 31, 2025). Customers using ikey-based global ingestion need to migrate to connection string-based regional ingestion.
solution: 1) To identify affected AI components per subscription: Use SAW device with Jarvis Actions → 'Read Telemetry Operations' → 'Check Global Ingestion' action (change endpoint from AIMON to AIPROD, paste subscription ID). Returns UseGlobal=True/False/Unknown for each component. 2) To identify instrumented applications: Run KQL query 'union AppRequests, AppDependencies, AppPageViews... | summarize count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d), _ResourceId, AppRoleName, AppRoleInstance, SDKVersion, Type, Url'. 3) Migrate to connection strings: https://aka.ms/MigrateToConnectionString
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/27572

symptom: Custom metrics visible in customMetrics/AppMetrics log table but missing from Metrics blade charts, or metric alerts incorrectly firing/not firing despite data being ingested into Application Insights
rootCause: MDM custom metric account exceeded 50,000 active time series limit (per subscription per region, over any 12-hour span). When 'Enable alerting on custom metric dimensions' is enabled, each unique combination of metric name + dimension key + dimension value creates a separate time series, drastically expanding the count. High-cardinality dimensions (e.g., CPU percentages, session IDs) can quickly exhaust the limit.
solution: 1) Check 'Custom Metrics Opt In' setting via AIMC tool (search by ikey/name → Applications tab). 2) If set to 'WithDimension' and not needed, disable it to reduce time series count. 3) Use KQL query on customMetrics table to estimate active time series: extend json=parse_json(customDimensions), bag_unpack, distinct *, summarize TotalActiveSeries=count(). 4) Identify worst offender metrics by name with count. 5) Reduce high-cardinality dimensions. 6) Verify throttling status via Jarvis QOS dashboard: CUSTOMMETRIC_{subId}_{region}/GenevaQos.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/27792

symptom: When migrating Application Insights to workspace-based using Terraform with AzureRM version older than 3.49, the existing classic resource is deleted and recreated with the same name, resulting in loss of all historical data
rootCause: The workspace_id parameter in older AzureRM versions (<3.49) had a ForceNew condition that destroyed and recreated the Application Insights resource instead of updating it in-place
solution: Upgrade AzureRM provider to version 3.49 or greater, which fixes the workspace_id parameter to allow in-place updates without resource recreation (fix: hashicorp/terraform-provider-azurerm#21029)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68672

symptom: After renaming or moving an Application Insights resource, ARM operations to update Availability web test metric-based alerts fail because the alert still references the old component resource URI
rootCause: Rename/move operations change the Application Insights resource URI, but the built-in metric-based alerts for Availability Tests retain a stale reference to the old component URI, causing ARM validation failures
solution: Manually update the metric-based alert ARM template to reference the new component resource URI (update the components_externalid parameter from the old name to the new name)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/69462

symptom: Application Insights SDK diagnostic logs not generated on App Service (LogFilePath on C: inaccessible)
rootCause: LogFilePath set to C: drive path which is read-only on App Service.
solution: Set LogFilePath to D:\HOME\SDKLOGS. Folder auto-created. Disable logging after collection.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Application Insights Diagnostic Logs/dot-Net Core

symptom: App Insights auto-instrumentation conflicts with SDK on App Service Linux; duplicate/missing telemetry
rootCause: AppAlreadyInstrumented=true in /home/LogFiles/ApplicationInsights/status/status_*.json
solution: KUDU BASH > cat status_*.json. If AppAlreadyInstrumented=true: disable auto-instrumentation or enable PreemptSdk.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Status Page - App Services Web Apps and Application Insights/Net (Core) Running on Linux Web App

symptom: App Insights not capturing HTTP 404 telemetry on IIS/App Service
rootCause: 404 set by IIS Web Core module at webserver layer, not managed pipeline. AI SDK only captures .NET managed layer.
solution: Capture FREB logs (statusCodes 200-999). Check MODULE_SET_RESPONSE_ERROR_STATUS ModuleName. IIS Web Core -> route through ManagedPipelineHandler.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Tools/Capture Failed Request Event Buffering logs (FREB Logs)

symptom: FREB logs truncated on App Service
rootCause: Default maxLogFiles=50 and file size too small. Half deleted when limit reached.
solution: applicationHost.xdt in d:\home\site\ with maxLogFileSizeKB=4096+ and maxLogFiles=100. Restart app. Verify at D:\local\Config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Tools/Capture Failed Request Event Buffering logs (FREB Logs)

symptom: Application Insights Event Counter metrics recorded as Trace telemetry with 'n/a' message and Critical severity, instead of expected customMetric. Each EventCounter metric appears as a Trace item with empty message.
rootCause: Both EventSourceTelemetryModule and EventCounterCollectionModule are enabled simultaneously. EventSource intercepts EventCounter events and writes them as Trace telemetry instead of allowing EventCounterCollectionModule to process them as customMetrics.
solution: Disable conflicting events in EventSourceTelemetryModule by adding DisabledSources for the event sources collected by EventCounterCollectionModule. Example: services.ConfigureTelemetryModule<EventSourceTelemetryModule>((module, o) => { module.DisabledSources.Add(new DisableEventSourceRequest() { Name = "Microsoft-AspNetCore-Server-Kestrel" }); }); This ensures EventCounter processes the events as metrics instead of EventSource capturing them as traces.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/61790

symptom: Application Insights Availability web tests suddenly fail in a specific region with TLS/SSL/Secure Channel errors and ~21 second duration. Errors include 'Could not establish trust relationship for the SSL/TLS secure channel' or 'Could not create SSL/TLS secure channel'.
rootCause: FedRAMP-mandated TLS 1.3 migration on Availability Test service infrastructure. Servers with TLS 1.3 enabled but improperly configured (not per RFC 8446) fail the TLS handshake. Regions progressively updated from Nov 2023 through 2024 (NCUS, SCUS, CUS, EUS, WUS2, FairFax regions).
solution: Fix TLS 1.3 configuration on the target web server per RFC 8446. Temporary mitigations: (1) Remove TLS 1.3 from server and use only TLS 1.2; (2) Change availability test to use a different, not-yet-migrated region. Diagnose by checking the TlsTransport step in test results via Executions/Results tabs. Note: servers without TLS 1.3 will negotiate down to TLS 1.2 automatically and are NOT affected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/62378

symptom: Application Insights request telemetry geo-location fields (client_City, client_StateOrProvince, client_CountryOrRegion) return blank for IPv6 client IPs
rootCause: Application Insights does not support geo-location lookup using IPv6 addresses (by design limitation)
solution: Implement a custom TelemetryInitializer to set Context.Location.* properties (City, StateOrProvince, CountryOrRegion) from the client side directly
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/84537

symptom: Permission error when creating or enabling Diagnostic Setting on Classic Application Insights component resource
rootCause: Caller lacks Contributor permissions on the Application Insights resource scope required for Diagnostic Settings creation
solution: Grant the user Contributor role on the Application Insights resource, then retry creating the Diagnostic Setting
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/87381

symptom: Heartbeat telemetry from VM is missing extra dimensions like azInst_vmId, azInst_name, azInst_location, azInst_resourceGroupName etc. Customer wants to filter heartbeat data by specific VM but these fields are absent.
rootCause: The application is not loading the AzureInstanceMetadataTelemetryModule. By default, the Application Insights SDK produces heartbeat telemetry with only basic fields (processSessionId, runtimeFramework, baseSdkTargetFramework, osType). The azInst_* fields require the AzureInstanceMetadataTelemetryModule to be explicitly enabled.
solution: Enable AzureInstanceMetadataTelemetryModule: (1) .NET Framework (ASP.NET): If using Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Web NuGet, the module is auto-added to ApplicationInsights.config. If programmatically configuring TelemetryConfiguration, explicitly add the module. Otherwise add to ApplicationInsights.config: <Add Type="Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.WindowsServer.AzureInstanceMetadataTelemetryModule, Microsoft.AI.WindowsServer">. (2) .NET Core: See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/asp-net-core?tabs=netcorenew#configure-or-remove-default-telemetrymodules
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/73062

symptom: Python Flask application in Visual Studio fails with 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named flask' when the Flask framework is not installed in the active Python environment
rootCause: Flask framework not installed in the Visual Studio Python environment. When the target framework is missing, importing flask in app.py raises ModuleNotFoundError.
solution: In Visual Studio IDE: Tools > Python > Python Environments > Open in PowerShell, then run 'pip install flask'. After successful installation, Flask apps can be run and tested locally.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Pre-reqs/Install Python

symptom: Running Python Flask app locally in Visual Studio fails with 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named flask'
rootCause: Flask framework not installed in the Visual Studio Python environment
solution: In Visual Studio IDE: Tools > Python > Python Environments > Open in PowerShell > run 'pip install flask'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Pre-reqs/Install Python

symptom: Security scanners flag Application Insights ai_user and ai_session cookies as vulnerable because they are not marked as HttpOnly
rootCause: The ai_user and ai_session cookies must be readable by the Application Insights JavaScript SDK in the browser. They cannot be set as HttpOnly because the SDK requires client-side read access to function.
solution: This is by design. Unsupported workaround documented at https://github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInsights-JS/issues/626 — customers can implement custom cookie handling, but it is not officially supported.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Browser telemetry

symptom: HTTP 500 or 500.53 errors occur after enabling Application Insights client-side monitoring via APPINSIGHTS_JAVASCRIPT_ENABLED app setting in Azure App Service
rootCause: Conflict between Application Insights automatic JavaScript snippet injection and IIS URL compression (dynamic or static). The injection modifies the response stream which is incompatible with certain compression configurations.
solution: Disable URL compression or use manual JavaScript SDK injection instead of auto-injection. See: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/azure-web-apps-net#appinsights_javascript_enabled-and-urlcompression-is-not-supported
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Browser telemetry

symptom: Page visit duration (time spent on page) is not tracked in Application Insights pageViews/AppPageViews
rootCause: The autoTrackPageVisitTime configuration option is disabled by default in the Application Insights JavaScript SDK.
solution: Enable autoTrackPageVisitTime in the JavaScript SDK configuration. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/javascript-sdk-configuration?tabs=javascriptwebsdkloaderscript#sdk-configuration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Browser telemetry

symptom: OMS Linux agent stops collecting custom logs after file truncation or circular log rotation. No new log entries appear in the custom log table despite new data being written to the file.
rootCause: OMS agent tracks byte position in .pos file. When file is truncated/cleared to size < previously read position, agent won't collect until file grows past that position. Circular rotation (overwrite in-place) is not supported.
solution: Use standard log rotation (rename old file + create new file) instead of circular/truncate rotation. The new file gets a different inode, and the agent treats it as a new file. Check log rotation config in /etc/logrotate.d/.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki?pagePath=/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent

symptom: OMS Linux custom log not collected. omsagent.log shows 'WARN <PATH> does not exist. Cannot tail the file.'
rootCause: The file path specified in the custom log rule does not exist on the system. Path may be incorrect or file was removed/renamed.
solution: Verify the file path in the custom log configuration matches the actual file location on the Linux system. Check /etc/opt/microsoft/omsagent/<workspace-id>/conf/omsagent.d/customlog.conf for correct paths.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki?pagePath=/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent

symptom: OMS Linux custom log not collected. omsagent.log shows 'WARN <PATH> is excluded since it's unreadable or doesn't have proper permissions.'
rootCause: The omsagent process does not have read permissions on the target log file.
solution: Fix file permissions so omsagent can read the file. Check ownership and permissions with ls -la. The omsagent user (typically 'omsagent') needs read access to the log file.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent
