Note: this is not reflected in ASC Ingestion tab because the TLS mismatch occurs before data reaches Breeze (the ingestion endpoint).
solution: 1) For .NET 4.X apps: add the following line early in application startup (e.g., global.asax): System.Net.ServicePointManager.SecurityProtocol = SecurityProtocolType.Tls12; 2) Use Windows registry settings to enforce TLS 1.2 client-wide — see: https://docs.microsoft.com/mem/configmgr/core/plan-design/security/enable-tls-1-2-client 3) For .NET-specific registry settings: https://docs.microsoft.com/dotnet/framework/network-programming/tls — collect a network capture (NetMon/Wireshark/netsh) to confirm the TLS version being used by the app process before implementing the fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Validate Network Connectivity/Validate TLS connectivity in Application Insights

symptom: App Service Profiler WebJob log shows 'Reached maximum allowed output lines for this run, to see all of the job's logs you can enable website application diagnostics' — profiler log entries stop appearing in KUDU WebJobs dashboard
rootCause: The App Service WebJob output log has a maximum line limit; once reached, subsequent profiler log entries are not written to the KUDU output window
solution: Enable application diagnostics on the Web App via the portal, or restart the application to reset the WebJob output log counter
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Profiler & Snapshot Debugger/Find and analyze Profiler Logs

symptom: DiagnosticsPlugin log on Azure VM or Cloud Service shows 'Skipping Profiler bootstrap based on deployment configuration' — Application Insights Profiler not starting
rootCause: The Azure Diagnostics profiler sink is not configured in the deployment configuration, so the profiler bootstrap step is skipped
solution: Configure the profiler sink using Azure Resource Explorer: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/profiler-vm#set-profiler-sink-using-azure-resource-explorer; after configuring the sink, '[Profiler]' entries should appear in DiagnosticsPlugin log
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Profiler & Snapshot Debugger/Find and analyze Profiler Logs

symptom: No '[Profiler]' entries found in DiagnosticsPlugin log for Azure VM or Cloud Service — Application Insights Profiler never configured on the machine
rootCause: Profiler has not been installed or configured on the machine; this typically occurs on VM or on-premises machines, not App Service hosted apps
solution: Follow VM onboarding: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/profiler-vm or Cloud Service onboarding: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/profiler-cloudservice; if issue recurs after onboarding, investigate whether deployment processes are overwriting profiler configuration settings
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Profiler & Snapshot Debugger/Find and analyze Profiler Logs

symptom: Clicking the 'collect snapshots' button in Application Insights Failures experience shows 'You don't have access' message even when the Snapshot Debugger RBAC role is assigned to an AAD group
rootCause: Bug: Application Insights Snapshot Debugger RBAC role does not work when assigned to AAD groups; only works when granted directly to individual users, contrary to documentation
solution: Explicitly grant the 'Application Insights Snapshot Debugger' RBAC role directly to individual users rather than groups. Product fix expected August 2025. ICM: 613574130
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/106335

symptom: Application Insights portal experiences stop working for AMPLS-associated resources: unable to query Logs, Failures/Performance blades show empty sections or 'No data available'. Issue is browser-specific (Chrome/Edge).
rootCause: Chrome/Edge 'Local Network Access Checks' feature (enabled by default) blocks private/local network requests in newer browser versions. This breaks AI portal experiences when using AMPLS private endpoints.
solution: Disable 'Local Network Access Checks' in browser flags: edge://flags/#local-network-access-check or chrome://flags/#local-network-access-check. Update SAP to Azure/Portal/Resource Blade Does Not Load. If workaround resolves, close with root cause 'Azure Portal/Framework/Core framework issue'. Otherwise redirect to Azure portal team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/156315

symptom: After migrating from classic Application Insights .NET SDK to Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Distro, custom tracestate header values are lost in distributed traces; downstream services expecting legacy correlation fields do not receive them
rootCause: Classic SDK uses TelemetryInitializers (coupled to TelemetryClient) for tracestate enrichment; OpenTelemetry SDK is vendor-neutral and requires SpanProcessors for custom enrichment. Hierarchical operation IDs and some legacy correlation features are not supported in OTel.
solution: Implement a custom LogRecord SpanProcessor to add activity.TraceStateString as a tag (data.Attributes). Alternatively use OpenTelemetry Propagators to customize context propagation. Enrich methods from instrumentation libraries are also supported. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/opentelemetry-add-modify
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/158085

symptom: Duplicate telemetry records appear in workspace-backed Application Insights (AI 2.1) tables; duplicate rows share identical values across all columns including timestamp, operation_Id, and id
rootCause: Diagnostic Settings configured to send Application Insights component logs to any Log Analytics workspace causes duplication. Draft API queries all linked LA workspaces, so telemetry sent via Diagnostic Settings to a LA workspace is returned twice (or more).
solution: Remove LA workspace from Diagnostic Settings (can still use Event Hub or Storage Account). Alternative: Use DCR Transformation Rules to rename tables in the second LA workspace. Or restrict user RBAC access to the target LA workspace (requires 'Require workspace permissions' access mode). Or use dedup/distinct in queries.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/22050

symptom: Application Insights Requests table client_Ip field shows the proxy/load balancer IP address instead of the actual end-user client IP address
rootCause: X-Forwarded-For header is present and Application Insights by design takes the rightmost (last) IP in the X-Forwarded-For list, which is the last proxy before the ingestion endpoint, not the original client IP
solution: Use TelemetryInitializer to intercept telemetry payload, parse the first (leftmost) IP from X-Forwarded-For header as the true client IP, perform custom geolocation lookup, and attach as a custom property. No built-in fix available; Microsoft monitoring demand for feature improvement.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/26201

symptom: After renaming or moving (between resource groups or subscriptions) a workspace-based Application Insights resource, only telemetry ingested after the ARM operation is visible; historical data appears missing in log-based experiences. MDM-based metrics in Overview blade are not affected.
rootCause: Workspace-based AI query experience uses the _ResourceId field to filter telemetry from the LA workspace. Rename/move operations change the resource ID, so all historical data with the old _ResourceId is no longer returned by AI query experience.
solution: No direct fix. Options: (1) Roll back the rename/move operation to restore old resource ID. (2) Query historical data from LA Logs blade using: AppRequests | where _ResourceId == '/old path' OR _ResourceId == '/new path'. (3) Create Dashboards/Workbooks querying LA workspace directly with both resource IDs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/26413

symptom: No telemetry data returned from workspace-based Application Insights (AI 2.1) in Azure Portal charts or Logs blade despite telemetry being actively sent. Draft API returns HTTP 200 with zero records.
rootCause: User has RBAC access to the Application Insights resource but lacks permissions on the underlying Log Analytics workspace. Draft API uses the user's AAD auth token to query LA and returns a silent HTTP 200 with no records when LA RBAC check fails.
solution: Assign the user 'Log Analytics Reader' RBAC role on the backend LA workspace. For strict access control, manage workspace RBAC and table-level permissions as needed. Reference: Manage access to log data and workspaces in Azure Monitor.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/26775

symptom: REST API calls to Application Insights Draft API (api.applicationinsights.io or api.applicationinsights.azure.com) using API Key return HTTP 200 with no telemetry records for a workspace-based AI component
rootCause: API Key (SAS-Key) requests to Draft API do not include an Azure AD auth token. If the backend Log Analytics workspace Access Control Mode is set to 'Require Workspace Permissions', all API Key requests silently return 200 with no data.
solution: Set the Log Analytics workspace Access Control Mode to 'Use resource or workspace permissions' on the LA Properties blade. This is required to allow API Key-based calls to Draft API. If AAD auth is feasible, use AAD tokens instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/26775

symptom: Availability Test starts failing consistently in one region with ~21000ms TCP-level connection timeout: 'DNS Status: Succeeded, Connection Status: Failed - A connection attempt failed because the connected party did not properly respond'. Issue intermittently self-resolves but recurs. Other regions pass and the website is confirmed up.
rootCause: Cause unclear; indications suggest an Azure-side issue with the availability test agent infrastructure in that specific region. Investigation was ongoing as of June 2023.
solution: No permanent resolution available. Mitigation: Remove the failing region from the test and add a different region. Caveat: the issue may eventually occur on the new region too. Recommend testing from multiple regions to reduce single-region failure impact.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/27462

symptom: Application Insights Performance/Availability/Failures blades show inflated request counts compared to actual traffic. Discrepancy observed between initial chart load (accurate) and values after user interacts with chart or clicks refresh (inflated). Occurs when extreme sampling (<=10%) is active.
rootCause: Initial chart load uses pre-aggregated Application Insights Standard Metrics (accurate). After user interaction, blades switch to Logs-based queries using sum(itemCount). During extreme sampling, itemCount values on retained records are inflated to represent sampled-out records, making sum(itemCount) less accurate than pre-aggregated metrics.
solution: Advise customer to use 'Application Insights Standard Metrics' namespace on the Metrics blade for accurate counts. Collect browser HAR trace to confirm whether chart is using Logs query (api.applicationinsights.azure.com) or Metrics query (management.azure.com/.../metrics). Check sampling rate using Sampling identification guide.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/27486

symptom: Application Insights shows request count as an exact multiple (2x, 3x, 4x, etc.) of expected count on Performance/Failures blades. Issue occurs in distributed architectures where a single user operation spans multiple services.
rootCause: Sampling is operation-based: all telemetry items sharing the same operationId are kept or dropped together. When a single user operation spans multiple services (root request → dependency → backend request), ALL resulting request records share the same operationId. Logs-based sum(itemCount) counts each request record's itemCount independently, causing the apparent request count to multiply by the number of services in the distributed operation.
solution: Check for distributed operations: query a single operationId for all telemetry records and confirm multiple Request telemetry records exist for one logical request. Use Application Insights Standard Metrics namespace on Metrics blade for accurate counts. Collect HAR trace to confirm logs-vs-metrics query type.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/27486

symptom: Application Insights Availability Ping or Standard Tests return failures (e.g. HTTP 404) even though the website is up and accessible from browsers/Postman without custom headers.
rootCause: Some web servers (notably NGINX) reject requests whose User-Agent is 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; MSIE 9.0; Windows NT 6.1; Trident/5.0; AppInsights)', treating it as an insecure/legacy browser (Internet Explorer 9). The User-Agent and HOST headers are hard-coded for Availability test clients and cannot be changed by customers.
solution: Customers must configure their web server to allow requests with the Availability test User-Agent value. To reproduce: send a Postman request including 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; MSIE 9.0; Windows NT 6.1; Trident/5.0; AppInsights)' and verify the 404 response. PG is investigating support for customizable User-Agent but it is not yet available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/27863

symptom: Net Core Web App logs startup exceptions: 'System.InvalidOperationException: Startup assembly Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.StartupBootstrapper failed to execute' or 'DiagnosticServices.HostingStartup failed to execute'. App function and AI data collection are not impacted despite the errors.
rootCause: Codeless auto-instrumentation attach is enabled for unsupported .NET Core scenarios: self-contained deployments, apps targeting full .NET Framework, or apps on unsupported framework versions (other than 3.1, 5.0, and 6.0+ for self-contained).
solution: 1. Remove or unset 'ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES' environment variable. 2. In App Services > Configuration, remove all Application Insights related app settings EXCEPT APPINSIGHTS_INSTRUMENTATIONKEY and APPINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING. Note: these exceptions only stop the startup error; the SDK will still not fully instrument unsupported scenarios.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/28165

symptom: Application Insights component (using Log Analytics / APM 2.1 workspace-based ingestion mode) shows missing or no telemetry data in tables. Other AI components sending to the same Log Analytics workspace show data normally.
rootCause: A pipeline bug causes data loss when the Application Insights resource ID string contains non-ASCII/Unicode characters. The resource ID (including resource group name, component name, etc.) is used in HTTP headers during the ingestion process, but HTTP headers only support ASCII characters.
solution: Mitigation: Create and use an Application Insights resource whose name, resource group, and all other components of the resource ID contain only ASCII characters. Use https://www.soscisurvey.de/tools/view-chars.php to inspect the resource ID for invisible Unicode characters. Permanent fix was under investigation at time of writing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/28300

symptom: Intermittent HTTP 4xx or 5xx errors returned when performing actions against Azure services via Azure portal, CLI, PowerShell, REST API, or SDK. Retrying the same action succeeds.
rootCause: Transient faults inherent to distributed client-server systems. Common causes: 409 Conflict (resource in non-modifiable state), 429 TooManyRequests (Azure limit reached), 500 InternalServerError (unknown backend error), 503 ServiceUnavailable (service temporarily unable to respond), 504 GatewayTimeout (ARM-proxied service did not respond within ~60s).
solution: Retry the operation. Implement transient fault handling in application code using Microsoft's retry guidance. Many Azure SDK implementations include built-in retry logic. If errors persist for extended durations (minutes) or recur frequently, raise a CRI to the relevant PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/28573

symptom: Activity Logs show entries initiated by 'Windows Azure Application Insights' or 'Azure Monitor System' (e.g. ListAccountSas operations on Storage Accounts), causing customer concern about unexpected access to their resources.
rootCause: By design. 'Windows Azure Application Insights' / 'Azure Monitor System' is a 1st-party AAD application used by Azure Monitor services (Metrics, Autoscale, Diagnostic Settings / Monitor Essentials family). Tenants created before March 2022 will continue to see the old 'Windows Azure Application Insights' name; tenants after March 2022 see 'Azure Monitor System'. Calls are triggered by Diagnostic Settings configured to write to Storage Accounts.
solution: Educate customer: calls are expected and required by Azure services. To identify the source resource: in ASC > Tenant Explorer search the caller ID as 'Serviceprincipal ObjectId'. Query Kusto: cluster('Azureinsights').database('Insights').RegistrationTelemetry | where customerStorageAccountId == '<id>' | summarize ... by name, resourceId, categories, customerStorageAccountId. To stop calls, disable Diagnostic Settings writing to the Storage Account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/29307

symptom: Azure App Service Web App reports incorrect cloud_RoleName in Application Insights telemetry after a deployment slot swap. The cloud_RoleName reflects the pre-swap slot identity rather than the post-swap one.
rootCause: Application Insights SDK versions prior to 2.14 use the 'WebSite_HostName' App Services environment variable to set cloud_RoleName. This variable is not updated correctly after a slot swap. SDK 2.14+ switched to reading the 'WAS-DEFAULT-HOSTNAME' HTTP header instead, which resolves the issue in most cases.
solution: 1. If using .NET/.NET Core SDK, upgrade to version >= 2.14. 2. If using codeless attach (uses older SDK): either switch to manual SDK >= 2.14, wait for App Services to fix WebSite_HostName behavior, or implement a custom Telemetry Initializer to control cloud_RoleName manually. In some edge cases an additional app restart after swap resolves the stale value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/29308

symptom: After the February 2021 App Service Web Apps Application Insights Extension update (v2.8.38), automatic instrumentation stops working and no telemetry is generated for apps targeting .NET Core 2.0, 2.1, 2.2, or 3.0.
rootCause: Extension v2.8.38 intentionally dropped support for .NET Core 2.0, 2.1, 2.2, and 3.0 as these versions are out of Microsoft support per the .NET Core support policy. By design.
solution: Migrate the application to a supported .NET Core version (3.1, 5.0, 6.0+). If migration is not immediately feasible, switch to manual SDK instrumentation (not recommended as a long-term solution). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/azure-web-apps#release-notes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/30206

symptom: A small random number of Application Insights telemetry records (exported via Diagnostic Settings to blob storage) appear in the wrong time-bucketed blob container. For example, records with TimeGenerated of 13:00 or 14:00 are written to the 12:00 blob container instead.
rootCause: Bug in the ingestion pipeline between Application Insights, Geneva, and Monitor Essentials (OBO). The original export logic uses each record's timestamp/TimeGenerated to select the target blob container, but processing delays cause records to be routed to the bucket corresponding to when ingestion started rather than when the record was actually generated.
solution: Workaround: When searching for specific telemetry records, scan all blob container files within a 48-hour window around the expected time. PG planned to fix this by switching blob routing to use 'Time Received' instead of 'TimeGenerated'; implementation was expected to take several months from November 2022.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/34075

symptom: Distributed tracing breaks when Azure EventGrid is in the middle of a call chain (e.g. APIM → EventGrid → Azure Functions, or Function App → EventGrid → App Services). Application Map and End-to-End Transaction Details show the chain split into two separate operations with different Operation IDs instead of one continuous trace.
rootCause: EventGrid uses the CloudEvents protocol instead of standard HTTP Correlation Protocol or W3C Trace Context headers. It does not propagate the 'Request-Id' or 'traceparent' headers required by Application Insights distributed tracing, causing a correlation break at the EventGrid boundary.
solution: Workaround: Insert an intermediate API between the calling service and EventGrid. The intermediate API must: (1) receive calls with App Insights SDK enabled, (2) extract the telemetry Operation ID from System.Diagnostics.Activity.Current.Context, (3) set it as 'traceparent' on the outgoing CloudEvent object via the EventGrid SDK, then call EventGrid. This propagates the correlation ID through EventGrid to downstream services. Note: workaround is untested by PG; set customer expectations accordingly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/37743

symptom: Customer receives an Availability Test alert notification but all monitored sites are up and responding normally. The alert notification looks identical to a genuine site-down alert.
rootCause: All Availability Standard Tests include a proactive SSL certificate lifetime check. When the certificate remaining lifetime crosses a configured threshold (default 7 days; options: 1, 30, 90, or 365 days), an alert notification is sent that is visually indistinguishable from a site-down alert.
solution: 1. Validate alert cause via webtest Results tab in the portal. 2. Query availabilityResults (or AppAvailabilityResults for workspace-based AI) for failed records and inspect the 'message' field for 'Certificate will expire in ### day(s)'. 3. If confirmed SSL expiration alert, renew the certificate. PG improvement tracking: ADO #14600490 - separate notification template for cert expiration alerts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/38005

symptom: Live Metrics 显示红色横幅 'Data is temporarily inaccessible'，Live Metrics 界面无数据
rootCause: 第三方浏览器扩展（如 UBlock Origin）拦截了对 *.monitor.azure.com 的请求，该域名专用于 Live Metrics 功能
solution: 逐一启用浏览器扩展定位罪魁祸首，然后禁用该扩展，或在其设置中放行 https://*.livediagnostics.monitor.azure.com/ 等 Live Metrics 所需 URL；可使用 HAR trace 捕获被阻止的请求
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/38124

symptom: ARM 模板或 Terraform 脚本尝试设置 Application Insights 的 ImmediatePurgeDataOn30Days 属性时，返回 400 Bad Request，错误消息 'ImmediatePurgeDataOn30Days cannot be set on current api-version'
rootCause: ImmediatePurgeDataOn30Days 设置仅适用于 Classic Application Insights 组件；Workspace-based 组件不支持修改此属性，属于 By Design
solution: Workspace-based Application Insights 组件无法调整此设置，这是预期行为。若必须使用此功能，需使用 Classic 类型的 Application Insights 资源
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/38329

symptom: Application Insights JavaScript SDK 记录的 PageView 遥测中，Windows 11 设备的操作系统显示为 Windows 10，影响 Edge、Firefox、Chrome 等所有基于 Chromium 的浏览器
rootCause: 浏览器 UserAgent 字符串冻结机制（Google 主导的行业决策）：浏览器故意将 UA 中的 OS 版本报告为 Windows 10，JS SDK 依赖 UA 字符串检测操作系统版本，因此无法获取真实 Windows 11 信息
solution: 目前无可用 Workaround。JS SDK 团队在研究使用 NavigatorUAData.getHighEntropyValues() API 的可行性，但该 API 存在异步延迟和用户同意弹窗问题，尚无修复 ETA
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/38332

symptom: .NET Framework 4.8.1 应用启用 Application Insights 后，看不到任何 SQL 依赖遥测（但 HTTP 依赖和请求遥测正常），无论是手动 SDK 还是自动插桩均受影响
rootCause: .NET 4.8.1 的 System.Data 命名空间存在回归 Bug：EventSource 配置失败，导致 SQL 事件从不触发；App Insights SDK 依赖 EventSource 监听 SQL 事件，因此在 .NET 4.8.1 上无法采集 SQL 依赖
solution: 当前唯一解决方案：降级到 .NET 4.x 旧版本（4.8.0 或更早），或将应用重写为 .NET Core/.NET 5+。.NET Framework 团队已有跟踪 Bug（DevDiv#1694192），但修复时间线不明确
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/39126

symptom: Palo Alto 防火墙虚拟设备配置了 Application Insights 遥测采集，出现：全部遥测丢失、部分字段缺失、特定时段遥测缺失，或客户询问如何修改遥测采集行为
rootCause: Palo Alto 使用已废弃的 Legacy Python SDK（zooba/AppInsights-Python）集成 Application Insights，且对 SDK 配置进行了抽象封装，CSS 能力有限。支持范围受限属于预期
solution: （1）检查 ASC Ingestion Tab 确认摄取端点是否丢弃数据；（2）通过 curl/nslookup 验证防火墙到 AI 端点的网络连通性；（3）若 CSS 排查指向 SDK 问题，告知客户联系 Palo Alto 支持继续调查；（4）明确设定支持边界预期
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/39351

symptom: 在 Application Insights 端到端视图（E2E View）中点击某个 operation 后，显示大量（有时近百个）不同的 operation ID，而非预期的单个 operation 链路
rootCause: By Design：_MS.links（Operation Links）属性用于在批处理场景（如 Service Bus、EventHub）中将多个独立 trace 关联起来——一个发送事件可被多个消费者读取，所有消费者的 operation ID 都被 stitched 到父 operation
solution: （1）禁用依赖跟踪（EnableDependencyTrackingTelemetryModule=false）——代价是失去所有出站调用遥测；（2）推荐：将 DependencyTrackingTelemetryModule.EnableAzureSdkTelemetryListener 设为 false，阻止 _MS.links 属性添加到依赖遥测中；（3）实现自定义 TelemetryInitializer 删除依赖遥测中的 _MS.links 属性
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/39404

symptom: Java 应用的 Application Insights 遥测完全停止流入，Java 自诊断日志中出现警告：'WARN c.m.applicationinsights.agent - System property -Djsse.enableSNIExtension=false is detected. If you have connection issues with Application Insights, please remove this.'
rootCause: JVM 参数 -Djsse.enableSNIExtension=false 禁用了 SSL 握手中的 Server Name Indication（SNI），导致与 Application Insights 端点的 SSL 连接失败
solution: 移除 -Djsse.enableSNIExtension=false 或将其改为 true（默认值）。若参数出现多次，确保没有重复出现设为 false 的情况（JVM 使用最后一个参数值），保持只有一个且值为 true
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/41351

symptom: 同时使用 Application Insights .NET SDK 和 OpenTelemetry Exporter 并行插桩（Side by Side）时，在负载情况下 Adaptive Sampling 生成的遥测记录数超出预期
rootCause: Application Insights .NET SDK 的 Adaptive Sampler 与 OpenTelemetry Exporter 并行使用时存在代码 Bug（GitHub Issue #2742），导致采样行为异常，摄取数据量超出采样配置上限
solution: 临时 Workaround：改用 Fixed Rate Sampling 替代 Adaptive Sampling，直到 OpenTelemetry GA 版本修复此问题
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/45601

symptom: 使用 workspace()/app() 跨资源查询时，若参数值使用资源名称（仅名称）或限定名称（subscriptionName/resourceGroup/componentName 格式），查询返回 '400 Bad Request: AmbiguousError' 或出现性能问题/超时
rootCause: 资源名称和限定名称两种格式在 cross-workspace 查询中已被废弃，存在歧义（用户有权访问多个同名资源时）和性能问题。公开文档已更新移除这两种推荐方式
solution: 在 workspace()/app() 函数参数中改用完整 Azure 资源 ID 或 workspace/app ID：推荐使用 workspace ID（不可变）；避免使用资源名或限定名
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/48639

symptom: （1）2023 年 3 月后出现数据丢失，而此前 Daily Cap 实际未被执行；（2）Daily Cap 配置后仍被超越；（3）Application Insights Daily Cap 未被触发但数据已丢失
rootCause: （1）2023 年 3 月修复前，Workspace-based AI 的 Daily Cap 实际未被正确执行，3 月后开始强制执行，之前超额摄取的情况不会再发生；（2）仅使用 Instrumentation Key（使用全球摄取端点）时 Daily Cap 不可靠，全球端点已废弃；（3）底层 Log Analytics Workspace 有独立的 Daily Cap，可能先于 AI 组件 Cap 被触发
solution: （1）使用带区域端点的 Connection String 替代纯 Instrumentation Key，确保 Daily Cap 可靠执行；（2）分别监控 AI 组件和底层 LA Workspace 的 Daily Cap；（3）使用 ASC Ingestion Tab 和 Kusto 查询（参见 How-to: Check if the daily cap was reached）判断是哪一层 Cap 被触发
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/49764

symptom: AMPLS 私有链接配置后，Application Insights Ingestion Endpoint 和 Live Endpoint 的 DNS 解析未验证，无法确认流量是否走私有 IP
solution: SSH 进入 Web App/Function App 控制台，对 Application Insights Ingestion Endpoint 和 Live Endpoint 分别执行 nslookup；若两者均解析为 AMPLS->PE->Private DNS Zone 中配置的私有 IP（而非公网 IP），则 AMPLS 私有链接配置正确
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/AMPLs Setup Guide - Hub and Spoke Networks Private Link Design/Test the Azure Monitor Private Link Connection

symptom: Application Insights telemetry not being ingested through AMPLS; ingestion fails or errors when running queries when application is hosted on App Service with AMPLS configured
rootCause: App Service free tier does not support VNet integration. The application resolves ingestion endpoints to a public IP instead of the private AMPLS endpoint, causing ingestion failure when AMPLS is configured in private-only mode.
solution: Ensure the web app or function app hosting the application is on a paid App Service plan that supports VNet integration. The free App Service plan does NOT support VNet and cannot use AMPLS for private ingestion. Upgrade to at least a Basic or Standard plan to enable VNet integration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/Support Topics/I receive no data or cannot run queries/Errors when running queries, or other issue accessing data

symptom: Resource resolves Azure Monitor endpoint FQDN to a public IP address instead of the expected private IP address within a private-link-enabled VNet
rootCause: The VNet is not using the Private DNS zones established by AMPLS and the Private Endpoint — either Private Endpoint DNS integration is disabled, or the client resource is not integrated into the VNet with AMPLS
solution: Ensure AMPLS is tied to a Private Endpoint on the same VNet as the client. During PE creation, confirm 'Integrate with private DNS zone' = Yes to auto-register private DNS zones. Run nslookup on the client to confirm the FQDN resolves to the private IP shown in the PE's DNS record sets.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Validate Known Good AMPLS configuration

symptom: Resource resolves Azure Monitor endpoint FQDN to a private IP but not the expected one (IP mismatch between nslookup result and AMPLS-defined IP)
rootCause: Another AMPLS resource was added to the same VNet or a peered VNet, creating duplicate/conflicting Private DNS zone records for Azure Monitor endpoints
solution: Follow guiding principle 'Avoid DNS overrides by using a single AMPLS'. In ASC, check App Insights component Properties > Private Links to identify all associated AMPLS resources. Remove the conflicting AMPLS so only one AMPLS covers each VNet. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/private-link-design#guiding-principle-avoid-dns-overrides-by-using-a-single-ampls
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Validate Known Good AMPLS configuration

symptom: AMPLS Private Link 配置完成后，Web App / Function App 中对 Application Insights 端点（dc.services.visualstudio.com / rt.services.visualstudio.com）的 nslookup 仍解析到公网 IP，而非私有 IP
rootCause: 以下一个或多个配置缺失：(1) Private DNS Zone 中未为 Spoke VNET 添加 Virtual Network Link；(2) Spoke VNET 与 Hub VNET 之间的 VNET Peering 未处于 Connected 状态；(3) Web App / Function App 的 VNET Integration 未绑定正确的 Spoke VNET
solution: 1) 检查 AMPLs → PE → Private DNS Zone 中是否存在针对 Spoke VNET 的 Virtual Network Link，若无则添加；2) 验证 Spoke ↔ Hub VNET Peering 状态为 Connected；3) 确认 Web App 出站 VNET Integration 使用的是 Spoke VNET
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/AMPLs Setup Guide - Hub and Spoke Networks Private Link Design/Test the Azure Monitor Private Link Connection

symptom: Azure Monitor data ingestion fails from a resource (web app/function app) within a VNet configured with AMPLS; nslookup on the Azure Monitor ingestion endpoint returns a public IP address instead of a private IP
rootCause: The resource is not using the Private DNS zones established by AMPLS and the Private Endpoint within the VNet. The resource either lacks VNet integration or the Private DNS zones are not linked to the VNet, causing DNS resolution to fall back to public Azure DNS
solution: 1. Run nslookup from the resource to confirm it returns a public IP. 2. Verify the resource has VNet integration enabled pointing to the VNet containing the AMPLS-associated Private Endpoint. 3. Verify Private DNS zones created by AMPLS + Private Endpoint are linked to the resource's VNet. 4. In ASC, find the Azure Monitor resource > Properties > Private Links to confirm the AMPLS resource association. 5. On the AMPLS resource, confirm the Private Endpoint is on the same VNet as the resource. 6. For Windows App Services, use nameresolver.exe instead of nslookup to check DNS resolution from Kudu console.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Validate Known Good AMPLS configuration

symptom: Azure Monitor data ingestion fails; nslookup on the Azure Monitor endpoint returns a private IP but it does not match the private IP shown in the AMPLS resource's Private Endpoint configuration
rootCause: A second AMPLS resource was added to the VNet or a peered VNet. AMPLS only allows one instance per VNet; adding a second AMPLS causes clients to resolve the Azure Monitor FQDN to the private IP defined by the wrong (conflicting) AMPLS resource
solution: 1. In ASC, find the Azure Monitor resource > Properties > Private Links to get the correct AMPLS and its Private Endpoint. 2. On the Private Endpoint properties, note the expected private IP for the Azure Monitor FQDN. 3. Compare the nslookup result with the expected IP. 4. Search all subscriptions in the network topology for other AMPLS resources that may be defining conflicting DNS records. 5. Consolidate to a single AMPLS per the guiding principle: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/private-link-design#guiding-principle-avoid-dns-overrides-by-using-a-single-ampls. 6. Remove or reconfigure the conflicting AMPLS resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Validate Known Good AMPLS configuration

symptom: App/resource on VNet resolves AMPLS-monitored Azure Monitor endpoint FQDN (e.g. App Insights ingestion endpoint) to a public IP address instead of expected private IP
rootCause: Resource not using Private DNS zones within the VNet; AMPLS Private Endpoint DNS integration ('Integrate with private DNS zone') not properly configured, or Private Endpoint not on the same VNet as the client resource
solution: Verify AMPLS Private Endpoint is attached to the same VNet as the client resource; ensure 'Integrate with private DNS zone' is enabled during Private Endpoint creation; confirm nslookup from client returns a private IP matching the AMPLS PE config (not a public IP); use ASC to navigate AI/LA component > Properties > Private Links to identify the associated AMPLS and PE details
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Validate Known Good AMPLS configuration

symptom: nslookup returns a private IP for an Azure Monitor endpoint but not the expected private IP defined in AMPLS configuration; telemetry or log data fails to reach the endpoint despite resolving to a private address
rootCause: Multiple AMPLS resources exist on the same VNet or peered VNets, causing DNS override conflicts; violates the 'single AMPLS per VNet' guiding principle — the incorrect AMPLS resource elsewhere in the network defines a different private IP for the same endpoint FQDN
solution: Identify the conflicting AMPLS resource: in ASC check all privateLinkScopes in the subscription and peered subscriptions; on each AMPLS check its associated Private Endpoint and 'Private DNS Zone Record Sets' to find which one defines the unexpected IP; remove the extra AMPLS resource or redesign network topology to use a single AMPLS per VNet per the 'Guiding principle: Avoid DNS overrides by using a single AMPLS' (https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/private-link-design#guiding-principle-avoid-dns-overrides-by-using-a-single-ampls)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Validate Known Good AMPLS configuration

symptom: 配置 AMPLS 后，Application Insights 遥测端点（dc.services.visualstudio.com、live.applicationinsights.azure.com）DNS 解析仍返回公网 IP 而非私有 IP，遥测数据未经私有链路传输
rootCause: 以下一项或多项缺失/错误：(1) Spoke VNET 的 Virtual Network Link 未添加到 AMPLs→PE→Private DNS Zone；(2) Spoke VNET 与 Hub VNET 的 VNET Peering 状态非 Connected；(3) Web App/Function App 的 VNET Integration 未配置为路由全部出站流量到 Spoke VNET；(4) AMPLS Ingestion Access Mode 未设为 Private Only
solution: 从 App Service Console/SSH 执行 nslookup dc.services.visualstudio.com 和 nslookup live.applicationinsights.azure.com，验证是否解析到私有 IP。若返回公网 IP，按序检查：(1) AMPLs→PE→Private DNS Zone 是否有 Spoke VNET 的 Virtual Network Link（Linked 状态）；(2) Spoke↔Hub VNET Peering 状态是否为 Connected；(3) Web App VNET Integration 是否使用 Spoke VNET；(4) AMPLS Ingestion Access Mode 是否为 Private Only
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/AMPLs Setup Guide - Hub and Spoke Networks Private Link Design/Test the Azure Monitor Private Link Connection

symptom: AMPLS Hub-Spoke 配置完成后，从 Web App SSH Console 执行 nslookup 发现 App Insights Ingestion Endpoint（dc.services.visualstudio.com）或 Live Endpoint 仍解析到公网 IP，而非 Private DNS Zone 中配置的私有 IP，导致遥测数据未能通过私有链路传输
rootCause: AMPLS Private Endpoint 关联的 Private DNS Zone（privatelink.monitor.azure.com）未链接 Spoke VNET，导致 Web App 所在网络内的 DNS 查询无法命中私有 IP 记录，仍走公网 DNS 解析
solution: 在 AMPLS → Private Endpoint → DNS configuration → privatelink.monitor.azure.com Private DNS Zone 中，添加指向 Spoke VNET 的 Virtual Network Link。完成后从 Web App SSH Console 运行 nslookup dc.services.visualstudio.com 和 nslookup live.applicationinsights.azure.com，验证返回结果为 Private DNS Zone 中配置的私有 IP 地址
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/AMPLs Setup Guide - Hub and Spoke Networks Private Link Design/Test the Azure Monitor Private Link Connection

symptom: nslookup / nameresolver.exe returns public IP for Azure Monitor ingestion endpoint (Application Insights or Log Analytics) despite AMPLS Private Link being configured; data may not be ingested via private channel
rootCause: The App Service or Function App is not using the Private DNS zones within the VNET that were established by AMPLS and the Private Endpoint — typically VNET integration is not enabled or subnet delegation is missing, so the app bypasses private DNS and resolves to the public endpoint
solution: 1. Run nameresolver.exe (Windows App Service/Function App Kudu → Debug Console → PowerShell) or nslookup on the ingestion endpoint FQDN. 2. If public IP returned, enable VNET Integration on the App Service/Function App pointing to the correct subnet. 3. Confirm Private DNS zones are linked to the VNET. 4. Re-run DNS check to confirm private IP is now returned. 5. To validate known-good: check ASC Properties of App Insights → Private Links → navigate to PE → DNS Zone Record Sets, confirm FQDN resolves to the private IP listed there.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Validate Known Good AMPLS configuration

symptom: AMPLS - VMs on VNET cannot resolve App Insights / Log Analytics / Azure Monitor endpoints after removing resource from Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS). DNS resolution returns 'domain not found' for privatelink.monitor.azure.com domains.
rootCause: When removing AI/LA from AMPLS, A-Records in Private DNS Zones are deleted but the Private DNS Zones themselves remain linked to the VNET. Private clients query the empty Private DNS Zone which returns 'not found' instead of falling through to public DNS.
solution: Three options: (1) Delete Virtual Network Links and/or Private DNS Zones (only if last AMPLS resource); (2) Add HOSTS file entries mapping endpoints to public IPs; (3) Manually add CNAME records in Private DNS Zones pointing to public Traffic Manager profiles. Option 3 requires ongoing maintenance as TM profile names may change.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/54404

symptom: Application Insights component in West Central US (westcentralus) cannot query logs, shows missing data. Customers may also be unable to delete, modify or create new App Insights resources in this region.
rootCause: West Central US is an internal-only 'AIMON' region accidentally exposed publicly during onboarding (April-May 2021). OBO service calls control plane to convert AppId to WorkspaceId but gets 404 since region doesn't hold knowledge of public resource ikeys/appids, silently dropping data.
solution: Inform customer WCU is not supported for public AI resources. Create new AI resource in another region, update connection string in applications. For cleanup, get customer confirmation then open CRI to CDS Control Plane team to manually delete the rogue WCU component (portal delete will fail).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/56388

symptom: Daily cap warning threshold alerts/emails never fire for workspace-based Application Insights components. Only the final daily cap reached email arrives, but the percentage-based warning threshold email does not.
rootCause: The 'Daily cap warning level' threshold notification was never implemented for workspace-based AI components. It only works for Classic Application Insights instances.
solution: Workaround: Create a log query scheduled alert that monitors ingestion volume against the configured threshold. A PowerShell script (ThresholdAlerts.ps1) can automate creating action groups and scheduled query alerts for all workspace-based AI components in a subscription. The script uses _BilledSize to compute daily ingestion volume and triggers when exceeding the warning threshold percentage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/56627

symptom: Customer wants to move Application Insights resource to a different Azure region. Move operation is not available.
rootCause: Cross-region move for Application Insights components is not supported by design. See microsoft.insights/components in the Azure Resource Manager move support matrix.
solution: Create a new Application Insights resource in the target region and swap instrumentation keys/connection strings. Refer to the public FAQ 'How do I move an Application Insights resource to a new region?' and the internal wiki page on Swap Instrumentation Keys.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/58109

symptom: Diagnostic settings blade is missing under the Monitoring tab for an Application Insights resource in Azure Portal.
rootCause: The AI resource is a classic (non-workspace-based) type. Classic Application Insights resources do not show the Diagnostic Settings blade.
solution: Migrate the classic Application Insights resource to workspace-based using the migration guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/convert-classic-resource#migrate-your-resource
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/59209

symptom: Trace/log items appear duplicated 3-4 times in the Application Insights traces table for Java apps running on Dell Boomi platform. BOOMI logs themselves do not show duplication.
rootCause: By design (external). The Boomi platform's default logging behavior causes repeated trace emissions to Application Insights.
solution: Set the 'Write Once Per Execution' flag to true in the Boomi configuration. Related ICM: 411078017.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/61601

symptom: Customer reports data latency query shows telemetry took 2+ days (sometimes 8 days) to be ingested into Application Insights / Log Analytics. Note: AI does NOT ingest data older than 48 hours, so 3+ day latency always indicates this issue.
rootCause: Internal KCM service moves App Insights data between Kusto databases (e.g., for service healing). This data movement operation resets the ingestion_time() value, making latency calculations appear artificially inflated. All moved records will show identical ingestion_time().
solution: No fix available. Inform customer that ingestion_time() was reset by internal data movement and their latency queries are inaccurate for those records. New data ingestion is not affected unless future data movement occurs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/61748

symptom: 'Profile Now' button in Application Insights Profiler fails instantly with 'Profiling Failed. Refresh and try again.' error when AI resource is in a closed Private Link Scope (public querying disabled). Automatic profiling works fine. Browser shows 403 from gateway.azureserviceprofiler.net with message 'This resource must be accessed via Private Link'.
rootCause: An internal configuration change broke Private Link IP address resolution for the 'gateway.azureserviceprofiler.net' hostname, which resolves to the 'diagservices-query' DNS Zone entry in AMPLS. The profiler gateway cannot be reached via Private Link for on-demand profiling. Related ICM: 440629501.
solution: Workaround: Enable public network access for 'Query' in the App Insights resource's 'Network Isolation' tab. This allows the Profile Now button to reach the profiler gateway through public endpoint. A permanent fix was planned before March 2024.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/62823

symptom: Node.js Application Insights SDK does not fully integrate with Next.js 13+ App Router model. Auto-collected dependencies (e.g., Azure SDK calls) are not tracked. Some SDK functionalities don't behave as expected.
rootCause: The Node.js SDK's diagnostic-channel-publishers patching mechanism has compatibility issues with Next.js App Router's initialization model. Exact root cause unclear but related to how Next.js and Node.js initialize together under the App Router paradigm.
solution: PG was planning a sample/cleaner integration path for Next.js App Router + Node.js SDK (estimated end of Jan 2024, may be delayed). Work with SME/TA/Manager for appropriate customer communication. Related ICM: 432003586.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/62914

symptom: Data Retention button on 'Usage and estimated costs' blade remains enabled/visible for a workspace-based Application Insights component that was migrated from classic.
rootCause: By design. Migrated classic AI components still retain historical telemetry in the original underlying classic resource. The Data Retention button controls retention for that legacy data store.
solution: No action needed. Wait for all historical data in the classic resource to age out based on the configured retention period. Once aged out, the Data Retention button will automatically become disabled, and all retention is controlled from the Log Analytics workspace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/63575

symptom: High number of failures with 4xx result codes (e.g. 404) on Application Insights Failures blade, typically on operations like 'GET /' or 'GET /admin/host/ping'. Requests appear at regular intervals (every 5-15 minutes), source IP is 127.0.0.1, and operation_SyntheticSource may show 'Bot'.
rootCause: App Service features like Always On, Health Check, or Automatic Scaling trigger HTTP requests to the application root path or '/admin/host/ping'. If the app (often a Web API) is not configured to respond to these paths, 404 errors are generated. Application Insights SDK simply logs these requests — it is not the source of the errors.
solution: 1) Identify the source: check if Always On, Health Check, or Automatic Scaling is enabled. 2) Disable Always On or configure Health Check with a specific valid path instead of root. 3) For Automatic Scaling, requests go to '/admin/host/ping' — this is by design. 4) Alternatively, use telemetry processors or data collection transformations to filter out the 4xx responses. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/api-filtering-sampling
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/50100

symptom: Telemetry stops showing or disappears from a workspace-based Application Insights component. Overview pane may display a banner indicating the workspace is no longer valid; other panes (Performance, etc.) show no data.
rootCause: The Log Analytics workspace that the Application Insights component is pointed to is no longer valid — it was either deleted or moved to a different resource group.
solution: 1) Go to the Application Insights Properties pane, scroll to bottom and verify the workspace reference. 2) If the workspace was deleted: recover it via https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/delete-workspace 3) If moved to another resource group: update the workspace reference. Note: any telemetry sent while pointing to an invalid workspace is lost and cannot be recovered.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/50424

symptom: Visual Studio CodeLens method annotations for Application Insights show zero '0' values for request count and exception rate and never update, despite the application producing telemetry.
rootCause: Two issues: 1) The VS extension's KQL queries contain '\r\n' newline characters that Draft fails to deserialize (returns BadArgumentError/InvalidJsonBody). 2) The ApplicationInsightsAnnotationResourceId XML element may be missing from the .csproj file — it is not added automatically by Connected Services.
solution: 1) Add both <ApplicationInsightsResourceId> and <ApplicationInsightsAnnotationResourceId> elements inside <PropertyGroup> in the .csproj file with the full resource ID path. 2) The \r\n query issue was fixed in Visual Studio 17.7-preview2 (GA August 2023) — ensure VS is updated. Related VS bug: https://dev.azure.com/devdiv/DevDiv/_workitems/edit/1818076
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/50460

symptom: Transaction Search in Application Insights shows only limited telemetry types. ASC Ingestion chart confirms only traces, custom events, and custom metrics are being received, with old SDK versions like 'w_ap:2.6.2010.2703', 'a_sc:1.3.7.5', 'dotnet:2.6.4-28384'.
rootCause: The application's Application Insights SDK is not sending telemetry. The limited data shown is from Profiler and Snapshot Debugger, which use old SDK versions that cannot be changed. These are not representative of application telemetry.
solution: 1) Verify the SDK versions using ASC Ingestion tab (Aggregate by Raw SDK Names) or query: union AppEvents, AppTraces, AppMetrics | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) | distinct Name, SDKVersion. 2) If only Profiler/Snapshot Debugger SDKs are present, the issue is with the application's SDK instrumentation — investigate why the app SDK is not sending telemetry. 3) Set proper SAP: 'Missing All Telemetry' or 'Missing Specific Telemetry Types'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/52029

symptom: Node.js App Service with Application Insights auto-instrumentation shows '__Empty' response codes on the Failures blade. Clicking the __Empty line returns no telemetry items. The __Empty response code cannot be found in request logs.
rootCause: The Node.js SDK pre-aggregated metrics tallies requests where the client disconnects/times out/aborts before the server returns a response code as '__Empty'. This value feeds MDM which powers the Failures blade. The underlying telemetry items are rejected by Application Insights ingestion because they lack a valid response code.
solution: Disable pre-aggregated metrics using JSON configuration: set 'enableAutoCollectPreAggregatedMetrics: false'. Configure via: 1) Create a JSON config file and set APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONFIGURATION_FILE env var to its path, or 2) Pass JSON string directly in APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONFIGURATION_CONTENT env var. See: https://github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInsights-node.js#configuration. Related GitHub issue: https://github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInsights-node.js/issues/782
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/54136

symptom: Live Metrics stopped working for apps using ApplicationInsights-node.js 2.9.0; 'Could not connect to your application' message on Live Metrics blade
rootCause: Bug in Live Metrics enablement shipped with Node.JS SDK version 2.9.0
solution: Update to Node.JS SDK 2.9.1 which fixes the Live Metrics initialization issue
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64488

symptom: End-to-End Transaction Details shows hundreds/thousands of unrelated telemetry items (requests, dependencies, exceptions, traces) associated with same operation ID, spanning hours apart
rootCause: APIM default correlation protocol is 'Legacy', using Request-Id header which may generate non-unique operation IDs when inbound requests lack this header
solution: Set APIM telemetry correlation protocol setting to 'None'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64614

symptom: Adding Azure Monitor resource to AMPLS exhausts subnet IPs; provisioning gets stuck then fails after ~25 min; all connected resources show 'Failed' provisioning state; all telemetry stops flowing with 403 invalid private link id
rootCause: Subnet too small (e.g. /27) to accommodate all required DNS-to-IP mappings for AMPLS resources; control plane bug between AMPLS, CDS and NRP teams causes complete AMPLS breakage when IP exhaustion occurs
solution: 1) Remove the resource that caused IP exhaustion; 2) Add a larger subnet (e.g. /26 with 59 available IPs); 3) Delete and recreate Private Endpoint selecting the larger subnet
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64682

symptom: Adding resource to AMPLS while previous resource still provisioning breaks all existing Private Endpoint connections; all Azure Monitor resources connected to AMPLS show 'Failed' state and telemetry drops
rootCause: NRP recreate private endpoints job uses different configuration than current AMPLS state during parallel provisioning; timing bug causes PE to become unhealthy
solution: Add or remove any resource from the AMPLS to trigger a 'refresh' that gives the NRP recreate job a second chance to succeed and restore connections
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64682

symptom: Comparing telemetry data against external log sources (HTTP logs, custom logs) shows random small percentage of records missing in Application Insights requests/traces tables on some days
rootCause: By design — Application Insights telemetry channels do not guarantee 100% delivery; see MS Learn docs on telemetry channel delivery guarantees
solution: No fix available; expected behavior if loss is within a small percentage. Investigate on case-by-case basis; set expectations early with customer
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65260

symptom: Azure Function with auto-attach to App Insights randomly stops ingesting telemetry; ingestion metrics show telemetry dropped with 'e' (wrong endpoint) status code
rootCause: TelemetryConfiguration has mismatched Instrumentation Key vs Connection String ikey values; Function App randomly toggles between them, causing 307 redirect to wrong region; SDK caches redirected endpoint URL for 12+ hours, subsequent requests with correct ikey get 400 Bad Request
solution: Delete APPINSIGHTS_INSTRUMENTATIONKEY setting and only use APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING. Verify ikey values match in both settings. Check for PnP Core SDK conflict and disable its telemetry (PNP-DISABLETELEMETRY=true) if present. Restart function app for immediate mitigation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65271

symptom: ASP.NET Core app with auto-instrumentation enabled shows Application Insights as enabled but no telemetry gets collected; attach status logs show StartupHook initializes but no AspNetCore-StartupBootstrapper events
rootCause: Customer code calls WebHostDefaults.HostingStartupAssembliesKey with custom assembly list that does not include Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.StartupBootstrapper; this overwrites the ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES environment variable, preventing App Insights SDK from loading
solution: Append the original ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES env var value to the custom startup assemblies list: .UseSetting(WebHostDefaults.HostingStartupAssembliesKey, string.Join(";", HostingManager.GetModules()) + Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES"))
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65580

symptom: Telemetry ingestion fails on workspace-based Application Insights components; IngestionMode property shown as 'Disabled'; ASC ingestion tab shows 'WK' error codes
rootCause: Broken/outdated ARM reference to associated Log Analytics workspace, usually after accidentally deleting and recreating the workspace with same name but different workspace ID
solution: If workspace in soft-delete state, recover it to restore ingestion. If permanently deleted and recreated with same name, link the AI component to a different workspace and then link it back to the original workspace to update ARM references
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65964

symptom: In .NET Core apps, telemetry from logging adapters (NLog, Log4Net, ILogger) behaves differently from App Insights SDK telemetry — not sampled even when SDK telemetry is heavily sampled, missing client IP information
rootCause: In .NET Core, logging adapters use TelemetryConfiguration.Active which creates a new TelemetryConfiguration without the same processors and initializers (AdaptiveSamplingTelemetryProcessor, ClientIpHeaderTelemetryInitializer, etc.) as the SDK's configuration; behavior change from .NET Framework
solution: Set EnableActiveTelemetryConfigurationSetup=true in ApplicationInsightsServiceOptions: builder.Services.AddApplicationInsightsTelemetry(o => { o.EnableActiveTelemetryConfigurationSetup = true; })
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/66073

symptom: App Service web app on old Application Insights private site extension version 2.6.5 (.NET Framework); SDK update doesn't work; auto-instrumentation fails
rootCause: Old private site extension (Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.AzureWebSites) no longer in use but still present on some App Services, blocking the new auto-instrumentation mechanism
solution: Delete the Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.AzureWebSites folder from D:\home\siteextensions via KUDU Debug Console. If 'Access Denied', stop the App Service first, or delete the applicationHost.xdt and scmApplicationHost.xdt files inside the folder first, then stop and retry deletion. Use AppLens 'Private SiteExtension Check for AppInsights' detector to confirm
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/66919

symptom: Failed OPTIONS preflight requests in browser dev tools to Application Insights ingestion endpoint (dc.applicationinsights.us/v2/track) when using Power BI JavaScript plugin (app.powerbigov.us); POST requests show ERR_FAILED
rootCause: CORS failure — Power BI plugin sends OPTIONS request with Origin 'https://app.powerbigov.us' but Application Insights endpoints don't respond to OPTIONS requests, returning 404 Not Found; browser blocks subsequent POST
solution: No user action needed — these failed requests don't impact the application. The telemetry calls are from the Power BI plugin's embedded code, not the customer's app. If prevention is needed, engage Power BI team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/67745

symptom: Live Metrics stopped working after updating to ApplicationInsights-node.js 2.9.0. 'Could not connect to your application' message on Live Metrics blade.
rootCause: Bug with Live Metrics enablement shipped in Node.JS SDK version 2.9.0.
solution: Update to ApplicationInsights-node.js SDK version 2.9.1 which fixes the Live Metrics initialization issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64488

symptom: Hundreds or thousands of unrelated telemetry items (requests, dependencies, exceptions, traces) associated with the same operation ID in End-to-End Transaction Details, even for distinct calls hours apart.
rootCause: APIM default correlation protocol is 'Legacy', using Request-Id HTTP header. If inbound request lacks this header, APIM generates a non-unique operation ID.
solution: Set the APIM telemetry correlation protocol to 'None' instead of 'Legacy'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64614

symptom: Adding Azure Monitor resource to AMPLS exhausts subnet IPs, all connected resources enter Failed provisioning state. Telemetry drops with 403 'invalid private link id'. NRP logs show SubnetIsFull error.
rootCause: Bug in control plane code between AMPLS, CDS and NRP teams. Subnet IP exhaustion during provisioning corrupts all existing AMPLS connections.
solution: 1) Remove the resource that caused IP exhaustion. 2) Add a larger subnet (e.g. /26 instead of /27). 3) Delete and recreate the Private Endpoint on the larger subnet. IP needs: AI first=10 addl=2; LA first=10 addl=2; DCR first=11 addl=3.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64682

symptom: Adding resource to AMPLS while a previous resource is still provisioning breaks existing PE connections. All scoped resources enter Failed state, telemetry stops.
rootCause: Timing bug: NRP recreates Private Endpoint with a different configuration than current AMPLS state when parallel provisioning operations overlap.
solution: Add or remove any resource from the AMPLS to trigger a 'refresh' that re-runs the recreate job. Avoid adding multiple resources simultaneously.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64682

symptom: Random, small percentage of telemetry records missing. HTTP log request counts vs AppRequests or custom logs vs AppTraces show occasional daily discrepancies.
rootCause: By design. Application Insights telemetry channels do not guarantee 100% delivery. Small random loss is expected behavior.
solution: No fix available (expected behavior). Set expectations early with customer. Refer to 'Investigate randomly missing telemetry' and 'Intermittent or Partial Loss of Telemetry' wiki guides.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65260

symptom: Azure Function App telemetry randomly drops with 'e' (wrong endpoint) status code in ingestion metrics. Telemetry works after restart but gaps reappear randomly.
rootCause: TelemetryConfiguration has mismatched IKey values between instrumentationKey and connectionString fields. Function App randomly toggles between them, causing 307 redirect to wrong region. SDK caches redirect URL for 12+ hours.
solution: Delete APPINSIGHTS_INSTRUMENTATIONKEY setting, only use APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING. Ensure matching ikey if both present. If using PnP Core SDK, set PNP-DISABLETELEMETRY=true. Restart for immediate mitigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65271

symptom: ASP.NET Core app on App Service/Function/VM with auto-instrumentation enabled but no telemetry collected. StartupHook initializes but no StartupBootstrapper events in attach status.
rootCause: App code calls WebHostDefaults.HostingStartupAssembliesKey with custom assemblies, overwriting ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES without including Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.StartupBootstrapper.
solution: Append original env var value to custom list: .UseSetting(WebHostDefaults.HostingStartupAssembliesKey, string.Join(";", HostingManager.GetModules()) + Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES"))
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65580

symptom: Workspace-based Application Insights component shows IngestionMode as 'Disabled'. Telemetry ingestion fails. ASC Ingestion tab shows WK error codes.
rootCause: Broken/outdated reference to associated Log Analytics workspace, typically from accidental workspace deletion. Recreating workspace with same name creates different workspace ID causing mismatch.
solution: If workspace in soft-delete: recover it. If permanently deleted and recreated: link AI component to a different workspace, then link back to original to refresh ARM references and restore ingestion.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65964

symptom: .NET Core app: telemetry from logging adapters (NLog, Log4Net, ILogger) not sampled while App Insights native telemetry is sampled. Or logging adapter telemetry missing client IP information.
rootCause: .NET Core SDK behavior change: logging adapters use TelemetryConfiguration.Active which returns a new config without standard processors (AdaptiveSamplingTelemetryProcessor) and initializers (ClientIpHeaderTelemetryInitializer).
solution: Set EnableActiveTelemetryConfigurationSetup=true: builder.Services.AddApplicationInsightsTelemetry(o => { o.EnableActiveTelemetryConfigurationSetup = true; }); This populates TelemetryConfiguration.Active with same processors/initializers as main config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/66073

symptom: App Service auto-instrumentation fails. Old Application Insights private site extension version 2.6.5 still present, preventing SDK updates.
rootCause: Outdated Application Insights private site extension (2.6.5) no longer in use but still installed in rare cases, blocking newer auto-instrumentation.
solution: Remove via KUDU: Advanced Tools > Debug Console > navigate to D:\home\siteextensions > delete 'Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.AzureWebSites'. If access denied: stop App Service first, or delete applicationHost.xdt and scmApplicationHost.xdt inside the folder. Use AppLens 'Private SiteExtension Check' detector to confirm.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/66919

symptom: Failed OPTIONS preflight requests to Application Insights ingestion endpoints (dc.applicationinsights.us/v2/track) in browser dev tools. 404 Not Found for CORS requests. Console errors: POST net::ERR_FAILED.
rootCause: Power BI JavaScript plugin (app.powerbigov.us) embeds code sending telemetry to AI ingestion endpoints. AI endpoints don't respond to OPTIONS requests, so CORS preflight fails with 404 and the POST never executes.
solution: No impact on the application - failed requests are harmless. The telemetry POST never executes due to CORS failure. If customer wants to prevent the calls, engage Power BI team. External plugin code outside customer control.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/67745

symptom: Duplicated Exception telemetry from API Management service into Application Insights. Exception telemetry becomes duplicated when APIM policy code throws C# exceptions while Application Insights integration is enabled.
rootCause: Bug in a gateway component within APIM's infrastructure that duplicates exception records (different _ItemId values in AppExceptions table).
solution: Known APIM bug. Work item Bug 18434395 exists for APIM team to fix. Reference IcM 469904134.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68078

symptom: PowerShell Update-AzApplicationInsights cmdlet strips existing Tags from Application Insights resource when updating the component.
rootCause: Bug in Azure PowerShell code: spelling error 'AddtionalProperties' instead of 'AdditionalProperties' causes existing tags to be lost during update.
solution: Workaround: Retrieve current tags first using Get-AzApplicationInsights, store in hashtable, then pass $tagHashtable via -Tag parameter to Update-AzApplicationInsights. Azure CLI is not affected. Fix expected in next Azure PowerShell milestone.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68086

symptom: Unexpected latency (up to 24 hours) in telemetry arriving at Application Insights from Dynamics 365 Power Platform CDS Data Export feature.
rootCause: By design: latency from D365 Power Platform CDS Data Export source can be up to 24 hours SLA, with typical delays of 30-90 minutes. Falls outside Application Insights PG scope.
solution: No resolution to improve this latency. Identify telemetry source via cloud_RoleName / AppRoleName field showing 'CDS Data Export'. Only applicable when delay is at source (client side); if ingestion pipeline delay, follow normal investigation. SAP: Dynamics/Power Platform Administration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68452

symptom: Application Insights E2E transaction view shows empty page with message 'This transaction doesn't contain any requests, dependencies, or exceptions' despite telemetry items existing in logs for the operation ID.
rootCause: Circular reference between two telemetry items' parent IDs — neither can be determined as root parent, so the E2E Gantt chart cannot render the hierarchy.
solution: Customer must update application code to break the circular reference between telemetry items' parent-child IDs. See custom operations tracking documentation. App Insights SDKs do not produce this by default; occurs with manual tracking or manual id/parentId setting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/69618

symptom: Live Metrics CPU Total % chart displays percentage higher than 100% (can reach 200-400+%).
rootCause: By design: Live Metrics monitors process-specific \Process(??APP_WIN32_PROC??)\% Processor Time which reports per-process CPU across all cores, while SDK monitors \Processor(_Total)\% Processor Time which is averaged. On multi-core machines, process CPU maximum = 100 * ProcessorCount.
solution: No fix, by design. Only impacts Live Metrics CPU chart. Inform customers the CPU values over 100% result from multi-core machines. OTel SDKs will have more consistent Live Metrics reporting in future.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/69742

symptom: Users column missing in Application Insights Failures pane operation name table.
rootCause: By design: the Failures blade query uses dcountif(user_Id,...) to calculate userCount. If userCount is zero (no user_Id populated in telemetry), the Users column is not displayed.
solution: Customer must produce telemetry that populates the user_Id field. Most common approach: collect browser-based client-side telemetry with Application Insights JavaScript SDK which auto-populates user_Id with default values (e.g., via PageViews telemetry).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70423

symptom: Unable to find or add NuGet packages (including Application Insights SDK) in Visual Studio project.
rootCause: NuGet public package source (nuget.org) not configured in Visual Studio's Package Manager Settings.
solution: Go to Tools > NuGet Package Manager > Package Manager Settings > Package Sources. Add new source with URL https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json. Save changes and restart Visual Studio. Ensure Package Source dropdown is set to 'All'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70443

symptom: String values in dynamic fields (customDimensions/Properties) automatically converted to datetime or other scalar types in Application Insights / Log Analytics. E.g., custom string '9814-05-01' becomes datetime '9814-05-01T00:00:00.0000000Z'.
rootCause: By design: Kusto auto-parses string values in dynamic fields that match supported datetime formats (####-##-##) into strongly-typed datetime values. Dynamic field type in App Insights default tables enables this behavior.
solution: Workaround: break the regex pattern Kusto uses for auto-parsing, e.g., prefix the string value to prevent matching datetime format. Changing table field type from dynamic to string would prevent this, but App Insights team will not change default table schema. Alternatively, use tostring() in queries to cast back.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70616

symptom: Slack CloudBot for Azure add-on fails when connecting to Application Insights resource to query telemetry using API key.
rootCause: Conflict querying telemetry from backend Log Analytics workspace. Common causes: (1) connecting to classic Application Insights resource, (2) associated LA workspace has been deleted, (3) workspace Access Control Mode set to 'Required workspace permissions' blocking API key queries.
solution: Ensure backend LA workspace exists and can accept API key queries. Set Access Control Mode to 'Use resource or workspace permissions'. If issue persists, redirect customer to CloudBot support (third-party tool, obscures REST API calls).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70970

symptom: Application Insights Profiler on Azure Functions fails with error 'Merging of ETL files has failed (0x80070006)'. COMException at DiagnosticsHub.StandardCollector.Host.Interop.ICollectionSession.Stop. No profiling sessions recorded.
rootCause: Believed to be resource limitations on the VM hosting the Function worker, causing ETL file merge to fail during profiling session stop.
solution: Potential workaround: scale App Service plan up/down to move worker to a new VM (not always successful). Check Antares version. Profiler team awaiting Antares 102 rollout which changes merge process and enhances troubleshooting. Reference IcM 454107428.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/71664

symptom: Exception telemetry duplicated when API Management (APIM) sends telemetry to Application Insights. Occurs when C# code in APIM policies throws exceptions. Different _ItemId values for each duplicate in AppExceptions.
rootCause: Bug in APIM gateway component causes exception telemetry to be recorded twice (Bug 18434395).
solution: APIM team bug fix pending (Bug 18434395). No customer-side workaround available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68078

symptom: Running Update-AzApplicationInsights PowerShell cmdlet clears all existing Tags from the Application Insights resource. Tags collection wiped on every update.
rootCause: Typo in Azure PowerShell code: AddtionalProperties instead of AdditionalProperties causes Tags to be lost during update.
solution: Workaround: retrieve current Tags via Get-AzApplicationInsights, build hashtable from Tag.AdditionalProperties, pass -Tag param. Azure CLI not affected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68086

symptom: Unexpected latency (up to 24h, typically 30-90 min) in telemetry arriving at Application Insights from D365 Power Platform CDS Data Export.
rootCause: By design. Power Platform Data Export has documented SLA of up to 24h latency. Outside App Insights PG scope.
solution: No fix. Identify source via cloud_RoleName = CDS Data Export. If server-side ingestion delay, follow normal delayed telemetry investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68452

symptom: E2E transaction view (Gantt chart) shows blank page with no requests/dependencies/exceptions message, but telemetry items exist in Logs for the operation_id.
rootCause: Circular reference between two telemetry items, each points to the other as parent (operation_ParentId). E2E view cannot determine root parent.
solution: Customer must fix application code to break circular parent-child reference. SDKs do not produce this by default; occurs with manual telemetry tracking.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/69618

symptom: Live Metrics CPU Total % chart shows values exceeding 100% (200-400%+) in Application Insights portal.
rootCause: By design. Live Metrics monitors process-specific Processor Time (per-process, can exceed 100% on multi-core), while SDK monitors total average capped at 100%.
solution: No fix. Max CPU = 100 * ProcessorCount on multi-core. OTel SDKs will have more consistent reporting. Only Live Metrics CPU chart affected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/69742

symptom: Failures blade missing Users column in operation name table. Only operation_Name, sum_itemCount, failedCount shown; no userCount column.
rootCause: By design. Failures experience hides Users column when all dcountif(user_Id) values are zero in query results.
solution: Populate user_Id in telemetry. Common approach: collect browser-side telemetry with App Insights JavaScript SDK which auto-populates user_Id.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70423

symptom: Cannot find NuGet packages (including Application Insights SDKs) in Visual Studio NuGet Package Manager. Package search returns no results.
rootCause: NuGet package source (nuget.org) not configured in Visual Studio Package Manager Settings.
solution: Add source https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json in Tools > NuGet Package Manager > Package Sources. Save, restart VS. Set Package source to All.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70443

symptom: String values in dynamic fields (customDimensions/Properties) auto-converted to datetime or scalar types upon ingestion. E.g., 9814-05-01 becomes 9814-05-01T00:00:00Z.
