rootCause: Second AMPLS on different network sharing same DNS server overwrote entries, or custom DNS not forwarding to Azure DNS 168.63.129.16
solution: Wrong IP: remove and recreate private endpoint to refresh DNS. No IP: verify DNS server connectivity and Azure DNS forwarding. Check NSGs between VMs and endpoints
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Network isolation and Private Link (AMPLS)

symptom: Need to purge specific telemetry data from Application Insights but no UI option available in portal
rootCause: No built-in UI for data purge. Must use Workspace Purge REST API with KQL query
solution: Use Workspace Purge REST API. Test KQL in Logs blade first. Save operation ID for status tracking via Get Purge Status API. May take days
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Purge telemetry

symptom: Need to recover accidentally deleted Application Insights component and restore telemetry data
rootCause: AI supports soft-delete recovery. For workspace-based, LA workspace must be recovered first if also deleted
solution: 1. Recover LA workspace via soft-delete. 2. Use ASC to find recoverable AI components. 3. Follow recovery procedure. Use Azure Lock to prevent future deletion
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Recover deleted resource

symptom: Application Insights telemetry dropped with reason code U or V after disabling local auth for Entra ID authentication
rootCause: Entra ID auth not correctly configured. Could be unsupported SDK version or incorrect credential setup
solution: Check SDK minimum version per language. Use Managed Identity for production. Check Dropped by Reason U/V/F in ASC. See MS Learn troubleshooting
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Secure ingestion with Entra ID

symptom: JavaScript SDK cannot use Entra ID authentication for Application Insights, no auth configuration option exists
rootCause: JavaScript SDK does not support Entra ID authentication. Current platform limitation with no ETA
solution: Use separate AI component with local auth for JS telemetry, or use Azure APIM as proxy with Entra auth. Do not disable local auth if JS SDK is required
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Secure ingestion with Entra ID

symptom: Application Insights data retention settings not preserved after Classic to workspace-based migration, drops to 90 days
rootCause: Migration does not carry over Classic retention settings. LA workspace defaults to 90 days for AI data types
solution: Manually configure LA workspace retention after migration. Use REST API for per-table or portal for workspace-wide retention
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Classic Application Insights retirement

symptom: Continuous Export stops working after migrating Application Insights from Classic to workspace-based
rootCause: Continuous Export deprecated, not supported for workspace-based. Must disable before migration
solution: Replace with Diagnostic Settings (different schema, breaks Stream Analytics). Consider LA archive policy for cost-effective long-term storage
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Classic Application Insights retirement

symptom: Classic Application Insights stopped ingesting telemetry after June 2025, no new data collected
rootCause: Microsoft disabled ingestion for Classic AI on June 19, 2025. Auto-migration with managed workspaces completed globally by early June 2025
solution: Convert to workspace-based Application Insights. Existing data accessible per retention (90 days default). AMPLS resources migrated June 16-18
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Classic Application Insights retirement

symptom: Classic URL ping test retirement notice - need to migrate availability tests to standard tests before Sept 2026
rootCause: Classic URL ping tests retiring September 30, 2026. No automated migration confirmed yet
solution: Follow MS Learn availability-test-migration. Check pricing changes. Use PowerShell script to identify AI resources with web tests and check Kind property
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Classic availability test retirement

symptom: Internal (Microsoft) users see Failed to create API Key error in Azure Portal when trying to create a new Application Insights API key
rootCause: As part of SFI (Secure Future Initiative), Microsoft has blocked creation of new API keys by internal customers ahead of the public retirement. External customers can still create API keys. Existing keys continue to work until full retirement.
solution: API Keys are deprecated. Migrate to Microsoft Entra ID authentication for querying Application Insights data. For custom solutions (Function App, Web App) see Entra ID authentication guides. For Logic Apps use the Azure Monitor Logs connector.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Data Access API key retirement

symptom: Application Insights API key returns error when calling REST API on a workspace-based resource
rootCause: Backend Log Analytics workspace Access Control Mode is set to Require workspace permissions, which requires Azure AD auth tokens. API key (SAS key) authentication is blocked in this mode.
solution: Open the Properties pane of the backend LA workspace and change Access Control Mode to Use resource or workspace permissions. Alternatively migrate to Entra ID authentication instead of API keys.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Data Access API key retirement

symptom: Live Metrics (Quick Pulse) stops working after September 30 2025 for Application Insights resources still using API key authentication
rootCause: Microsoft is retiring API key authentication for Live Metrics on September 30 2025. After this date Live Metrics will not function unless the application has switched to Entra ID authentication.
solution: Switch from API key to Microsoft Entra ID authentication for Live Metrics before the retirement date. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/azure-ad-authentication and https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/live-stream#secure-the-control-channel
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Live Metrics API key retirement

symptom: Duplicate telemetry data appears after enabling Diagnostic Settings on a workspace-based Application Insights resource
rootCause: Diagnostic Settings for workspace-based Application Insights collects the same data that Application Insights itself already sends to the Log Analytics workspace. When the destination is the same workspace, all data is duplicated.
solution: Create the Diagnostic Setting to send data to a DIFFERENT Log Analytics workspace or another destination (Storage Account, Event Hub). Do not point Diagnostic Settings to the same workspace the Application Insights resource is based on.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Diagnostic settings/Duplicate data after enabling

symptom: Exported data schema changes when migrating from Continuous Export to Diagnostic Settings for Application Insights
rootCause: Diagnostic Settings uses a fundamentally different schema than Continuous Export by design. There is no workaround or configuration to preserve the old Continuous Export schema format.
solution: This is expected behavior. Adjust downstream data consumers and pipelines to handle the new Diagnostic Settings schema. No alternative schema format is available. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/export-telemetry#diagnostic-settings-based-export
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Diagnostic settings/Exported Data Schema Changed

symptom: Recently created telemetry records missing from expected Storage Account folder in Diagnostic Settings export, or records appear in unexpected folder paths
rootCause: Behavior change: Diagnostic Settings Export to Storage now writes telemetry based on received/ingestion time instead of log creation time (_timestamp_). Delayed log entries go to the ingestion-time folder, not the creation-time folder.
solution: Look for missing records in Storage folders within 48 hours of when the record was originally created. The newest folder always contains the most recently processed logs. Adjust downstream processing to account for ingestion-time-based folder paths.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Diagnostic settings/No data at export destination

symptom: No historical/pre-existing data appears at Diagnostic Settings export destination after enabling. Only newly ingested data is exported.
rootCause: Diagnostic Settings (like Continuous Export) does not support backfilling data ingested before enablement. Only data arriving after the setting is enabled will be exported.
solution: For backfilling critical data: (1) Use Log Analytics API to programmatically query and write data (has row/size limits). (2) Use Logic Apps with Export data from Log Analytics to Storage approach. (3) For large/high-profile customers, escalate to SRE team with TA/SME approval for one-time export (requires special approval).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Diagnostic settings/Recover or backfill exported data

symptom: Python Application Insights SDK based on OpenCensus is deprecated/retired (since Sept 30 2024). Telemetry still ingested but no support or fixes available.
rootCause: OpenCensus Python SDK was officially retired on September 30, 2024. OpenCensus was a predecessor of OpenTelemetry and its GitHub repositories are now closed. No further patches or support will be provided.
solution: Migrate to Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Python Distro. Follow official migration guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/opentelemetry-python-opencensus-migrate. Be aware of documented Changes and limitations during migration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Legacy SDK support

symptom: Logic Apps integration with Application Insights stops working; deprecated Application Insights connector fails after API key retirement
rootCause: Legacy Application Insights connector for Logic Apps relied on API Keys which have been retired. The old connector is deprecated and no longer functional.
solution: Use Azure Monitor Logs connector (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/connectors/azuremonitorlogs/) instead of the deprecated Application Insights connector.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Integration with Application Insights/Logic Apps

symptom: Power BI shows fewer rows than expected from Application Insights query; e.g. query returns 750K rows but only 500K appear in Power BI
rootCause: REST API has dual hard limits: maximum number of rows AND maximum total data size. Both limits apply independently.
solution: Query data in batches to work around API limits. See Azure Monitor Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/service-limits#la-query-api
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Integration with Application Insights/Power BI

symptom: API Key (SAS Key) authentication fails for workspace-based Application Insights REST API queries
rootCause: Backend Log Analytics workspace Access control mode set to Require workspace permissions blocks SAS Key/API Key authentication.
solution: Open Properties pane of backend LA Workspace. Change Access control mode to Use resource or workspace permissions. Generate and test with a new API key.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Integration with Application Insights/REST API access

symptom: REST API returns 499 response codes for Application Insights or Log Analytics draft API queries
rootCause: Client times out the request before the draft API returns a response. Caused by concurrent API calls from dashboards or Grafana (default 30s timeout).
solution: Extend client-side query timeout. For Grafana, increase default 30-second timeout. Reduce concurrent draft API calls.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Integration with Application Insights/REST API access

symptom: Application Insights client_IP field shows 0.0.0.0 or blank; no IP address collected in telemetry
rootCause: IP collection/unmasking is not enabled by default. Application Insights masks IP addresses to 0.0.0.0 unless explicitly configured.
solution: Enable IP address collection: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/ip-collection?tabs=net#storage-of-ip-address-data
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Client IP and geolocation

symptom: Application Insights SDK logs internal 503 ServiceUnavailable error codes in trace telemetry
rootCause: SDK temporarily unable to communicate with Application Insights ingestion service (transient connectivity issue).
solution: Informational only - no action required. SDK automatically retries per telemetry channel retry logic. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/telemetry-channels
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Crashes or slowness after enabling

symptom: Application Insights telemetry appears delayed; IngestionTime field shows large gap after customer recently changed retention settings on the table
rootCause: Changing retention settings on a Log Analytics table causes the IngestionTime field to be reset to the time of the retention change, resulting in falsely reported ingestion delays (Known Issue #61748)
solution: Verify if retention settings were recently changed. If so, the delay is cosmetic -- IngestionTime was reset at the time of retention change and will normalize for newly ingested data. No action needed beyond customer education.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Delayed telemetry

symptom: Application Insights shows exact duplicate telemetry records at high rates; all or most records appear twice in Log Analytics queries
rootCause: Diagnostic Settings enabled on the Application Insights resource configured to send allLogs to the backing Log Analytics workspace, causing telemetry to be written twice -- once via normal SDK ingestion and once via Diagnostic Settings export
solution: Run Telemetry Flow Diagnostic Script (Test-AppInsightsTelemetryFlow.ps1) to confirm. Remove or modify the Diagnostic Settings on the Application Insights resource to stop exporting allLogs to the same LA workspace. Add case number to Known Issue #22050.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Duplicate telemetry

symptom: Python application sends duplicate telemetry to Application Insights
rootCause: Multiple Application Insights SDKs/distros enabled simultaneously -- either OpenCensus SDK alongside OpenTelemetry Distro, or OpenTelemetry Exporter alongside OpenTelemetry Distro
solution: Ensure only one SDK/Distro is active at any time. Disable the deprecated OpenCensus SDK if using OpenTelemetry Distro. Remove the standalone OpenTelemetry Exporter if using the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Distro.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Duplicate telemetry

symptom: Custom metrics stop reporting or are dropped in Application Insights; metrics work initially then stop after some time
rootCause: Exceeded one of four custom metric limits: 50,000 active time series, 64KB combined UTF-8 metric names, 10 dimension keys per metric, or 256 character length for namespaces/names/dimension keys/values
solution: Review metric cardinality and reduce unique dimension value combinations to stay under 50K active time series. Check Known Issue #27792. Reduce dimension count to <= 10 per metric.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Incorrect telemetry values (fields, metrics, counters)

symptom: cloud_RoleName and cloud_RoleInstance fields are empty or missing in Application Insights telemetry for applications running outside standard Azure PaaS services
rootCause: Application Insights SDKs auto-populate cloud_RoleName only for known Azure resource types (App Services, Functions). Custom or external/on-premises environments do not provide this metadata automatically.
solution: For OpenTelemetry SDKs, set OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES env var: service.name=custom-role-name,service.instance.id=custom-role-instance. For classic SDKs, implement a custom TelemetryInitializer to set cloud role name.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Incorrect telemetry values (fields, metrics, counters)

symptom: Custom Track calls (TrackEvent, TrackTrace, TrackMetric) lost or not appearing in Application Insights after auto-instrumentation enabled on App Service
rootCause: Auto-instrumentation with XDT_MicrosoftApplicationInsights_PreemptSdk=true preempts the manual SDK, silently dropping custom telemetry calls made via the classic API
solution: Check App Settings for XDT_MicrosoftApplicationInsights_PreemptSdk. If manual SDK with custom Track calls is needed, disable auto-instrumentation or set PreemptSdk to false.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Missing telemetry types

symptom: Hundreds or thousands of telemetry items share the same operation ID in Application Insights distributed tracing
rootCause: Client application (especially SPAs) sends multiple HTTP requests to backend with the same traceparent/Request-Id correlation header, causing all requests to inherit one shared operation ID
solution: Identify the calling client. For SPAs, enable enableAutoRouteTracking in JavaScript SDK config. For other clients, ensure each HTTP request uses a distinct traceparent header value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Distributed tracing or correlation issues

symptom: Intermittent or partial telemetry loss; some telemetry fields appear stripped, modified, or record counts do not match expected
rootCause: Data Collection Rules (DCRs) with Workspace Transforms configured on the Log Analytics workspace reshape, filter, or drop fields/records before data reaches the destination tables
solution: Run Telemetry Flow Diagnostic Script to detect active DCRs. Review DCR transformation KQL queries for unintended filtering. See wiki: Check for Data Collection Rules (DCRs) and Ingestion Time Transformations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Intermittent telemetry

symptom: Application Insights Profiler not available or not functioning on Linux-based App Service web apps
rootCause: App Service Profiler feature is only supported on Windows-based web apps. Linux-based apps require the NuGet package Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Profiler.AspNetCore instead.
solution: For Linux-based App Service apps, enable Profiler using the NuGet package Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Profiler.AspNetCore. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/profiler/profiler-aspnetcore-linux
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Profiler or Snapshot Debugger/Profiler - Configuration or performance impact

symptom: Application performance degradation or unexpected autoscale events suspected to be caused by Application Insights Profiler
rootCause: Profiler runs approximately 2 minutes per hour and adds 5-15% CPU overhead during execution. On instances already near autoscale CPU thresholds, this additional overhead can trigger scale-out actions.
solution: Verify if performance issue timing correlates with Profiler execution using HOWTO: Determine if Profiler is running. If confirmed, temporarily disable Profiler to validate. Review autoscale threshold settings. Default overhead is expected behavior (5-15% CPU for ~2 min/hour).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Profiler or Snapshot Debugger/Profiler - Configuration or performance impact

symptom: Application Insights Profiler traces not collected on App Service; Profiler appears enabled but no traces appear
rootCause: App Service Always On feature is not enabled. Without Always On, the application becomes idle, causing Profiler to timeout and fail to complete profiling sessions.
solution: Enable Always On in App Service Configuration > General settings. Always On is required for Profiler to run properly and avoid timeout issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Profiler or Snapshot Debugger/Profiler - Traces are not collected

symptom: Application Insights Profiler not functioning on containers or IaaS VMs despite NuGet package being installed
rootCause: Environment variable COMPlus_EnableDiagnostics is set to 0, disabling .NET diagnostics including Profiler. PaaS environments set this to 1 by default, but IaaS/container environments may not.
solution: Set environment variable COMPlus_EnableDiagnostics=1 in the container or VM configuration. Required for Profiler to function in non-PaaS environments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Profiler or Snapshot Debugger/Profiler - Traces are not collected

symptom: Application Insights Profiler fails to start with no diagnostic logs available for troubleshooting
rootCause: NuGet package Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Profiler.AspNetCore version is below 2.7.3, which lacks additional startup logging needed to diagnose Profiler startup failures.
solution: Upgrade Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Profiler.AspNetCore NuGet package to at least version 2.7.3 for additional Profiler startup logging.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Profiler or Snapshot Debugger/Profiler - Traces are not collected

symptom: Customer requests Application Insights Profiler support for Azure Functions app
rootCause: Profiler for Azure Functions is NOT in Azure Monitor support boundary.
solution: Per support boundaries, this scenario is not supported by Azure Monitor. Refer to https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/profiler/profiler-azure-functions. Redirect to Azure Functions support if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Profiler or Snapshot Debugger/Profiler - Traces are not collected

symptom: Snapshot Debugger enabled but no snapshots appearing; customer expects immediate snapshot collection
rootCause: Snapshot Debugger requires at least 2 exceptions of the same type to occur before the first snapshot is triggered (by design).
solution: Explain that at least 2 exceptions of same type must occur before first snapshot triggers. Verify Snapshot Debugger is enabled, then wait for sufficient exceptions. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/snapshot-debugger/snapshot-debugger#snapshot-debugger-process
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Profiler or Snapshot Debugger/Snapshot Debugger - Snapshots not collected

symptom: Customer inquires about billing charges or costs associated with Snapshot Debugger
solution: No subscription billing charges for Snapshot Debugger. No charges for ingesting or storing dump files. Snapshots are stored separately from Application Insights telemetry at no cost.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Profiler or Snapshot Debugger/Snapshot Debugger - Configuration or performance impact

symptom: Customer requests help analyzing or resolving the exception shown in Snapshot Debugger debug snapshot
rootCause: Resolving exceptions from snapshot data goes beyond Application Insights support scope and expertise.
solution: Redirect to team supporting the API or framework involved in the exception. See Application Insights support boundaries documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Profiler or Snapshot Debugger/Snapshot Debugger - Analyzing snapshots

symptom: Alert or Smart Detection case created under Application Insights SAP instead of Alerts and Action Groups SAP
rootCause: Alert and Smart Detection features are supported by the Alerts and Action Groups team, not AppInsights. The SAP exists under AppInsights only for customer discoverability.
solution: Change SAP to Azure > Alerts and Action Groups > appropriate sub-topic. For Smart Detection: Azure > Alerts and Action Groups > Issues with smart detections.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Smart detection and alerts/Alerts

symptom: Cannot delete Failure Anomalies (Smart Detection) alert rule from Azure Portal; or need to recreate a deleted Smart Detection alert rule
rootCause: No UI in Azure Portal to delete Smart Detection alert rules.
solution: To delete: use az resource delete CLI (https://docs.microsoft.com/cli/azure/resource#az-resource-delete). Better option: disable via Portal (AppInsights > Smart Detection > Settings). To recreate: use ARM template per https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/proactive-arm-config#failure-anomalies-alert-rule
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Smart detection and alerts/Smart Detection

symptom: Customer has issues using Azure SDK for CRUD operations against Azure Monitor resources or needs SDK usage guidance
rootCause: Azure Monitor team supports REST API issues but not Azure SDK assistance. SDK issues handled by MSaaS Developer App and Services Dev team.
solution: For SDK CRUD failures: verify REST API works correctly first, then collaborate with MSaaS Developer App and Services Dev queue. For SDK usage/code questions: redirect to MSaaS Developer team. Include HTTP requests/responses with headers when collaborating.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Azure Monitor/Collaboration Guides/Collaborating with Azure SDK developer support

symptom: Customer has Azure Monitor for SAP Solutions issues: deployment failures, missing monitoring data, marketplace issues
rootCause: Azure Monitor for SAP Solutions is supported by Azure IaaS Incubation team (Azure Specialized queue), not Azure Monitor team.
solution: Redirect or collaborate via Service Desk to Azure IaaS Incubation team. Use appropriate SAP under SAP Solutions topics. Route to Azure Specialized queue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Azure Monitor/Collaboration Guides/Collaboration Guide: Azure Monitor for SAP Solutions

symptom: Customer did not choose to share diagnostic information when creating Azure support request via portal. ASC shows limited data making it difficult to troubleshoot issues.
rootCause: Customer selected No to the advanced diagnostic information collection question when creating the SR, or the SR was created outside Azure portal which does not support diagnostic info sharing.
solution: Use the email template from Evergreen (topic/507eab3d-6d19-1428-8e10-6c2d039e4eb5) to request the customer to change their diagnostic sharing preference. If SR was created outside Azure portal, request customer to create a new SR via Azure portal and close the existing one as duplicate. Do not pressure the customer if they decline. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-portal/supportability/how-to-manage-azure-support-request#allow-collection-of-advanced-diagnostic-information
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Azure Monitor/How-To/Azure Support Center/How to use Azure Support Center when customer did not choose to share diagnostic information

symptom: Azure Support Center (ASC) displays an error or is not working as expected during case troubleshooting.
rootCause: Bug or issue in Azure Support Center platform affecting displayed data or functionality.
solution: Click the Frowny Face icon on the affected tile to report the problem (creates a work item/bug to ASC team). If an error is displayed, click Show Details. Navigate to the created repair item to add relevant links, screenshots, and attachments. An email with a link to the repair item will be sent. Contact SME/TA/EEE if unsure. Reference: https://azsupportdocs.azurewebsites.net/asc/articles/FeedbackSupport.html
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Azure Monitor/How-To/Azure Support Center/How to provide feedback when Azure Support Center is not working as excepted

symptom: Container Insights with Managed Identity authentication shows no data in Log Analytics; checking DCR associations reveals no DCR is associated with the AKS cluster
rootCause: DCR Association is missing while the Container Insights agent is configured to use Managed Identity authentication. Without a DCR, the agent has no configuration to determine what data to collect and where to send it.
solution: Disable Container Insights on the AKS cluster and re-onboard to create proper DCR and DCR Association. Verify via Azure Portal (Monitor > Data Collection Rules > Resources) or Azure Support Center Resource Explorer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/How-To/Check DCR and DCR Association for AKS Cluster

symptom: Container Insights with Managed Identity on a Private AKS cluster has DCR and DCR Association in place but still no data flowing to Log Analytics
rootCause: Private Link is not properly configured for the private AKS cluster. The agent cannot reach the data ingestion endpoints through the private network without proper AMPLS/Private Link setup.
solution: Verify and configure Private Link setup for the private AKS cluster following the documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/containers/container-insights-private-link
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/How-To/Check DCR and DCR Association for AKS Cluster

symptom: Container Insights agent not sending data; curl to monitoring endpoints (*.ods.opinsights.azure.com, *.monitoring.azure.com) returns SSL certificate error (error 60) or openssl shows non-Microsoft certificate
rootCause: Network appliance or proxy is performing HTTP/SSL inspection on Container Insights agent traffic, replacing the Microsoft SSL certificate with its own, causing the agent to reject the connection.
solution: Configure network firewall/proxy to exempt Container Insights endpoints from HTTP/SSL inspection. Required endpoints: *.ods.opinsights.azure.com:443, *.oms.opinsights.azure.com:443, dc.services.visualstudio.com:443, *.monitoring.azure.com:443, login.microsoftonline.com:443. For Managed Identity also: global.handler.control.monitor.azure.com:443, <region>.handler.control.monitor.azure.com:443, <region>.ingest.monitor.azure.com:443.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/How-To/Check Network Connectivity for Container Insights

symptom: Container Insights agent processes (fluentd, fluent-bit, or mdsd) not running on ama-logs pod; ps aux | grep shows no matching processes; no data being collected from the AKS cluster
rootCause: One or more Container Insights agent component processes (fluentd, fluent-bit, mdsd) crashed or failed to start within the ama-logs pod.
solution: Delete the affected ama-logs pod to trigger recreation: kubectl delete pod ama-logs-xxxxx -n kube-system. After recreation, verify processes are running with: kubectl exec -it <pod> -c ama-logs -n kube-system -- ps aux | grep -E 'mdsd|fluent'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/How-To/Check if Services are Running on the Cluster (fluentd, fluent-bit, mdsd)

symptom: "NO DATA" displayed on Azure Managed Grafana dashboard using Managed Prometheus (Azure Monitor Workspace) data source; no data populates in panels
rootCause: Azure Monitor Workspace (AMW) has public network access disabled, blocking Grafana from querying metrics via public endpoint
solution: Enable private access via Private Endpoint between Grafana and AMW, or change AMW network settings to allow public access
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Azure Monitor dashboards with Grafana (preview)/Issues using Azure Monitor dashboards with Grafana (preview)

symptom: "NO DATA" on Azure Managed Grafana dashboard; Managed Prometheus data source dropdown does not populate resources
rootCause: Browser ad blocker extension blocks API calls from Grafana UI to Azure Monitor Workspace endpoints
solution: Pause or disable the ad blocker extension in the browser, then refresh the Grafana dashboard to reload data
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Azure Monitor dashboards with Grafana (preview)/Issues using Azure Monitor dashboards with Grafana (preview)

symptom: "NO DATA" on Azure Managed Grafana dashboard with Managed Prometheus; AMW resource not appearing in dropdown selection
rootCause: The Azure Monitor Workspace subscription is not included in the user default subscriptions list in Azure portal, causing resource to be filtered out
solution: Add the AMW subscription to the default subscriptions list in Azure portal (Settings > Default subscriptions filter), then refresh the Grafana dashboard
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Azure Monitor dashboards with Grafana (preview)/Issues using Azure Monitor dashboards with Grafana (preview)

symptom: Azure Monitor data source 'Save & Test' fails in Azure Managed Grafana with authentication or permission error
rootCause: The Managed Identity (or App Registration) used by Grafana does not have the required permissions (Monitoring Reader) on the target subscription
solution: Assign Monitoring Reader role to the Grafana Managed Identity at subscription level for the data source subscription; verify via Azure Portal IAM blade, then retry Save & Test
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Concepts/Data Sources | Azure Monitor

symptom: Unable to connect Azure Managed Grafana to external data source (Prometheus, on-prem DB, ADX cluster) blocked by destination firewall
rootCause: Grafana outbound IP is dynamic by default and cannot be whitelisted in the destination resource firewall rules
solution: Enable the Deterministic Outbound IP feature on the Azure Managed Grafana instance to get a static IP, then whitelist that IP in the destination resource firewall. For supported resources, consider using Managed Private Endpoint (preview) for private connectivity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Concepts/Networking | Azure Managed Grafana

symptom: Container Insights ama-logs pods experiencing high CPU or memory usage exceeding default resource limits, causing OOMKill restarts and potential log collection gaps
rootCause: Current workload (log volume, InsightsMetrics scale) exceeded default memory/CPU limits for ama-logs pods (Linux daemonset: CPU 1/Memory 1Gi; replicaset: CPU 1/Memory 1.5Gi; Windows: CPU 2/Memory 2Gi)
solution: Query Log Analytics to verify CPU/memory usage against default limits using Perf table (memoryRssBytes, cpuUsageNanoCores). If exceeding limits, submit ICM to Container Insights PG requesting limit increase with cluster resource ID and suggested values. Confirm with customer before increasing as it takes more node resources. For high log scale, consider enabling High Scale Logs Collection preview.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Agent Management TSGs/TSG High CPU or Memory in ama-logs Pods

symptom: Container Insights agent cannot change Log Analytics Workspace because the previously connected workspace has been deleted; unable to remove or reconfigure Container Insights monitoring
rootCause: When the Log Analytics Workspace is deleted, the Container Insights agent still references it via the DCR resource ID, preventing workspace change through normal UI flow
solution: Delete the DCR associated with the cluster (named MSCI-{region}-{clusterName}) from Data Collection Rules blade: 1) Remove resource associations from Resources tab, 2) Delete the DCR itself, 3) Navigate to AKS cluster > Insights > Monitoring Settings > Advanced settings to select a new/existing workspace, 4) Click Configure to re-onboard
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Agent Management TSGs/TSG Unable to Change Log Analytics Workspace for Container Insights Agent After Deletion

symptom: Fluent-bit built-in multiline Java parser does not support custom exceptions in Container Insights multiline logging feature; custom Java exception stack traces not stitched into single log entry
rootCause: Known limitation - Fluent-bit built-in multiline Java parser only supports standard Java exceptions, not custom exception types
solution: This is a known limitation (by design). Custom Java exceptions are not supported by the Fluent-bit multiline parser. File ICM to Container Insights PG if customer has strong business need. Ref ICM 560446623.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Agent Management TSGs/TSG Multiline Logging Issues

symptom: Container Insights multiline logging does not stitch arbitrary log lines together; only exception call stack traces for supported languages (.NET, Go, Python, Java) are supported
rootCause: By design - multiline logging feature only stitches exception call stack traces for supported languages, not arbitrary multi-line log entries
solution: This is by design. Multiline logging only supports stitching exception call stacks for .NET, Go, Python, and Java. Arbitrary log lines are not stitched. Max supported log message with this feature is up to 64KB (default runtime truncation is 16KB). Ref ICM 565341097.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Agent Management TSGs/TSG Multiline Logging Issues

symptom: Associating more than 30 ContainerLogV2 DCRs to an AKS cluster in Container Insights multi-tenancy logging causes data loss
rootCause: Known limitation in Container Insights multi-tenancy logging feature - exceeding 30 ContainerLogV2 DCR associations on a single cluster leads to data loss
solution: Keep the number of ContainerLogV2 DCR associations per AKS cluster at or below 30. If more DCRs are needed, consider consolidating namespace filtering rules into fewer DCRs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Container Insights Configuration TSGs/TSG Container Insights Multi-Tenancy Logging Issues

symptom: Container Insights identified as root cause for high data ingestion into Log Analytics Workspace; customer wants to reduce the amount of data collected by Container Insights agent
rootCause: By default Container Insights collects all logs (stdout, stderr, environment variables) from all namespaces, which can result in very high data volume especially in clusters with verbose logging workloads
solution: Multiple mitigation options: 1) Use cost optimization feature (requires Managed Identity); 2) Disable stdout/stderr collection via ConfigMap cluster-wide or per namespace; 3) Disable environment variable collection via ConfigMap; 4) Auto cleanup completed jobs with TTL in job definition; 5) Move to Azure Monitor managed service for Prometheus and/or basic logs tier for ContainerLogV2. Identify top data sources using LA queries on ContainerLogV2 by namespace and LogSource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/High Data Ingestion or Cost TSGs/TSG Container Insights Sending Too Much Data to Workspace

symptom: Container Insights Insights page in Azure Portal shows no data but Log Analytics workspace has the data when queried directly
rootCause: Network isolation settings on the Log Analytics Workspace have public access for querying set to disabled, preventing the Azure Portal Insights UI from retrieving data
solution: Check the network isolation settings on the Log Analytics Workspace and enable public access for querying. The Insights portal UI requires public query access to display data.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Container Insights Configuration TSGs/TSG Container Insights

symptom: Container Insights logs appear intermittently or stop being collected; some time periods have data and others do not in Log Analytics workspace
rootCause: Log Analytics Workspace has a daily cap (daily ingestion limit) configured; once the cap is reached, all subsequent data is dropped until the next day
solution: Run query: _LogOperation | where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d) | search 'OverQuota' to confirm daily cap is being hit. If results returned, customer needs to either remove the daily cap or raise it to accommodate Container Insights data volume. Also check Operation table for full visibility.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Container Insights Configuration TSGs/TSG Container Insights

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana SMTP email notification setup fails in Microsoft internal (1P) tenants because SFI restrictions prevent creating client secrets on App Registrations, which are required for Azure Communication Services SMTP authentication
rootCause: SFI (Secure Future Initiative) restrictions block secret creation on Entra ID App Registrations in MS tenants. The SMTP setup with Azure Communication Services requires an App Registration client secret for authentication.
solution: This is a known limitation for MS internal tenants. SMTP email notifications via Azure Communication Services cannot be configured in MS tenants due to SFI restrictions. Consider alternative notification methods (webhook, Teams) or use a non-MS tenant for testing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/How-To/Setting up and testing SMTP with Azure Managed Grafana

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU resource shows Evicted status and the Grafana instance is inaccessible
rootCause: Essential SKU Azure Managed Grafana instances are hosted on SPOT VMs with no SLA. When Azure reclaims the SPOT VM capacity, the instance gets evicted.
solution: Wait for the instance to come back (can take 30 minutes up to 24 hours based on excess VM capacity), create a new Azure Managed Grafana resource in a different region, or upgrade to Standard SKU for SLA-backed availability.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Support Boundaries

symptom: User with proper permissions logs into Azure Managed Grafana endpoint but only sees a Welcome to Grafana Enterprise splash screen instead of the normal Grafana UI
rootCause: Instance requires patching by the Azure Managed Grafana PG team. This is not resolvable by CSS.
solution: Submit an ICM immediately to Azure Managed Grafana PG with all relevant info. The instance needs to be patched to restore normal operation. CSS cannot resolve this issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Support Boundaries

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana with private access enabled cannot connect to services (e.g. Prometheus, MySQL) running on Azure VMs in the same VNET. Data source connection test fails even though VMs are accessible from other VMs in the VNET.
rootCause: AMG private access only establishes a private endpoint for browser-to-Grafana connectivity. It does not enable Grafana-to-VM connectivity directly. VMs are not listed as supported resources for Managed Private Endpoint.
solution: Create an Internal Load Balancer in the VM VNET with Inbound NAT rules pointing to each VM service port. Create a Private Link Service on top of the LB. Then create a Managed Private Endpoint in AMG targeting the Private Link Service. Approve the connection manually. Use the Private Link Service private IP as the data source host in Grafana.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Learning Resources/Lab | Connecting services running on Azure VMs as a Grafana data source

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana cannot connect to Azure Database for MySQL/PostgreSQL Flexible Server with public network access disabled. Managed Private Endpoint does not list MySQL/PostgreSQL as supported resources.
rootCause: MySQL and PostgreSQL Flexible Servers are not supported as direct Managed Private Endpoint targets in Azure Managed Grafana. Deterministic outbound IPs method also fails when public access is disabled on the database.
solution: Use Azure Firewall DNAT approach: Create an Azure Firewall in the database VNET with AzureFirewallSubnet. Create a Route Table associated with the database subnet routing to the firewall. Create a NAT rule in the firewall translating the firewall public IP to the database private IP on the service port (3306 for MySQL, 5432 for PostgreSQL). Use the firewall public IP as the Grafana data source host.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Learning Resources/Lab | Connecting Private Databases (MySQL Flexible Server and PostgreSQL Flexible Server) to Azure Managed Grafana

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana cannot connect to MySQL or PostgreSQL databases running inside an AKS cluster as data sources
rootCause: Services running inside AKS pods are not directly accessible from Azure Managed Grafana. A Private Link Service with an internal load balancer is needed to expose the database ports.
solution: Deploy a Kubernetes Service with type LoadBalancer and azure-pls annotations (azure-load-balancer-internal=true, azure-pls-create=true) targeting the database pod (port 3306 for MySQL, 5432 for PostgreSQL). This creates an Internal LB and a Private Link Service automatically. Create a Managed Private Endpoint in AMG targeting the Private Link Service. Approve the connection and use the Private Link Service private IP as the Grafana data source host.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Learning Resources/Lab | Connecting Databases (MySQL and PostgreSQL) running on an AKS cluster to Azure Managed Grafana

symptom: Container Insights data missing from ContainerLog/ContainerLogV2 or KubeMonAgentEvents tables collected by Fluent-Bit. Fluent-bit log at /var/opt/microsoft/docker-cimprov/log/fluent-bit.log shows mem buf overlimit messages.
rootCause: Fluent-Bit memory buffer limit is too low for the volume of logs being generated on the node, causing data to be dropped.
solution: Increase Fluent-Bit memory buffer limit via ConfigMap (agent_settings.fbit_config section): set tail_mem_buf_limit_megabytes, tail_buf_chunksize_megabytes, tail_buf_maxsize_megabytes. Or migrate to High Scale logs collection (Preview). If pods OOMKilled, also increase memory limits per TSG High CPU or Memory in ama-logs Pods.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Collection Issues With Data From fluent-bit

symptom: ama-logs pods in CrashLoopBackOff with no ContainerLogV2 logs ingested. Fluent-bit logs show Too many open files errors. File descriptor exhaustion prevents fluent-bit from tailing container log files.
rootCause: Node file descriptor limit (fs.file-max or inotify) is too low for the number of container log files being tailed by fluent-bit, causing process crash.
solution: For AKS clusters: create new node pool with custom kernel parameters to increase fs.file-max. For Arc-enabled K8s: directly increase limits via sysctl (fs.inotify.max_user_instances=8192, fs.inotify.max_user_watches=524288) and persist in /etc/sysctl.conf. Temporary workaround: reduce log files to tail or use tail_ignore_older in ConfigMap.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Collection Issues With Data From fluent-bit

symptom: Annotation fluentbit.io/exclude true is present on pods but Container Insights data is still being collected and appearing in Log Analytics Workspace. No ConfigMap setting for filter_using_annotations is active.
rootCause: Container Insights agent does not follow Fluent Bit annotations by default. Annotation-based filtering must be explicitly enabled via ConfigMap setting.
solution: Enable annotation filtering in ConfigMap: set [log_collection_settings.filter_using_annotations] enabled = true. Apply ConfigMap via kubectl. After applying, ama-logs pods restart automatically. Verify excluded pods no longer send data. Manual restart: kubectl delete pod -n kube-system -l rsName=ama-logs-rs and kubectl delete pod -n kube-system -l component=ama-logs-agent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Collection Issues With Data From fluent-bit

symptom: Missing data or gaps in KubePodInventory, KubeNodeInventory, KubeEvents, ContainerNodeInventory, KubePVInventory, or KubeServices tables on large AKS clusters with high pod/node count.
rootCause: Known issue in the ruby plugin (Fluentd in ama-logs-rs) parsing large JSON payloads. Default PODS_CHUNK_SIZE is 1000, which is too large for clusters with many pods/nodes, causing parsing failures and data gaps.
solution: Configure smaller PODS_CHUNK_SIZE in container-azm-ms-agentconfig ConfigMap: download template from GitHub, uncomment agent_settings.chunk_config and PODS_CHUNK_SIZE lines, apply via kubectl. If still seeing gaps after reducing chunk size, enable the large cluster toggle. Monitor for remaining data gaps after applying.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Missing Data in Kube Tables on Large Clusters

symptom: Missing logs, InsightsMetrics, and Prometheus metrics in Container Insights for AKS cluster behind a firewall. Error messages in ama-logs-daemonset-mdsd (appinsights_err.log, mdsd.err, mdsd.info) and ama-metrics prometheus-collector show connection refused or transport dial errors to Azure Monitor endpoints.
rootCause: Firewall rules blocking outbound connections from AKS cluster to required Azure Monitor endpoints. Container Insights agent requires outbound access to specific endpoints for data ingestion.
solution: Add firewall rules (Azure Firewall Application Rule Collection or on-premise) to allow connections to required Azure Monitor endpoints per Network firewall requirements docs. Collect agent logs using troubleshooting script to identify specific blocked endpoints. Check describe_ama-logs-xxxx.txt for container status errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Missing Logs and Metrics in Container Insights and Prometheus - Firewall rules

symptom: Container Insights data missing on AKS cluster configured with HTTP Proxy. ama-logs mdsd.err shows proxy connection failures to Azure Monitor endpoints.
rootCause: HTTP Proxy configuration on AKS cluster interfering with Container Insights agent (MDSD) communication to Azure Monitor endpoints. Proxy settings are applied to agent traffic, blocking or corrupting connections.
solution: Apply ConfigMap for CI Agent to ignore proxy configuration: download container-azm-ms-agentconfig.yaml from GitHub, uncomment the proxy ignore settings section, apply via kubectl. Verify proxy config from ASC: AKS cluster > HTTP Proxy Config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Missing Logs and Metrics in Container Insights and Prometheus - Firewall rules

symptom: Missing logs and metrics in Container Insights and Prometheus on AKS cluster using Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS). MDSD error: Data collection endpoint must be used to access configuration over private link (ErrorCode:-2146172665). OTLP exporter shows connection refused to 127.0.0.1:55680.
rootCause: AMPLS misconfiguration for Container Insights and Managed Prometheus - missing or incorrect Data Collection Endpoint (DCE) configuration, Private Link not properly set up for both Container Insights ingestion and Prometheus metrics collection.
solution: Reconfigure AMPLS: create private link connection through Azure portal, ensure DCE is properly configured for private link. Set up Private Link for data ingestion for Managed Prometheus and Azure Monitor workspace. Check ama-logs container logs (kubectl logs) and ama-metrics prometheus-collector container (mdsd.err at /opt/microsoft/linuxmonagent/mdsd.err) for specific error messages.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Missing Logs and Metrics in Container Insights and Prometheus using AMPLS

symptom: Intermittent or missing Container Insights data in Log Analytics workspace. Customer reports data appearing and disappearing. _LogOperation table shows OverQuota entries indicating daily cap reached.
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace daily cap is being reached, causing all subsequent data ingestion to be dropped until the cap resets. This affects all data sources including Container Insights.
solution: Query: _LogOperation | where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d) | search OverQuota to confirm. Also check Operation table for full visibility. Resolution: remove or raise the daily cap on the Log Analytics workspace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG No Data in Log Analytics and Missing Logs in Container Insights

symptom: IIS logs not appearing in ContainerLogV2 in Log Analytics for IIS deployed in AKS cluster. All other container logs are collected normally. IIS writes W3C access logs to files inside container, not to STDOUT.
rootCause: IIS writes W3C access logs to files inside the container (e.g. C:\\inetpub\\logs\\LogFiles), not to STDOUT/STDERR. Container Insights only collects STDOUT and STDERR streams stored in ContainerLogV2. This is by-design Kubernetes behavior.
solution: Use Microsoft-supported LogMonitor (Windows Container Tools) to forward IIS W3C logs, EventLog, and ETW to STDOUT. Test-only workaround: add PowerShell to container spec to tail IIS log files to STDOUT. No AKS/ConfigMap/DCR setting can make Container Insights read IIS log files from inside a container.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Missing IIS Container Logs in Log Analytics

symptom: Syslog data not appearing in Log Analytics after enabling syslog collection in Container Insights for AKS cluster using Cilium enterprise networking. HostPort on port 28330 not bound.
rootCause: HostPort functionality is not enabled by default when using Cilium enterprise for AKS networking. Syslog collection requires HostPort to be enabled on port 28330 for syslog events to be received by the agent.
solution: Verify HostPort status: exec into ama-logs pod, run netstat -a | grep 28330 and cat /var/log/syslog | grep 28330. If netstat returns empty, HostPort is disabled. Involve AKS team or customer networking team to enable HostPort.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Syslog Collection Issues

symptom: Syslog data not appearing in Log Analytics after enabling syslog collection in Container Insights for AKS. DCR missing syslog data source, or rsyslog config file /etc/rsyslog.d/70-rsyslog-forward-mdsd-ci.conf missing on AKS node.
rootCause: Syslog onboarding did not complete successfully, or DCR was modified after onboarding removing syslog as a data source. Missing rsyslog forwarding configuration prevents syslog events from reaching the Container Insights agent.
solution: 1) Verify DCR has syslog data source and is associated with cluster. 2) Check parsed DCR configs under /etc/mdsd.d/config-cache/configchunks in ama-logs DaemonSet pods. 3) Verify rsyslog config at /etc/rsyslog.d/70-rsyslog-forward-mdsd-ci.conf on AKS nodes (debug via kubectl debug node). 4) Check ci-syslog-watcher.path is active. 5) Ensure AKS Linux node image meets minimum requirements and CI Agent >= ciprod12032022-c9f3dc30. Re-onboard if missing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Syslog Collection Issues

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana dashboards or alerts return HTTP 529 errors from Azure Monitor Metrics API. In Grafana Alerting, appears as DatasourceNoData alert with 529 error payload.
rootCause: HTTP 529 is a backend throttling error indicating the Metrics API server is busy (different from 429 per-customer throttling). The REST API query during alert evaluation or dashboard refresh fails to retrieve data.
solution: For alerting: modify alert configuration to show state as OK when no data is returned. Recommended: create equivalent alert in Azure Monitor instead of Grafana alerting. For dashboards: wait and retry refresh.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Managed Grafana Alerts and Dashboards Returning 529 Errors

symptom: Grafana dashboard panels querying Log Analytics produce HTTP 429 throttling errors. Panels fail to load data due to too many concurrent queries from the Managed Identity or App Registration.
rootCause: All Grafana dashboard queries run under a single identity (Managed Identity or App Registration), which can exceed Log Analytics concurrent query limits (max 5 concurrent queries per user). Long-running or complex queries and large time ranges exacerbate the issue.
solution: 1) Enable query caching in data source settings (set Min Interval). 2) Reduce dashboard time range. 3) Simplify queries or reduce panel count per dashboard. 4) Verify via Kusto query against armprodgbl cluster for 429 status codes on MICROSOFT.DASHBOARD targetResourceProvider. 5) PG is working on long-term mitigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Managed Grafana Dashboard Errors with 429 Throttling Error

symptom: When creating a new Azure Managed Grafana private endpoint, the SSO private DNS zone record (sso.weu) gets overwritten with the new instance IP address, causing concern about SSO access for existing Grafana instances.
rootCause: The SSO endpoint (e.g. sso.weu.privatelink.grafana.azure.com) is a global/regional login endpoint shared by all Managed Grafana instances in the same region. It does not distinguish between instances. The overwrite is expected behavior and does not break SSO for existing instances.
solution: No action needed for the overwrite itself - SSO traffic works regardless of which PE IP the SSO record points to. Caveat: if the PE whose IP is in the SSO record is deleted, all SSO records are removed. Manually re-add the sso record pointing to another PE IP. For isolated VNETs, disable Private DNS integration and create a CNAME: sso.<region> -> <region>.rp-prod.grafana.azure.com.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Multiple Managed Grafana instances sharing the same SSO private DNS zone record

symptom: After deleting a Managed Grafana private endpoint, other Grafana instances sharing the same private DNS zone lose SSO login capability. The sso.<region> DNS record disappears.
rootCause: When a private endpoint with DNS integration is deleted, Azure automatically removes all associated DNS records including the shared SSO record. Other Grafana instances depending on this SSO record lose their login endpoint.
solution: Manually re-add the sso.<region> A record in the private DNS zone pointing to another existing PE private IP. Alternatively, use CNAME record: sso.<region> -> <region>.rp-prod.grafana.azure.com to route SSO through the public endpoint (works even with public access disabled via AzureManagedGrafana service tag).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Multiple Managed Grafana instances sharing the same SSO private DNS zone record

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana plugin management feature installs an older version of a plugin than expected, or customer requests a new plugin that is not available in the plugin management catalog.
rootCause: By design, the Managed Grafana PG vets each plugin through a testing process before allowing it. Plugin versions available through plugin management may lag behind the latest upstream release.
solution: Submit an ICM to the Grafana PG with: plugin name, currently installed version, desired version (or new plugin details for new requests). The PG testing/approval process takes approximately 3-4 weeks. Set customer expectations accordingly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Plugin Management Feature Installing Old Version of Plugins or Requests for new Plugins

symptom: Customer accidentally deleted their Azure Managed Grafana resource and wants to restore it. The Grafana instance and all its dashboards/data are gone.
rootCause: Grafana resource was deleted (accidentally or intentionally). ARM does not support direct restore of Grafana resources.
solution: 1) Verify deletion within last 30 days via Kusto query on armprodgbl (filter httpMethod DELETE on resource URI). 2) Have customer create NEW Grafana instance in SAME REGION. 3) Submit ICM to Grafana PG requesting restore of deleted instance contents into new instance. Prevention: use az grafana backup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Restore a Grafana Resource or Dashboard

symptom: Customer accidentally deleted a Grafana dashboard within their Azure Managed Grafana instance and wants to restore it.
rootCause: Grafana does not natively support restoring deleted dashboards (per GitHub issue #9235). Dashboard deletion is permanent.
solution: If deleted within last 30 days: submit ICM to Grafana PG requesting the dashboard JSON schema. Customer can import the JSON to recreate the dashboard. Note: if full Grafana resource is restored, only data is restored not dashboards - but PG may provide JSON schema. Prevention: use az grafana backup regularly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Restore a Grafana Resource or Dashboard

symptom: Microsoft Entra Team Sync settings operation for Azure Managed Grafana fails. Error appears when attempting to sync Grafana teams with Microsoft Entra groups via Portal.
rootCause: Public access is disabled on the Grafana instance and the user is accessing the Azure Portal from a machine outside the VNET connected to the Private Endpoint. The Portal API call to /api/teams cannot reach the Grafana instance.
solution: 1) If PE exists and public access is disabled: access Portal from a machine within the PE VNET. 2) If no PE exists: create a PE (Grafana > Networking > Private Access) then access from within that VNET. 3) Enable public access (last resort). 4) Verify user has Grafana Admin role. Diagnostic: check ASC Workspace Metadata PublicNetworkAccess (0=Enabled, 1=Disabled). Analyze HAR file for /api/teams call result.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Sync Grafana teams With Microsoft Entra Groups Failure

symptom: AKS Windows node metrics not collected in Managed Prometheus after enabling monitoring on the cluster
rootCause: windowsexporter and windowskubeproxy values in ama-metrics-settings-configmap are set to false by default; Windows scrape targets are configured but disabled out of the box
solution: Deploy or update ama-metrics-settings-configmap in kube-system namespace with windowsexporter=true and windowskubeproxy=true, then deploy the windows-exporter-config ConfigMap in the monitoring namespace to install the Windows exporter
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Learning Resources/Lab | Collecting AKS Windows metrics (default and custom) to Managed Prometheus

symptom: ama-metrics-win-node pods not created on AKS cluster with Windows node pools after enabling Managed Prometheus
rootCause: Managed Prometheus metrics addon was not enabled with the Windows recording rules parameter
solution: Disable monitoring with az aks update --disable-azure-monitor-metrics -n <cluster> -g <rg>, then re-enable with the --enable-windows-recording-rules parameter to properly deploy Windows metric collection pods
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Learning Resources/Lab | Collecting AKS Windows metrics (default and custom) to Managed Prometheus

symptom: Customers receive a bill and are asking for more detail about Azure Log Analytics data usage than is available in their Azure bill.
rootCause: The information in the Log Analytics Usage table in the Azure Log Analytics blade cannot help them because the default retention period is 1 month and they need to see data from 2 or 3 month in the past.
solution:
Introduction
    Customers occasionally create support requests with Azure billing or with Log Analytics when they get an unexpectedly high bill. The information that billing provides often does not have sufficient granularity to tell the customer exactly what has changed that caused the high bill. 
    This KB will describe queries that can be executed by the customer in their workspace to get detailed information about the data that has been ingested. 
      Background
    Documentation for Azure Log Analytics billing information is located at these links:
      Azure Log Analytics
      Security Center Pricing
      Insights and Analytics Pricing
      Automation & Control Pricing
    This document does not describe how actual dollar amounts that are on a customers bill are computed. There are many factors that may affect the actual charged amount such as Enterprise Agreements, promotional deals, etc.
    The document is designed to help explain to a customer where the data consumption is coming from so that they can understand changes in their environment that may have impacted the bill.
      Security Group
    Currently the data in the Usage tables which are accessible by the customer are retained for the customer set retention period. The default is 31 days for paid workspaces and 7 days for free workspaces.  There is an internal database, InMem, 
    which retains 90 days of data
    . This is normally sufficient to answer a customers question.
    To access the DB you must join the La Ingestion Telemetry, lainmemtel, security group to access the OperationInsights_InMem_PROD DB to execute the queries. Go to 
      https://idweb
     and search for lainmemtel 
    Security Group:
      Introduction
    Customer questions are typically in the form of My month X bill is higher than my month X-1 bill. What is the cause? or sometimes more specific, Starting some date my bill went from about $X/day to 3*$X/day. What is the cause?
    If the ca... [truncated, see KB]

    Performing the reinstall

symptom:
When the 
    Application Insights Log Analytics solution is added to a different workspace the user will get the following error:
    Application is linked to another workspace (workspace id: de38fc84-****-45d6-****-
    b4eb****bf7a). Applications may only be linked to one workspace at a time.
rootCause:
There is currently an outstanding bug. When a Log Analytics workspace is deleted that contained the Application Insights solution, Applications Insights is not completely removed. The effect of this is that if Application Insights is added to a different workspace the user will get the following error:
    Application is linked to another workspace (workspace id: de38fc84-****-45d6-****-
    b4eb****bf7a). Applications may only be linked to one workspace at a time.
    This error can be generated 2 ways:
    1) Workspace exists
    2) Workspace has been deleted. 
    Detailed procedures for both if these cases are described below
solution:
DETAILED PROCEDURE WHEN WORKSPACE EXISTS
    The workspace Id in the error message still exists and contains the Application Insights Solution.
    If the workspace exists have them delete the Application Insights solution manually using the Azure portal as described in this section.
    1)
    Open the workspace blade in the Azure portal
    2)
    Open the Application Insights solution blade
    3)
    Open the OMS portal using the link in the blade in step 2)
    4)
    Click the Application Insights tile
    5)
    Unselect all the applications being monitored by Application Insights
    6)
    Return to the home page in the OMS portal, the Application Insights tile should display Requires Configuration
    7)
    Return to the Azure portal in step 2)
    8)
    Click delete
      Detailed procedure when workspace is deleted
    To execute this procedure you need to have: 
    Access to the Orchestrator web site:  
      http://aoioneclickprod:82
     , Ramweb group 14048
      Create folder in 
        \
        \aoioneclickprod\OneClickDeployment
    Use this convention for the folder name:
    RFC958418_IcM12345678
     or 
    RFC958418_SR123556789012345
    Normally to execute a change like this an RFC request would need to be created and approved before it could be executed. Since this is recurring problem a permanent RFC, 958418, has been created to bypass the approval process and facilitate quick resolution to the customer issue.
      Copy from File Share
    Copy zip file from 
      https://microsoft-my.sharepoint.com/:u:/p/stevechi/EWwAlzGSDuhIi-FI1U878VwBTQp6UJsBmSE37QbP3eXeLw?e=oT6Uzf
    Unzip the file and add the bin folder, AppInsightsCmd.PROD.xml , AppInsightsCmdLauncher.ps1 to the folder created in 
      \\aoioneclickprod\OneClickDeployment
    The new folder in
      \
      \aoioneclickprod\OneClickDeployment
     should look like this:
      Edit the AppinsightsCmd.PROD.xml file
    This file is ca... [truncated, see KB]
